He is a lecturer in the Singapore Polytechnic Diploma in Cardiac Technology Course and is part of the faculty of Duke-NUS Medical School and the SingHealth residency programme. His research interests include intravascular physiology and imaging, as well as clinical outcomes in patients with percutaneous coronary intervention. He has presented his research findings in local and international conferences and is first/co-author of numerous research papers in local and international peer reviewed journals. He is actively involved in research collaboration with Erasmus Medical Centre on interventional cardiology with an aim towards achieving a final research thesis. In addition, Clin Asst Prof Fam has special interests in the subjects of anticoagulation and point-of-care testing devices and on hospital committees, which look at the policies and practices, with regards to the use of anticoagulation and point-of-care testing devices.

name: Fan Hao
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Adjunct Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Fan is Senior Principal Investigator at the Bioinformatics Institute (BII) A*STAR, and Adjunct Associate Professor at Synthetic Biology Translational Research Program, School of Medicine, NUS, and Adjunct Associate Professor at Cancer and Stem Cell Biology Program, DUKE-NUS. Dr. Fan received his B.A. from University of Science and Technology of China (USTC) in Biological Sciences. He obtained his Ph.D from University of Groningen (RUG) in Biophysical Chemistry supervised by Prof. Alan Mark. After PhD study, he did postdoctoral research at University of California, San Francisco (UCSF) supervised both Prof. Andrej Sali and Prof. Brian Shoichet. Dr. Fan established his first independent laboratory at the Bioinformatics Institute (BII) A*STAR as Principal Investigator. His research has focused on developing computational methods to study protein-ligand interactions, including protein structure modelling, molecular docking, molecular dynamics simulations, chemoinformatics, and machine/deep learning. The developed methods are used in basic research, to help understand and regulate biological processes, in particular, structure-function mechanisms of GPCRs, transporters, and kinases. Meanwhile, these methods were also employed in applied research, including preclinical drug development, enzyme engineering, and chemical toxicity identification. His laboratory has developed small molecule inhibitors for kinases, beta-catenin, and proteins from human pathogens through collaborations.

name: Fan Qiao
department: Centre for Biomedical Data Science
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Fan is an Associate Professor at the Centre for Biomedical Data Science (CBDS), Duke-NUS Medical School. She received her master's degree from the University of Minnesota and a PhD from the Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health, National University of Singapore. Dr. Fan has extensive expertise in genomics research focused on complex human traits. Her research program investigates susceptibility genes and genetic modifiers for complex conditions, including ocular traits, while exploring genetic pleiotropy across health and behavioral phenotypes. With a core interest in advancing precision medicine through high-dimensional data analysis, she also applies her statistical skills to clinical and epidemiological projects. At Duke-NUS, Dr. Fan serves as a quantitative mentor for medical students in the Scholarly Development Program and as a thesis advisor for the PhD program in Quantitative Biology and Medicine (QBM).

name: Farida Ithnin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Ithnin is currently a Senior Consultant in the Department of Women’s Anaesthesia, KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital, Singapore. She was educated at the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland, Dublin before completing her Masters in Medicine (Anaesthesiology) from the National University of Singapore. Her main interest is obstetric anaesthesia, being actively involved in the clinical, educational, research and management aspects of the specialty for many years. Her scholarly activities are focused on medical education and clinical research in the field of obstetric anaesthesia. She is currently the Associate Program Director for SingHealth Anaesthesiology Residency Program and Supervisor of Training for Australian and New Zealand College of Anaesthetists.

name: Fatimah Binte Abdul Lateef
department: Faculty Advancement Department
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Prof Fatimah Lateef graduated with an MBBS from the Faculty of Medicine, National University of Singapore (NUS). She also qualified with an FRCS from the Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh as well as specialisations in Emergency Cardiovascular and Neurovascular Care and Prehospital Care Medicine from the Medical College of Virginia and the University of Cincinnati. She is also a Fellow, Academy of Medicine Singapore and holds Fellowship in Emergency Cardiac Care & Emergency Medical Services. She serves as Senior Consultant, Director of Undergraduate Training and Education and Director of Clinical Service and Quality at the Department of Emergency Medicine, Singapore General Hospital (SGH). She holds faculty positions as Adjunct Professor at Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School and Yong Loo Lin Medical School, NUS. She teaches part-time at the School of Health Sciences, Nanyang Polytechnic. Her other portfolios include Vice Chair, Research - Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme at SingHealth and Director, SingHealth Duke-NUS Institute of Medical Simulation, the largest simulation training facility in SE Asia. Her sub-specialisations include Prehospital Care Medicine, Cardiovascular and Neurovascular Emergencies. She has contributed and shared her professional expertise in countries such as the UK and USA and in many Asian countries. She serves as an International Consultant to Maxihealthcare, India. Prof Fatimah’s medical education research interests are broad and wide-ranging. Her current research and studies include ‘sepsis and early goal-directed therapy’ and ‘the area of consciousness practice in medicine’. Emergency Medicine, Traumatology, Emergency Cardiac Care, Neurovascular Emergencies and Prehospital Care Medicine are her speciality and clinical interests. She champions improving communication skills of healthcare professionals using inter-disciplinary practices like drawing on “Look, Listen and Feel” (LLF) concept. A strong advocate of lifelong learning and empowerments, she has presented over 500 abstracts at international conferences, published more than 300 scientific papers in peer-reviewed journals and is on the editorial board and reviewer for some 40 international journals. She leads various leadership groups. grooming youths and doctors to excel, which is also befitting her role as a core faculty, nurturing residents in Emergency Medicine. She is also an active volunteer in International Humanitarian Medicine and Acute Disaster Response.

name: Federico Tesio Torta
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Federico Torta is a Research Assistant Professor at Duke-NUS Medical School and at the Singapore Lipidomics Incubator (SLING), National University of Singapore. He is also the director of the lipidomics unit of SingMass, the Singapore National Laboratory for Mass Spectrometry. He received his Ph.D. degree in Biochemistry from the University of Parma, Italy. His expertise includes sphingolipidomics, high-throughput technologies for the assessment of lipidomes in large human cohorts and the study of protein-lipid complexes.

name: Felicia Ang Jia Ler
department: Lien Centre for Palliative Care
title: Research Fellow
summary: Dr Felicia Ang is a health services researcher with training in psychology, organizational behavior, and health services and systems research. Her work focuses on serious illness communication and decision making, supporting caregivers across the illness trajectory, and measuring and improving care quality and experience across serious illness. Felicia's research spans paediatric, adolescent, and adult populations using both qualitative and quantitative methods, patient- and caregiver-reported measures, and preference-based approaches. S Her work includes the development and psychometric assessment of instruments, development and evaluation of communication and support interventions, and applied research aligned with health system priorities in palliative care, ageing, and person-centred care. Her research is supported by competitive grant funding and conducted in close collaboration with clinical, policy, and community partners.

name: Firth Carl Aslan Jason Morton
department: Office of Innovation & Entrepreneurship
title: Adjunct Associate Professor
summary: Dr Carl Firth is Chief Executive Officer of ASLAN Pharmaceuticals, a US-listed clinical-stage immunology focused biopharmaceutical company developing innovative treatments to transform the lives of patients. Prior to founding ASLAN, Carl was Head of Asia Healthcare at Bank of America Merrill Lynch, supporting public and private financing of healthcare companies and advising on M&A transactions. Before joining the banking industry, Carl worked for AstraZeneca across various commercial and R&D roles, including Regional Business Development Director, Asia Pacific, and Director of New Product Development, China. Carl holds board positions at various biotechs, including JAGUAHR Therapeutics and DotBio. Previously, Carl served as an independent director of A*ccelerate, the commercialisation arm of Singapore’s Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR), an independent director of Hong Kong listed Uni-Bio Sciences, a commercial stage Chinese pharmaceutical company, and was a member of Singapore’s Health and Biomedical Sciences International Advisory Council. Carl is an Adjunct Associate Professor at Duke-NUS Medical School. He has a PhD in Molecular Biology from Cambridge University (Trinity College), an Executive MBA from London Business School; and a BA in Molecular Biology from Cambridge University. Carl is also the Founder of Pink Lions, a group for LGBTQ professionals working in healthcare and life sciences in Singapore.

name: Fock Kwong Ming
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Prof Fock is an clinical professor of Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School Singapore and a clinical professor with the Faculty of Medicine, National University of Singapore. He was a master with the Academy of Medicine from 2008 to 2010. He was also chairman of the Medical Board of CGH (1999 – 2007). He has been president (2010 – 2014) and current board member of Asia Pacific Digestive Week Federation (APDWF) and counselor of APSDE. Prof Fock has authored more than 180 papers and spoke at numerous Gastroenterology conferences and seminars. His research interests include H-pylori, GERD, gastric cancer, functional dyspepsia and irritable bowel disease. He has also authored consensus on H.Pylori Infection, gastric cancer and GERD. Prof Fock was named outstanding reviewer for GIE in 2015 to 2016 as well as 2016 to 2017. Prof Fock has been a visiting professor at University of Arizona School of Medicine, Tuscon, USA since 2007 and University of Utah School of Medicine, Utah, USA since 2003. Prof Fock has fellowships at the American College of Gastroenterology (FACG) and American Gastroenterology Association (AGA) as well as American College of Physicians (FACP) and Royal College of Physicians (FRCP – London & Edinburgh). He is a honorary fellow of The Hong Kong Society of Gastroenterology and has served on editorial board of Gastrointestinal Endoscopy, Gastroenterology and Hepatology and Journal of Clinical Gastroenterology.

name: Fong Kam Weng
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Fong received training in Clinical Oncology in SGH and the Royal Marsden Hospital UK. He spearheaded implementation of a clinical oncology network system in the department and the NCC. His clinical care focus on Head & Neck as well as Thoracic cancers. Dr Fong initiated Lung stereotactic body radiotherapy program.

name: Fong Kok Yong
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Professor
summary: Professor Fong Kok Yong is the Deputy Group Chief Executive Officer (Medical and Clinical Services), SingHealth. He is the Seah Cheng Siang Professor in Medicine, SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme and Senior Consultant, Department of Rheumatology and Immunology at SGH. As the former Chairman of the Medical Board, SGH, he is responsible for setting standards and maintaining clinical quality as part of good clinical governance. Prof Fong is also the Chairman of the Residency Advisory Committee for Rheumatology (Ministry of Health) and Member of the Residency Advisory Committee for Internal Medicine (Ministry of Health) since 2012. He is also the Associate Editor for APLAR Journal of Rheumatology since 2006 and for Singapore Medical Journal since 2008. He is a Fellow of Academy Medicine, Singapore in Internal Medicine and Rheumatology and Royal College of Edinburgh, UK. Prof Fong has over 100 peer-reviewed publications to date. He serves as the Senior Associate Dean for the Duke-NUS School of Medicine and a faculty member of the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore.

name: Fong Weng Seng Warren
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Warren Fong is a Senior Consultant in the Department of Rheumatology and Immunology, Singapore General Hospital (SGH). He graduated from National University of Singapore, Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine and completed his Advanced Specialty Training in Rheumatology in SGH in 2014. Dr Fong’s area of interest is in inflammatory arthritis. In 2013, Dr Fong was awarded the Manpower Development Plan Fellowship for further training in the use of advanced therapeutics in the management of patients with inflammatory arthritis, as well as incorporating translational research through the conduct of early-phase trials at the Department of Rheumatology of University Hospital Southampton NHS Foundation Trust and the NIHR Wellcome Trust Clinical Research Facility. With a keen interest in medical education, Dr Fong is currently the Department’s Director of Postgraduate Education, Associate Program Director for SingHealth Internal Medicine Residency Program and Assistant Program Director for SingHealth Rheumatology Senior Residency Program. He is heavily involved in planning educational programmes for postgraduates and has conducted medical education research and presented his findings in regional conferences such as the Asia Pacific Medical Education Conference (APMEC).

name: Foo Chao Ming, Reuben
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Reuben Foo obtained his Medical Degree from the National University of Singapore in 2012 and underwent Ophthalmology residency training at the Singapore National Eye Centre. He served as the Lead Resident of the SingHealth Ophthalmology Residency Programme in 2019 and was the Vice-Chair of the Students’ and Residents’ Relations Sub-Committee from 2016 to 2018. He obtained his postgraduate MMed(Ophth) qualification in 2017 and his specialist accreditation in 2020. He has completed a combined Neuro-Ophthalmology and Glaucoma fellowship training in SNEC in 2022 and is serving currently as a Consultant in both departments. He has a particular interest in managing conditions related to the optic nerve. Dr Foo has also shown a keen interest in medical education and strongly believes in nurturing the next generation of doctors. He participates actively in the teaching Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine students and enjoys helping them hone their clinical knowledge and skills. He was the supervising faculty for the recipient of the Singhealth Medical Student Talent Development Award in 2022. He is also involved in the teaching of Duke-NUS graduate medical students and often has students attached to his clinic. As recognition of his teaching efforts, he was awarded the prestigious Dean’s Visit Award for Junior Doctors by the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine in 2019. Dr Foo is also a tutor for the Graduate Certificate in Ophthalmic Nursing Course and Graduate Diploma for Ophthalmic Technicians Course.

name: Foo Chee Liam
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Following his MBBS in Singapore, Dr Foo Chee Liam was trained in General Surgery in the UK and became a Fellow of the FRCS (England) and FRCS (Edinburgh). He developed an interest in Plastic Surgery and trained at Mt Vernon, East Grinstead and subsequently at Singapore General Hospital. He attained Fellowship of the Academy of Medicine in 1988. He subspecialized in Craniofacial Surgery with HMDP Fellowships to the Australasian Craniofacial Unit (1990) and the USA (1995). As the first Chief of the Plastic Surgery Service at KKH, he has regularly participated in medical missions overseas since 1991 and continues to do so actively. Serving as President of SAPS from 1998 to 2000, President of the Asia-Pacific Craniofacial Association and currently as President of the Society for Surgery and Medicine Singapore. Dr Foo is also involved in education as Chairman of RAC (Plastic Surgery) and STC (1998 to present) and various committees at MOH overseeing Aesthetic Practice, Liposuction, Plastic Surgery Training and Medisave.

name: Foo Fung Joon
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Foo Fung Joon did his medical training in the UK at Nottingham followed by surgical training in Leeds and Manchester. At the end of his specialist training, he completed a Colorectal Fellowship at Singapore General Hospital. He then did a fellowship in Inflammatory Bowel Disease in Paris awarded by the European Society for Coloproctology. He was then appointed a colorectal consultant in Singapore General Hospital and more recently Sengkang General Hospital where he leads for the Colorectal Service, Perioperative Optimisation Programme and Hospital Nutrition Service. He is also Director of Operating Theatres and Endoscopy. His interests include colorectal cancer, inflammatory bowel disease and surgical nutrition. He is currently Secretary of the Society for Colorectal Surgeons Singapore and Singapore Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition Society.

name: Foo Hui Xian, Valencia
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Valencia Foo is an Associate Consultant with the Cataract & Comprehensive Ophthalmology Department at the Singapore National Eye Centre (SNEC), and also a sub-specialty fellow with the Oculoplastic Department under the Advanced Clinical Specialist Program. Dr Foo completed her medical training at Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore in 2015. She went on to complete her specialist training in Ophthalmology at SNEC in 2023. During her training, she represented Ophthalmology on the SingHealth Residents’ Committee, where she was conferred the SingHealth Residents’ Committee Appreciation Award. She obtained her Master of Medicine in Ophthalmology in 2021 and was inducted as a Fellow of the Royal College of Ophthalmologists (FRCOphth) in 2022. Dr Foo completed the Ophthalmology Exit Examination where she was awarded a Gold medal and was appointed Fellow of the Academy of Medicine, Singapore (FAMS) in 2023. Dr Foo is actively involved in clinical research. She has presented at numerous international and local scientific meetings, obtained research grants and published scientific papers in peer-reviewed journals. She won the American Academy of Ophthalmology Best Poster Award in 2019 and received the Young Investigator Award (Clinical) at the Singapore General Hospital 19th Annual Scientific Meeting in 2013. She is an Adjunct Research Fellow with the Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme (Eye ACP) in Duke-NUS Medical School. She also contributes to educating medical students and was appointed as the SingHealth Associate in Education in 2022. Dr Foo strongly believes in giving back actively to society and has participated in free screening events for the community.

name: Foo Jie Sheng (Fu Jiesheng)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clin Asst Prof Foo Jie Sheng is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Cardiology and Co-Director of the Adult Congenital Heart Disease (ACHD) programme at the National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS). His subspecialty interest is in adult congenital heart disease.

name: Foo Keong Tatt
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Prof Foo is currently an Emeritus Consultant and Advisor Department of Urology at the Singapore General Hospital having served as the Head of Department from 1988 to 2001. He is a Clinical Professor, National University of Singapore, and an Adjunct Professor with Duke-NUS Medical School. He is in the Board of Examiners for MBBS as well as M Med (Surgery) Degree and member of the Residency Advisory Committee (RAC) for Urology, under the Specialist Accreditation Board, Singapore having served as Chairman (STC Urology) previously. He was conferred the honorary member of the Malaysian Urological Association in 1999, the Honorary member of the Singapore Medical Association in 2007, Honorary Member Chinese Urological Association in 2010 and Honorary Fellow Sri Lankan Association of Urological Surgeon in 2011. He was given the Wu Jieping Medical Foundation (China) trophy for his contributions to Urology, in 2000. He was accorded the Lifetime Achieving Award by World Chinese Urological Society in May 2008, Carl Storze Life Time Achievement award by the Endourological Society in Dec 2008 and SIU Distinguished Career Award: Societe Internationale d’Urologie in 2009. He was awarded the Distinguished Golden Achievement Award under GCEO Excellence Awards in 2011. He was the Visiting Professor to Peking University in 2000 and, Fudan University Shanghai April 2007. He was the Dr Virendas Desai Visiting Professor 2007, to Muljibhai Patel Urological Hospital, Nadiad Gujarat, India. He was appointed Honorary Chief Consultant, Xiamen First Hospital, Xiamen, China Oct 2005. He was awarded the Public Administrative Medal (Silver) in 2001 by the President of Singapore. He is the Founder President of the Singapore Urological Association (President 1986 to 1988) and currently member, Board of Trustees for the Association, having served as Chairman previously. He was the Vice-Chairman for the 5th International Consultation on BPH in Paris, July 2000 and was a member of the Steering Committee for the International Consultation on Urological Diseases (ICUD) (2000 to 2006). He was the Secretary-General to the Urological Association of Asia from 1998 to 2006, Director of Asian School of Urology from 2006 to 2010 and currently is the Honorary Executive Director of the Urological Association of Asia (UAA) since 2006.

name: Foo Li Lian
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Foo Li Lian is a Consultant with the Cataract and Comprehensive Ophthalmology Department at the Singapore National Eye Centre. She graduated from the National University of Singapore with a bachelor's in engineering (Chemical) (1st Class Honors) in 2008, received the Dean’s List and NUS Awards for Study Abroad (Exchange Awards) in 2006, and was awarded Doctor of Medicine (MD) by Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School in 2012 and certified by Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates (ECFMG) in 2013. She embarked on Ophthalmology training subsequently and became a fellow of the Royal College of Ophthalmologist in 2016. In 2017, she received her Master's of Medicine in Ophthalmology and Graduate Diploma in Family Medicine. Dr Foo has published nine papers in renowned peer reviewed journals, delivered multiple presentations at international conferences and achieved accolades including Young Investigator Award (Clinical) at the Singapore General Hospital 19th Annual Scientific Meeting in 2011. She also co-authored two highly successful ophthalmology examination guidebooks titled “Practical Guide to VIVA and OSCE in Ophthalmology Examinations” and “Fundamental OSCE Guide in Ophthalmology” in 2017 and 2019 respectively. Empower with the vision to transform global technological landscape in healthcare, she pursues her interest in healthcare innovation by enrolling into the highly prestigious and selective MIT-Harvard Medical School Healthcare Innovation Bootcamp held in Boston 2019. She was among the seventy participants handpicked from more than two thousand applicants after rigorous rounds of interviews and selections. Out of the thirteen highly motivated teams at the bootcamp, her team emerged Runner-up in the elevator pitch on Demo Day. Moreover, she also completed the highly competitive Eureka-Singapore Monsoon School on Translational Medicine in 2018 to advance further in the field of entrepreneurship and innovation. She is currently the very first Myopia fellow of Singapore National Eye Centre (SNEC) and in the whole of Singapore. She aims to find novel solutions and improve treatment options to curb the current Myopia endemic through innovations. As an aspiring clinician-innovator, she aims to harness her skills and passion in developing new instruments and treatment options to improve clinical care, bridge gaps and conceptualize clinical innovations

name: Foo Wai Yin Marjorie
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Foo graduated from Queen’s University Belfast in 1989, MB ChB. BAO (Belf), attained MRCP (UK) 1992, FAMS 2003 and FRCP (Glasgow) 2004, FRCP (London) 2011 and FRCP Edinburgh (2015). She was trained in University College Hospital, The Middlesex Hospital and Hammersmith Hospital London, UK. Her training in Nephrology was completed in 2001. Assoc Prof is fully trained in all aspects of Nephrology; i.e. transplantation, general nephrology, haemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis. Her main subspecialty interests are peritoneal dialysis and the use of telemedicine in the monitoring of chronic diseases and treatments, quality of life measures as well as dialysis device innovation.

name: Foo Yang Yann
department: Education Advancement Department
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Foo Yang Yann is an Assistant Professor in the Office of Education at Duke-NUS Medical School. Dr Foo obtained her PhD from the National Institute of Education at Nanyang Technological University, Singapore, specialising in education research and qualitative methodology. Dr Foo's scholarship draws on systems theories to provide the levers for change to shift individual and social practices. As systems incoherence often constrains the capability of individuals and groups to perform optimally, she is interested in understanding the contextual factors that could harmonise systems to liberate that constrained capability. In 2022, Dr Foo was awarded the Tertiary Research Fund grant by Singapore’s Ministry of Education.

name: Fua Tzay-Ping
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Fua Tzay-Ping is a consultant with the Department of Emergency Medicine, Singapore General Hospital.

name: Gan Han Nee @ Gan Mee Chin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Gan Han Nee completed her basic medical degree in Singapore in 2000. Since then, she has been working in the Department of Emergency Medicine at Changi General Hospital (CGH).

name: Gan Shien Wen, Sheryl
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Sheryl Gan graduated from the University of Glasgow in 2005 with MBChB (Glasgow, UK) and attained her membership from the Royal Colleges of Physicians of the United Kingdom (MRCP, UK) in 2009. She completed her Advanced Specialist Training at Singapore General Hospital and was conferred Specialist Accreditation in Renal Medicine by the Ministry of Health, Singapore in 2013. She trained in Nocturnal & Home Haemodialysis and Geriatric Nephrology at the University Health Network, Toronto, Canada from 2015 to 2016. Dr Gan is a General Nephrologist trained in various aspects of Nephrology including Chronic Kidney Disease, Glomerulonephritis, Dialysis, Intensive Care Nephrology and Transplantation. Her clinical interests include Haemodialysis, in particular Nocturnal & Home Haemodialysis, and Geriatric Nephrology.

name: Gan Shuyi, Esther
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: Esther is a postdoctoral research fellow in the Endogenous Peptide Lab within the Cardiovascular and Metabolic Disease Programme at Duke-NUS. She trained in infectious diseases during her PhD, focusing on preclinical vaccine and therapeutic development, and contributed to the discovery of novel monoclonal antibodies against Yellow Fever and Zika. During the COVID-19 pandemic, she collaborated with Arcturus Therapeutics on the development of the first self-amplifying mRNA vaccine licensed for human use. She is also a co-inventor of a gene therapy for genetic dilated cardiomyopathy developed in collaboration with Nuevocor Biotech. Esther joined the Endogenous Peptide Lab to drive commercialization and translational efforts for small endogenous peptides (SEPs) through a bridge programme with 65Labs and LIVE Ventures.

name: Gan Wee Hoe
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Gan Wee Hoe is dually accredited in Occupational Medicine and Aviation Medicine. He is Senior Consultant of the Department of Occupational and Environmental Medicine at the Singapore General Hospital and Senior Consultant at Changi General Hospital’s Changi Aviation Medicine Centre. At the SingHealth level, he is Chief Executive Officer of SingHealth Community Hospitals. A/Prof Gan completed his Basic Specialty Training in Internal Medicine, followed by Advanced Specialty Training in Occupational Medicine. He received his Aviation Medicine training in the United Kingdom and belongs to the pioneer batch of Aviation Medicine specialists in Singapore. He pursued the Master of Public Health programme at the National University of Singapore and graduated with distinction, winning the Tye Cho Yook Gold Medal, the Society of Occupational Medicine Gold Medal and the Richard Gillis Prize. A/Prof Gan was with the Singapore Armed Forces for more than 17 years, with his last appointment being Chief Air Force Medical Officer and concurrently Assistant Chief of Medical Corps. During his tenure, he led the transformation of PACES 3, the SAF’s electronic medical records which won an award at the Health IT Excellence Award 2017. A/Prof Gan is a member of the Preventive Medicine Residency Advisory Committee and core faculty of the Residency program. He has published scientific papers and textbook chapters in Occupational and Aviation Medicine, including serving as the Co-editor of the Textbook of Occupational Medicine Practice (5th edition). For his outstanding contribution to the science and practice of Occupational Medicine, he was conferred the Honorary Fellowship of the Faculty of Occupational Medicine, Royal College of Physicians (London) in 2022. A/Prof Gan sits on several Ministry of Health and Ministry of Manpower specialist working groups, and co-chairs the Workplace Health Committee under the National Workplace Safety and Health Council. He is also a member of the Civil Aviation Medical Board under the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore. He is the past President of the Society of Aviation Medicine Singapore and the current President of the College of Public Health and Occupational Physicians, Academy of Medicine, Singapore.

name: Gandhi Mihir Purushottambhai
department: Centre for Biomedical Data Science
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Mihir Gandhi is a health outcomes and utility measurement expert whose research focuses on the development, validation, and valuation of patient-reported outcome measures for use in clinical research, health technology assessment, and value-based healthcare. He is an Assistant Professor affiliated with the Centre for Biomedical Data Science (CBDS) and the Lien Centre for Palliative Care at Duke-NUS Medical School, and is also affiliated with the SingHealth Duke-NUS Global Health Institute. He is a Chartered Statistician of the Royal Statistical Society (UK) and a Chartered Scientist of the Science Council (UK). His scholarly work sits at the interface of outcomes research, health economics, and applied biostatistics, with methodological contributions that have informed the measurement and valuation of health-related quality of life in diverse patient populations. He has published over 110 peer-reviewed journal articles and has an h-index of 37 (Google Scholar), with more than 4,000 citations. His work has contributed to the development and evaluation of preference-based instruments such as Quality of Care for Patients with Advanced Illness (QCPAI), Singapore Caregiver Quality of Life (SCQOLS), EQ-5D variants, and EORTC QLU-derived utility measures, supporting locally relevant value sets and quality-of-care measurement in Singapore and the Asia-Pacific region. He has been recognised as a Top 2% Scientist by citation impact in 2023 and 2024 and has served as Principal Investigator on grants from the National Medical Research Council (Singapore), EuroQol Research Foundation (Netherlands), and the Singapore Cancer Society.

name: Gao Long
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Research Fellow

name: Gavin James Smith
department: Signature Research Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases
title: Professor
summary: Professor Gavin Smith is the Director of the Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases (EID) at Duke-NUS Medical School. I am broadly interested in the ecology, genetic and antigenic evolution, vaccine strain selection, molecular diagnostics, pathogen discovery, population dynamics, molecular epidemiology, and interspecies transmission of infectious diseases. My research aims to understand viral disease ecosystems in Asia, including at the animal-human interface, to inform and enhance disease control. While I primarily work on influenza and coronaviruses, my lab studies a wide range of respiratory and enteric viruses. I conduct human and animal disease surveillance throughout Asia via an extensive network of collaborators. The gathered information is used in large-scale computational analyses to generate hypotheses on the role of virus mutations on their phenotype, such as fitness or virulence. We then test these hypotheses in the laboratory using tissue culture and animal models

name: Gayathri Devi D/O Nadarajan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Gayathri is an Emergency Medicine Consultant at the Singapore General Hospital and a pre-hospital doctor within the Unit for Pre-hospital Emergency Care (UPEC). She is interested in emergency care systems development in LMICs, psychological well-being amongst emergency healthcare workers and the role of global health education in the professional development of medical students and junior doctors. She is the Global Resuscitation Alliance representative within the Asian Association for EMS and is currently working to develop an evaluation tool for pre-hospital systems in low and middle income countries. She is also pursuing her Masters of Public Health as a global health fellow at the Singhealth Duke-NUS Global Health Institute. She is actively involved in overseas humanitarian projects and strongly believes that research and education are important pillars for humanitarian work to be effective and sustainable. Annually, she brings doctors, allied healthcare workers and medical students to the mountainous regions of Nepal as part of the university’s overseas community involvement Project.

name: Gene Ong Yong Kwang
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Gene Ong is a paediatrician working at the KK Women's and Children's Hospital's Children's Emergency. He has a keen interest in paediatric resuscitation science, paediatric Point-of-care ultrasonography, paediatric toxicology and simulation training and education. Some of his academic roles include being a core faculty in the SingHealth Emergency Medicine Residency programme, a decision editor of the Emergency Medicine Journal and is also an emeritus member of the ILCOR pediatric life support taskforce.

name: Germaine Loo Jie Min
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Germaine Loo is an Associate Consultant from the Department of Cardiology at National Heart Centre Singapore.

name: Gina-Maria Pomann
department: Centre for Biomedical Data Science
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Pomann is a biostatistician and educator with extensive research, leadership, and administrative experience. Her methodological contributions include the development of novel statistical methodology and predictive modeling techniques with applications to functional data and brain imaging. Collaborating with investigators across biomedical fields, Dr. Pomann has published in areas such as Surgery, Urology, and Hospital Medicine, among others. To support the development of efficient and effective data-intensive research across the biomedical sciences, Dr. Pomann’s primary research focuses on the science of team science. She works with collaborators with diverse scientific expertise to develop administrative structures, scientific operational processes, methods for organizing quantitative collaboration units, and workforce development programs. Dr. Pomann currently serves as an Associate Professor of Biostatistics and Bioinformatics and is the Director of the Biostatistics, Epidemiology, and Research Design (BERD) Methods Core, a group of faculty and staff with diverse quantitative expertise (biostatisticians, data scientists, bioinformaticians, clinical informaticians, epidemiologists, etc.) contributing to groundbreaking research in clinical and translational domains. She has directed the development and management of 30 collaboration teams comprising biostatistics staff, faculty, and students. She has successfully developed collaboration teams across various medical fields, partnering with entities such as the Department of Pediatrics, the Global Health Institute, and the Department of Neurosurgery, to name a few. The programs she directs have helped over 1100 investigators identify appropriate collaborators with quantitative expertise and have led to the co-authorship of more than 550 collaborative manuscripts by quantitative staff and faculty within the BERD Core. More information about BERD can be found on our website: https://biostat.duke.edu/berd-methods-core. In addition to building innovative processes and organizational structures to facilitate collaborations to drive data-intensive research, Dr. Pomann leads numerous workforce development programs for quantitative scientists. She develops processes and best practices to accelerate science and meet the evolving needs of data-driven clinical and translational research. Dr. Pomann developed the BERD Core Training and Internship Program (BCTIP), to provide a hands-on collaboration experience for Masters of Biostatistics students at Duke. She also oversees the Duke AI Health Fellowship Program, a two-year post graduate training program in data science and AI in Healthcare. Dr. Pomann is also MPI of an R25 titled "Quantitative Methods for HIV/AIDS Research" facilitating internships for quantitative students as well as a mentored scholars program and workshop series for biomedical investigators. Dr. Pomann holds a joint appointment at Duke National University of Singapore which allows her to develop training programs for the international workforce. The programs she directs have supported and trained more than 20 faculty, 40 staff, and 95 student interns to engage in data-intensive biomedical research.

name: Go Yun Yun
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Go is a senior consultant at the National Heart Center Singapore, specializing in multimodality imaging. Dr Go was the recipient of the ASEAN Pre-university Scholarship in 1999 and the National Research Foundation-Ministry of Health Healthcare Research Scholarship in 2011. In 2015, Dr Go was awarded the Exxon Mobil-National University of Singapore Research Fellowship to pursue advanced cardiac imaging research under the mentorship of Professor Stuart Cook, past director of National Heart Research Institute Singapore. In 2017, Dr Go was awarded the Healthcare Manpower Development Program Fellowship and she was trained in advanced stress echocardiography under Professor Patrizio Lancellotti, past president of the European Society of Cardiovascular Imaging. She was awarded the Singhealth Publish Award in 2019 for her work in defining new heart failure phenotypes using machine learning tools.

name: Goh Bee Tin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinc Assoc Prof Goh Bee Tin is Chief Executive Officer at the National Dental Centre of Singapore (NDCS). Clinc Assoc Prof Goh is also Academic Chair in the SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Program. Clinic Assoc Prof Goh obtained her Bachelor of Dental Surgery from the National University of Singapore (NUS) in 1993. She went on to specialise in the field of OMS, obtaining her Dental Fellowship with the Edinburgh Royal College of Surgeons in 1996 and Master of Dental Surgery from NUS in 1997. She was admitted as a Fellow of the Academy of Medicine, Singapore in 2000. In that same year, she was sent on a government scholarship for advanced training in cleft lip and palate and orthognathic surgery in Canada. In 2004, she furthered her training in craniofacial implantology in Sweden and the United Kingdom. She obtained a PhD in 2009 from Radboud University Nijmegen in the Netherlands for her novel research work on modular endoprosthesis for mandibular reconstruction. She received the Osteopore Innovation Award for her work in clinical tissue engineering and the SingHealth GCEO Outstanding Clinician Researcher Award for her contributions to research at the NDCS. Dr Goh is also appointed Clinical Associate Professor at the NUS Dental Faculty and Adjunct Associate Professor at the Nanyang Technological University. Clinic Assoc Prof Goh is active in teaching and training postgraduate students. Her research interests include bone tissue engineering and mandibular reconstruction.

name: Goh Boon Bee George
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr. George Goh is a Senior Consultant Gastroenterologist and Hepatologist at the Singapore General Hospital. After Gastroenterology specialist training at Singapore General Hospital, he underwent further advanced subspecialty training in Hepatology at the Cleveland Clinic (USA), with a research focus on non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD). He returned to establish a dedicated Metabolic Liver Clinic with multi-disciplinary involvement that helps to identify, prognosticate and manage patients with severe NAFLD. In addition, he remains actively involved in research and is the local Principal Investigator of several ongoing international NAFLD trials. He has published in the fields of NAFLD, liver cirrhosis, hepatocellular carcinoma and organ transplantation, and has been an invited faculty at both local and international conferences. Dr. Goh is an appointed Clinical Associate Professor at Duke-NUS Medical School and Clinical Lecturer at Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore. He is the current Chairman of the National Foundation of Digestive Disease (NFDD), which aims to increase awareness of such diseases among the general public.

name: Goh Eng Neo Anne
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Dr Anne Goh is a Senior Consultant of the Paediatric Allergy Service and Respiratory Medicine Service at the Department of Paediatrics, KK Women's and Children's Hospital, Singapore. Her job scope encompasses both Allergy and Respiratory-related diseases as well as General Paediatrics. Dr Goh received her medical degree from National University of Singapore and her specialist training in Toronto Sick Children's Hospital in Canada. She developed the Flexible Bronchoscopy Service for the Respiratory Medicine Service. She also developed the Allergy Service which offers management of food and drug allergies as well other allergic conditions. She actively participates in training of Residents and Medical students in her work. She is also the President of the Singapore Paediatric Society. She is a member to many professional committees, including Clinical Advisory Panel for Rehabilitation, Committee for development of Acute Viral Bronchiolitis Clinical Pathway, Asthma Clinical Pathway and Pneumonia Clinical Pathway and Committee for Respiratory Tract Infections Antibiotic Guidelines. Dr Goh is a reviewer for Asian Cardiovascular & Thoracic Annals, Singapore Medical Journal, Respirology, Annals Academy of Medicine, Paediatric International, Pediatric Pulmonology, Emerging infectious Diseases, Clinical and Developmental Immunology, NMRC Exploratory and Developmental proposals and for the MIMS Respiratory Guide. She is also Adjunct Associate Professor of the Yong Yoo Lin School of Medicine as well as Adjunct Associate Professor of the Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School. She also teaches the Lee Kong Chian Medical School undergraduate students. With clinical and research interests in the areas including respiratory infections, bronchial asthma management, obstructive sleep apnoea and allergy, Dr Goh has authored or co-authored several peer-reviewed papers as well as given many presentations at national and international courses and conferences. She is also actively involved in many asthma-related, infectious disease and allergy clinical trials.

name: Goh Guat Keng, Yvonne
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Yvonne Goh is an Emergency Physician at Changi General Hospital in Singapore with a passion for medical education and interprofessional education and collaborative practice. After completing medical school in 2003 and specialist training in 2007, Dr Goh pursued a fellowship in Simulation and Education and completed her Master of Medical Education Leadership (MMEL) in 2015. Dr Goh has since become a leader in postgraduate residency education and interprofessional education and is currently the ADIO for SingHealth CGH and CGH IPE/IPCP director. She also contributes as a part time consultant with MOH PTAS to improve medical education standards in Singapore.

name: Goh Jing Wen
department: Centre for Ageing Research & Education
title: Research Fellow
summary: Jing Wen joined Centre for Ageing Research and Education (CARE) as a Research Fellow in July 2023. In CARE, she works on falls- related project that aims to implement a multicomponent and tailored falls prevention intervention among ageing population with fall risk in Singapore. She is passionate about research that focuses on the ageing population. Jing Wen received her Ph.D. in Health Sciences (Physiotherapy) from National University of Malaysia. In her Ph.D. studies, she came up with a valid and reliable fall risk self-screening mobile application among older adults, to improve awareness and prevention behavior with regards to falls prevention strategies among older adults in community settings. She has great experience in assessing fall risk and delivering falls related prevention information to older adults, families and other healthcare professionals. She also contributed to the design of falls prevention education package with the ultimate goal of upgrading the fall risk assessment mobile application for a wider usage in future.

name: Goh Kah Yong
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: I graduated from University of Alabama at Birmingham (UAB) with a Ph.D. in Biomedical Sciences. After graduation, I pursued my first post-doctoral fellowship at National Cancer Center Singapore (NCCS) to study the effects of targeted therapies on cancer. Currently, I am doing my second post-doctoral fellowship at the laboratory of RNA Metabolism under the mentorship of Dr. Tang Hong-Wen researching on skeletal muscle stem cell function and myogenesis under physiological and pathological conditions.

name: Goh Kim Poh Brian
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Professor Goh is presently Head of the Department of Hepatopancreatobiliary and Transplant Surgery at Singapore General Hospital and the National Cancer Centre Singapore. He is also the Director of Singhealth Liver Transplant Service, and Chairman of Robotic Surgery, Division of Surgery, SGH. He is a highly-experienced and high-volume subspecialty HPB/transplant surgeon whose clinical practice focuses on the management of complex benign and malignant (cancers) conditions affecting the liver, pancreas, biliary tract and gallbladder. As of 2024, he has performed over 1,800 major subspecialty HPB/transplant surgeries after completing surgical training and becoming a Singapore board-certified specialist surgeon. He is the only surgeon in Singapore certified by both the American Society of Transplant Surgeons (ASTS) and Ministry of Health in abdominal(liver/kidney/pancreas) transplantation. He had previously worked as a surgical fellow for 2 years at the world-renowned Mayo Clinic Rochester performing numerous complex surgical procedures. He is well-recognized as an international thought leader due to his vast clinical experience and extensive research work in the field. Dr Goh has received several awards and has been an invited member to numerous international expert consensus panels. Presently, he is one of the most experienced surgeons in Southeast-Asia for laparoscopic HPB surgery. Since returning to Singapore in 2011, he has personally performed over 800 laparoscopic and robotic major HPB surgeries as the lead surgeon and mentored/proctored over a dozen subspecialty regional colleagues in many more such procedures. He is widely-recognized as an international expert in minimally-invasive HPB surgery. Regionally, Dr Goh is a pioneer in advanced laparoscopic HPB surgery and is frequently invited to lecture and proctor local/regional surgeons. He pioneered robotic HPB surgery in the region in 2013 and has personally performed over 150 robotic major HPB surgeries as the main console surgeon. He is the only local robotic HPB proctor and has tutored over a dozen subspecialty colleagues in the region in numerous robotic HPB procedures. He is also the only local HPB surgeon listed in the Intuitive Surgery (Da Vinci robotic surgery) surgeon locator and remains only one of a handful of high-volume HPB surgeons regionally who routinely performs laparoscopic or robotic surgery for highly complicated procedures such as major hepatectomies, pancreatectomies (Whipples procedure/ pancreatoduodenectomy), associated liver partition and portal vein ligation for staged hepatectomy (ALPPS), repair of bile duct injuries, resections for bile duct/ gallbladder cancers and resection of recurrence cancers with a high-success rate. Dr Goh frequently uses transplant oncology techniques to perform complex multi-organ resections with major vascular reconstructions for borderline-resectable cancers which may be considered inoperable by other surgeons. He is also experienced in treating rare conditions such as choledochal cysts, gastrointestinal stromal tumours(GIST), neuroendocrine (carcinoid) tumours and pancreatic cystic tumours such as intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasms (IPMN).

name: Goh Lay Kuan, Jeannette (Wu Lijuan, Jeannette)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Goh Meng Huat
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Goh Meng Huat is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Anaesthesiology at Singapore General Hospital. He is also a Visiting Consultant with the Department of Cardiothoracic Anesthesia at National Heart Centre Singapore.

name: Goh Pak Liang
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Goh Pak Liang is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Accident & Emergency Medicine (A&E) at Changi General Hospital (CGH). He completed his HMDP fellowship in Emergency and Trauma in Toronto, Canada from 2005 to 2006 and he is the Director of the Short Stay Unit in CGH A&E. Dr Goh also has an interest in trauma, ultrasound and observational medicine.

name: Goh Pheck Suan June
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Goh graduated from the National University of Singapore in 1990, had her anaesthesia training in Singapore and the United Kingdom. She was trained in Neuroanaesthesia and Neurocritical Care in Addenbrookes Hospital, Cambridge. She is a member of the Core Faculty Anaesthesia Residency Programme, Adjunct Assistant Professor Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School and Clinical Lecturer in the Yong Loo Lin Medical School. She is the administrator of the SGH hospital wide Sedation Course and in MRI Safety Teaching and Accreditation as well. Dr Goh's areas of interest lie in neuromonitoring for traumatic brain Injury and anaesthesia for neurosurgery under intraoperative high field MRI guidance.

name: Goh Qi Mei, Orlanda
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Goh Seo Kiat
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Goh Seo Kiat is a fellowship-trained senior consultant orthopaedic surgeon in the department. He reads medicine in the University of Cambridge in England and subsequently qualified in 2000 with degrees of Master of Arts, Bachelors of Medicine and Surgery. He subsequently returned to Singapore, completed his orthopaedic surgical training in 2008 and was elected a fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh. Dr Goh was amongst the pioneer batch of doctors who was awarded the Ministry of Health Training Scholarship in 2008. He went on to pursue an eighteen-month surgical fellowship in London, England. During this time, he worked with master surgeons in two major London teaching hospitals. He has spent time as a pelvis, and hip and knee replacement fellow in the St. George’s Hospital NHS Trust, and then as a hip fellow in the Guys & St. Thomas Hospital NHS Trust. His clinical interests are in the areas of reconstruction of pelvic and acetabular trauma, osteoporotic fractures in the elderly, hip and knee replacements as well as minimally invasive treatment of hip disorders in young adults. Dr Goh is a strong advocate of introducing the latest computer technologies into orthopaedic surgery. He is well-versed in computer navigation-assisted total knee and hip replacements. He is also one of four surgeons in Singapore who has been trained in South Korea in the field of Robodoc-assisted total knee replacement. Besides his clinical practice, Dr Goh has been active in medical research and has published articles in top peer-reviewed orthopaedic journals. He is also a regular reviewer of regional and international orthopaedic journals.

name: Goh Siang Hiong
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Clinical Professor Goh Siang Hiong is an Emergency Medicine (EM) Senior Consultant specialist and is currently the Director for Medical Education at Changi General Hospital. He has a deep interest in EM medical education, as well as safety and quality issues in the ED setting. He is presently an instructor for several life support courses. He is also interested in Emergency Trauma Care, Disaster Medicine and Medical Informatics.

name: Goh Soo Chye Paul
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: ​Dr Paul Goh is currently the Director of Polyclinics Development in SingHealth Polyclinics. He is also a Senior Consultant managing the full spectrum of primary care conditions. He has served in several senior leadership roles such as the first Chief Executive Officer of SingHealth Polyclinics, as well as in the Ministry of Health. He was also awarded a Health Manpower Development Fellowship in Melbourne for Family Medicine at the Royal Australian College of Family Physicians, Monash University, Alfred Hospital and the Royal Children’s Hospital, Melbourne. As a medical educator, he is Clinical Assistant Professor in Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School and Adjunct Assistant Professor in Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, NUS, and Clinical Physician Faculty in the SingHealth Family Medicine Residency Programme. He is examiner in the GDFM and Master of Medicine (Family Medicine) Examinations and Chief Examiner of the Collegiate as well as the Fellowship of the College of Family Physicians Exit Examinations. As a senior clinician and educator, he had served as Censor-in-Chief of the College of Family Physicians, where he played a key role overseeing and setting the standards in all the training programmes conducted in the College of Family Physicians, Singapore. He is currently the Advisor of the Board of Censors, College of Family Physicians, Singapore. He was also the first Co-Chair of the Primary Care Advisory Council at the Eastern Health Alliance/CGH, working collaboratively with GPs to improve the delivery of care in the Community. He is actively involved in the teaching of Medical Law and Ethics to doctors and is currently the Deputy Chair of the SingHealth Centralised Institutional Review Board.

name: Goh Su-Yen
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Assoc. Prof. Goh Su-yen is a Senior Consultant with SGH's Department of Endocrinology. She is also the Clinical Director with the Future Health System department.

name: Goh Xian-Yang, Charles (Wu Xianyang)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Charles Goh obtained his medical degree from the National University of Singapore in 2006. He obtained specialist qualifications in Diagnostic Radiology from the Royal College of Radiologists and National University of Singapore in 2013 and was awarded the Rohan Williams and Peh Eng Teck Memorial medals. Following that, he completed further training in Nuclear Medicine for dual accreditation in Diagnostic Radiology and Nuclear Medicine in 2017. He is actively involved in the training of Nuclear Medicine and Radiology residents and is a faculty member for the National Nuclear Medicine Residency Training Programme, the National Diagnostic Radiology Residency Training Programme and the National Final MMed / FRCR Preparatory Course. He has a strong interest in informatics, having previously managed the SAF medical informatics systems and the medical informatics systems for the Expo Community Care Facility for COVID-19 positive patients. He is presently completing a Masters in Health Informatics and is serving as Chief Medical Informatics Officer and Deputy Director for Imaging Informatics.

name: Goh Yeow Tee
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Dr Goh is a Senior Consultant at Singapore General Hospital and was formerly Group Director, Research (Research Integrity, Compliance and Ethics) at SingHealth. Prior this, he held positions which include Acting Chairman for Division of Research, Director of Clinical Trials and Research Centre (CTRC), Director of Department of Clinical Research, Director of Office of Translational Medicine Oversight (TMO) and Director of Research Quality Management. He is currently Clinical Professor at Duke–NUS Medical School and Adjunct Associate Professor at the NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine. As an experienced researcher, Prof Goh has accumulated an extensive research portfolio. He was awarded over 10 research grants as PI or higher (funds in excess of S$15 million) and led over 70 clinical trials as PI; published over 120 peer-reviewed research articles; and presented over 150 papers at conferences. His reputation has led to active engagements as invited speaker, chairperson, advisory board member and steering committee member for regional and international events. Prof Goh has played a key role in shaping and leading the training of haematologists in Singapore as member of Specialist Accreditation Committee for Haematology from 1998 and as the Chairman from 2004-2012. He oversaw the transition to residency programmes as Chairman of the Resident Advisory Committee. He actively mentors clinical scientists, trainees, medical and doctorate students. He was also a founding member of the Bone Marrow Donor Programme and Leukemia Lymphoma Foundation.

name: Goh Yew Seong
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Goh Yew Seong graduated from NUS Medical school in 1999, completed his basic internal medicine training in Singapore in 2005 before sub-specialising in cardiology. He then wen on to complete his cardiology training in 2008 and was awarded HMDP for interventional cardiology training in Australia (2010). Dr Goh serves as a senior consultant at Changi General Hospital (CGH) as a visiting consultant at National Heart Centre. He is also the director of the CGH Cardiac Catheterization Lab.

name: Gopal Krishnan Priyadarshini
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Research Fellow

name: Gou Qian
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Research Fellow
summary: Gou Qian is a Research Fellow in the Cancer and Stem Cell Biology (CSCB) Programme, working under the supervision of Dr. Tang Hong-Wen. She received her Ph.D. in Clinical Medicine from Jiangsu University, China. Her current research focuses on elucidating the molecular mechanisms underlying skeletal muscle aging, with the goal of identifying effective therapeutic strategies for the treatment of sarcopenia.

name: Goy Wee Lip Raymond
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Goy is a Senior Consultant Anaesthesiologist and Deputy Campus Education Director (Postgraduate Education) at the KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital. He obtained his postgraduate certification (Master of Medicine Anaesthesia, Singapore and Fellowship of the Australian and New Zealand College of Anaesthetists) in 2003, and Masters of Health Professions Education (Maastricht) in 2017. His medical education research interests include reflective learning and practice, complex learning, work-based assessment and feedback. He is a member of the SingHealth Interprofessional Collaborative Practice (IPCP) Taskforce.

name: Graves Nicholas
department: Signature Research Programme in Health Services Research & Population Health
title: Professor
summary: Prof Graves is the Deputy Director of the Programme in Health Services & Systems Research at Duke-NUS and the SingHealth Duke-NUS Health Services Research Institute. His areas of knowledge include health economics, health services research, decision making and cost-effectiveness. He is interested in projects that show high and low-value care, as well as the processes around implementing new policies. His major focus is on showing how health services can be improved at low cost, or even improved with cost savings. He enjoys collaborating with clinicians who wish to improve the performance of health services. Prof Graves has made contributions of international significance, publishing over 250 articles in top-ranking peer reviewed journals such as JAMA, BMJ, AIDS, Health Economics, Clinical Infectious Diseases, Lancet Infectious Diseases, The Journal of Infectious Diseases and Emerging Infectious Diseases.

name: Guinto Ramon Lorenzo Luis Rosa
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Global Health Institute
title: Associate Professor
summary: Renzo R. Guinto, MD DrPH is Associate Professor of Global and Planetary Health and Lead of the Planetary Health Initiative at the SingHealth Duke-NUS Global Health Institute (SDGHI), Duke-NUS Medical School, National University of Singapore. He is also an affiliate faculty at Duke-NUS’s Signature Research Programme in Health Services & Systems Research. One of the world’s pioneers of the new field of planetary health and Southeast Asia’s leading “pracademic” of climate and health, Renzo works on diverse aspects of the climate and health nexus such as climate-resilient and environmentally sustainable health systems; impacts of climate change on mental health; climate, migration, and health; climate implications for global health interventions; climate and health education; ethical issues around climate and health policy; climate and health governance and diplomacy, among others. A Senior Honorary Visiting Fellow of the University of Cambridge, Renzo is the convener of Planetary Health Philippines – a community for nation building and planetary healing in the Philippines – and was the former Director of the Planetary and Global Health Program of the St. Luke’s Medical Center College of Medicine in the Philippines and inaugural Chief Scientist of the Sunway Centre for Planetary Health in Malaysia. Currently, Renzo is a member of the National Panel of Technical Experts of the Philippine Climate Change Commission; World Health Organization’s Technical Advisory Group on the ethics of climate and health; board of directors of Health Systems Global; editorial boards of several journals including The Lancet Planetary Health; and several Lancet Commissions, most notably the Lancet Commission on Sustainable Healthcare and the O’Neill-Lancet Commission on Racism, Structural Discrimination, and Global Health. An Obama Foundation Asia-Pacific Leader and Aspen Institute New Voices Fellow, Renzo has served as consultant for various organizations including the WHO, World Bank, USAID, and Wellcome. Renzo obtained his Doctor of Public Health from Harvard University and Doctor of Medicine from the University of the Philippines Manila, and received further training from Oxford, LSHTM, Copenhagen, Western Cape, Institute of Tropical Medicine-Antwerp, Karolinska Institutet, Stockholm School of Economics, and East-West Center in Hawaii. He has traveled to and lectured in more than 70 countries and 100 universities across the world; published more than 200 reports and articles in scientific journals, books, and popular media; and directed and produced short films that communicate the message of planetary healing to the world. In 2020, Renzo was included by Tatler Magazine in its Gen.T List of 400 leaders of tomorrow who are shaping Asia’s future. In 2022, he was named one of The Outstanding Young Men (TOYM) of the Philippines for pioneering the field of planetary health in the Philippines and Southeast Asia. In 2023, Renzo was awarded the inaugural CUGH-Velji Planetary Health Innovation Award by the Consortium of Universities for Global Health. In 2025, he was bestowed the McNulty Prize for “finding solutions to the urgent global health threat of climate change,” and the inaugural Kiyoshi Kurokawa Award by Japan’s Health and Global Policy Institute for “extraordinary contributions to health policy and innovation in the Asia-Pacific region.”

name: Guo Changming
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Guo Changming is a senior consultant and practising orthopaedic surgeon in Singapore General Hospital. His subspecialty interests include minimally invasive spinal surgery (decompression, TLIF, Kyphoplasty), scoliosis surgery and cervical disc arthroplasty. He did his fellowship at Harvard Medical School. He is currently a Visiting Consultant at both the Kadang Kerbau Women’s and Children’s Hospital and the School Health Service. Other than his clinical work, Dr Guo has a keen passion for both teaching and research. He currently teaches both at the Duke – NUS Medical School and the National University of Singapore. He has also been an invited speaker at several regional conferences. He has organized many spine related basic and speciality courses. He was awarded the National Medical Research Council award to do research at Harvard Medical School. His research interests include tissue engineering with human mesenchymal stem cells and fabrication of injectable bone cements with chitin-based biomaterials.

name: Guo Jing
department: Centre for Vision Research
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: Jing Guo is a postdoc fellow at Center for Computational Biology. She has experience in handling cohort-level multi-omics data analyses, including genomics, (single-cell and bulk) transcriptomics, epigenetics and metabolobmics data. As a member of the data analytic team in the Center for Computational Biology (CCB), she is passionate about providing advanced bioinformatics support to various research teams.

name: Guo Kunyao, Alvin
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: Alvin Guo, PhD is Senior Research Fellow in Koji Itahana Laboratory of Tumor Suppression at Cancer & Stem Cell Biology (CSCB) program. Dr Guo obtained his BSc and PhD at the Department of Biological Sciences, National University of Singapore (NUS). He uncovered an interesting role of NF-κB in mechano-signal transduction in p53-deficient MEFs. After joining Duke-NUS Medical School, he discovered a novel mutant p53 interacting protein BCAR1 which contributes to cancer cell invasion. Currently he is focusing on studying the role of p53 in macrophage biology, in particular iron metabolism and phagocytosis.

name: Haglund Michael Martin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Global Health Institute
title: Professor
summary: My clinical areas of expertise include spinal surgery, especially cervical spine surgery where I have performed over 7,500 cervical spine procedures and recently was ranked the top cervical spine surgeon in the country by MPIRICA (an analytical company that reviews surgical outcomes). I believe the whole patient is important and we emphasize time with the patient and careful discussions regarding possible surgery. Our excellent results are due to a great team of physicians, nurses, CRNAs, and anesthesiologists. I also believe in the spiritual side in taking care of my patients. Through my Masters in Academic Medicine degree, I developed the Surgical Autonomy Program which is now used in many Neurosurgery Programs across the country and is an innovative way to teach, assess, and provide feedback to residents in the intraoperative environment. In 2014 we built on our previous seven years of experience in Uganda and have developed the first ever Division of Global Neurosurgery and Neurology in the country, where I serve as the Division Chief and the Division boasts over 125 members including faculty, graduate and medical students, undergraduate students and an outstanding staff of researchers, most located in the Duke Global Health Institute. The Division has published over 100 manuscripts between 2014 and 2022. We have primarily worked in building capacity, teaching, and collaborative research projects in Uganda. In 2019, I was invited to join the faculty at the SingHealth Duke-NUS Global Health Institute and we are planning branching out in collaboration with the SDGHI to work with Dr. Jai Rao in Singapore to develop capacity, training, and research in Jaffna, Sri Lanka.

name: Hairil Rizal Bin Abdullah
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Hairil Rizal is a Senior Consultant Anaesthesiologist and the Clinical Director for the Office of Value-based Healthcare (OVBH) at SGH. He completed his anaesthesiology specialty training in Singapore, and further pursued fellowship and a MSc degree in Perioperative Medicine at the University of Toronto, Canada and University College London, UK respectively. Additionally, he holds a PhD in Clinical and Translational Science, focusing on Data Science in Healthcare, which were supported by the prestigious Lee Kuan Yew Scholarship in 2022 and the National Medical Research Council (NMRC) Research Training Fellowship in 2020. As a perioperative clinician, he led a transformative initiative in SGH that culminated in a one-stop Preoperative Assessment Centre, and introduced the “PREPARE” prehabilitation program for elderly patients, earning him the National Healthcare Innovation and Productivity Award in 2020. Concurrently a Clinician Scientist funded by the NMRC and a Faculty Fellow for Singapore Biodesign, Hairil is a leading figure in healthtech innovations. As the Clinical Director of OVBH, his work in operationalizing Value Based Healthcare has been recognized with the "Distinguished Champion of Change Leader Award" by SingHealth in 2022. His focus on innovation, education, and patient care positions him as a prominent leader in his field.

name: Hannah Yeo Xuan En
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Research Assistant

name: He Yingke
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr He Yingke is a Consultant with the Department of Anesthesiology. She graduated with MD degree from Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School in Singapore in 2013. She was awarded the Master of Medicine by the National University of Singapore in 2017. She has special clinical interests in Perioperative Medicine and Obstetric Anaesthesia. As a perioperative physician, she has pursued a Master in Perioperative Medicine from the University College of London. She is also involved in setting up the perioperative medicine service and preoperative anaesthesia assessment clinic in Singapore National Eye Center (SNEC). Dr He is leading a few research projects including perioperative smoking cessation programs in the department. She is also actively involved in quality improvement projects to improve patients' care. She is working closely with obstetricians to improve the care of obstetric patients in SGH as well.

name: Helen Tjia Tjoei Lian
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Neuroscience Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Helen Tjia is a clinical neurologist who primarily sees outpatients for general neurology, headaches, stroke, and movement disorders. Dr. Tjia also has teaching sessions at the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, Duke-NUS Medical School and Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine.

name: Hendrik Luesch
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Professor
summary: Hendrik Luesch, Ph.D., a professor of medicinal chemistry, received his Diplom in Chemistry at the University of Siegen (Germany) in 1997. He studied marine natural products chemistry at the University of Hawaii at Manoa and obtained his Ph.D. with Professor Richard E. Moore in 2002. He undertook three years of postdoctoral studies as an Irving S. Sigal Fellow at The Scripps Research Institute with Professor Peter G. Schultz in functional genomics and chemical biology. Since 2005 he has been faculty at the University of Florida (UF) and is currently Professor and Chair of the Department of Medicinal Chemistry. He leads a comprehensive multidisciplinary marine natural products program with function-driven chemistry- and genomics-based discovery platforms and is UF’s founding Director of the Center for Natural Products, Drug Discovery and Development (CNPD3). Since 2024, he holds a joint appointment at Duke-NUS Medical School in Singapore. Highlights of his research include the discovery of the microtubule-destabilizer dolastatin 10 from a marine cyanobacterium, pinpointing the source of this important compound. Dolastatin 10 served as the starting point for MMAE and MMAF, which are the cytotoxic payloads for several approved antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). Other noteworthy discoveries include gatorbulin-1 (microtubule destabilizer), apratoxins (Sec61 inhibitors), and largazole (class I HDAC inhibitor). He has published over 180 papers, received the UF Technology Innovator Award for several years and the UF 2021 Innovation of the Year for the discovery and characterization of gatorbulin-1, targeting a novel tubulin pharmacological site. He is co-founder of two companies, and serves as the Chief Scientific Officer of Oceanyx Pharmaceuticals, Inc. He has significant Singapore experience and ties to various local institutions, as he was on sabbatical at A*STAR’s IMCB in 2014 and a Visiting Professor at the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, Nanyang Technological University (NTU) for 3 years (2018–2020), followed by 3-months sabbaticals at the School of Biological Sciences at NTU (2022) and then Duke-NUS Medical School (2023), before joining Duke-NUS part-time in 2024.

name: Henry Soeharno @Henry Soeharno Lan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Henry Soeharno graduated in 2005 from the University of New South Wales, Sydney Australia.
