summary: Trained in clinical nuclear medicine (diagnostic and therapeutic), I have a keen interest in nuclear medicine therapeutics, in particular, the radionuclide treatment of rare diseases like paediatric neuroblastoma and neuroendocrine tumours. Have interest in clinical research including translating nuclear medicine probes from the bench to bedside and promoting radionuclide therapies within the local population of Singapore. Have been working with various clinicians from SGH and KKH to establish protocols for the treatment of paediatric patients with neuroblastoma using high dose I-131 metaiodobenzylguanidine.

name: Keng Yung Jih, Felix
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinical Associate Professor Felix Keng is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Cardiology and the Director of Nuclear Cardiac Imaging at the National Heart Centre Singapore. His sub-specialty interest is in nuclear cardiology and cardiac computed tomography. He regularly lectures for the International Atomic Energy Agency in countries around the world. Clin Assoc Prof Keng received his Certification in Nuclear Cardiology from the Certification Board of Nuclear Cardiology, USA in 1998. He also received Board Certification in Cardiac CT in 2010. Clin Assoc Prof Keng did his fellowship at the Methodist Hospital, Baylor College of Medicine and at the UCLA Pet Centre, USA. For his Cardiac CT fellowship, he visited the University of Erlangen and Klinikum Pasing, Munich, Germany and University of California, Irvine, USA. He is a Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians, Edinburgh, UK; Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians, London, UK; Fellow of the Academy of Medicine (Cardiology), Singapore and Fellow of the Asia Pacific Society of Cardiology (FAPSC) since 2007.

name: Kenneth Wong Pak Leung
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Kenneth Wong is a full-time Senior Consultant at the Musculoskeletal Centre KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital (KKH). Having experienced treating children as a junior doctor under Paediatric Medicine, Paediatric Surgery and Paediatric Orthopaedic Surgery from 2008-2010, Kenneth went on to specialize in Orthopaedic Surgery with a focus on children limb conditions. He is passionate about improving treatment outcomes in children limb issues. He actively teaches the new generation of doctors and specialists. Kenneth is also keen on sharing novel techniques and treatment methods with the paediatric orthopaedic community. He regularly participates in regional and international discussions. Kenneth is an examiner at the local universities and serves as a reviewer for multiple local and international journals.

name: Kenny Loh Wei Tsen
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Kenny Loh is Head and Senior Consultant with the Department of Anaesthesiology from Singapore General Hospital, and a Senior Consultant from Department of Cardiothoracic Anaesthesia from National Heart Centre Singapore. Dr Kenny Loh graduated from The University of Melbourne (MBBS) 2002 and started his internship in Singapore in 2003. He completed his Postgraduate in MMED Anaesthesiology 2009 and was appointed Consultant in Singapore General Hospital in 2013. He has been active in medical mission works and has participated on a few medical missions, the most recent being Operations Smile Mission to Uzbekistan 2009.

name: Kenny Tay Xian Khing
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Tay is from the Trauma Service. His areas of interest involve the management of patients with fractures and orthopaedic injuries, particularly periprosthetic fractures. This drives his research interests, with publications and presentations covering orthogeriatric trauma and hip fractures. Dr. Tay completed his medical degree at the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine in 2008 and underwent his residency in Orthopaedic Surgery in Singhealth from 2012 – 2019. He undertook an Australian Orthopaedic Association arthroplasty fellowship at Epworth Healthcare in Melbourne, participating in revision surgeries, particularly that for periprosthetic fractures. He has completed an AO Foundation Trauma fellowship in complex orthopaedic trauma surgery in Universitätsspital Basel, Switzerland. He holds appointments in all 3 local medical schools in Singapore and is the Associate Programme Director of the Singhealth Orthopaedic PGY1 programme and Physician Faculty of the Singhealth Orthopaedic residency programme. He was awarded the Singhealth Outstanding Resident Award in 2018 and 2019, and the Singhealth Outstanding Faculty Award in 2019 and 2021. His administrative appointments include previously being in the Medical Board, the Division Patient Safety and Quality Officer, and the SGH Campus Phase 2B Hospital Planning Committee. He has received accolades such as the Service with A Heart award and the Singapore Health Quality Service Award, as well as for his contributions against COVID-19.

name: Khoo Boon Kheng James
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr James Khoo received his MBBS degree from the National University of Singapore in 1984. He is trained in Diagnostic Radiology primarily at the Singapore General Hospital and qualified himself as Fellow of the Royal College of Radiologists (London) in 1992. Between 1995 & 1996, Dr Khoo underwent subspecialty training in Head and Neck Radiology at the Stanford University Hospital, USA and he stayed on at Stanford the following year from 1996-1997 for training in Body Imaging. In the year 2000, he joined the National Cancer Centre, Singapore to take up new challenges in the area of cancer imaging. His main clinical interest is in the Imaging of Head & Neck Cancers for which he has had numerous research publications as well as minor interest in Breast Imaging & Body Imaging. Dr Khoo teaches at the Duke-NUS Medical School and also at the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore. Presently, he is an examiner at the Master of Medicine (Radiology) - FRCR examination and was Past President of the Medical Ultrasound Society of Singapore. He author/co-author of over 50 peer-reviewed publications and book chapter and reviews several local and international journals too.

name: Khoo Chun Yuan (Gu Chunyuan)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clin Asst Prof Khoo Chun Yuan is presently a Senior Consultant from the Department of Cardiology at the National Heart Centre Singapore, with clinical interests in Cardiomyopathy, Heart Failure and Echocardiography. She completed her medical undergraduate training at the National University of Singapore and obtained her master's in medicine and Membership of the Royal College of Physicians (United Kingdom). She completed her Cardiology specialist training accreditation (Singapore) in 2017. She has obtained postgraduate diplomas from the American Board of Echocardiography. She was awarded the 2022 Singhealth HMDP and Scholarships Awards and completed her subspecialty training in Heart failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction, Cardiac Amyloidosis and Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy at the Bluhm Cardiovascular Institute, Northwestern Memorial Hospital, Chicago in July 2024. She also successfully completed the Northwestern Cardiac Amyloidosis Fellowship program during her time at Northwestern. Apart from her clinical duties, Dr Khoo is also actively involved in clinical trials and education. She is a Clinical Assistant Professor at the Duke-NUS Medical School. She is also an appointed Clinical Tutor at the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, and the Nanyang Polytechnic advanced diploma in Nursing programme.

name: Khoo Tan Hoon Seng
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: ​Dr. Khoo Tan is a Senior Consultant Radiation Oncologist at the National Cancer Centre, Singapore. He has been working in radiation oncology since 1985 and completed his specialty training in 1991. He is also a Visiting Consultant at Kandang Kerbaul Women’s and Children’s Hospital. Dr. Khoo Tan graduated from the National University of Singapore in 1983. He obtained his post-graduate degree in Clinical Oncology from the Royal College of Radiologist, United Kingdome in 1991. He also spend two years training at the Royal Marsden Hospital London from 1988 to 1990. His current subspecialty is in Gynae- Oncology.

name: Khoo Yoong Khean
department: Centre for Outbreak Preparedness
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Khoo Yoong Khean is a medical doctor by training with more than 15 years of clinical and public health experience. He started his career as a physician in Malaysia in primary healthcare, emergency and trauma medicine, nephrology, cardiology and infectious diseases. Prior to joining Duke-NUS, he was a clinical data analyst in a public hospital in Singapore, developing patient safety and quality initiatives for hospital-level programmes and a member of several credentialing and privileging committees. He is also active in medical humanitarian work, with several medical service work during natural disasters and street clinics for the urban poor. He obtained his Master of Business Administration in 2022. Yoong Khean is currently the Deputy Director and Assistant Professor at the Centre for Outbreak Preparedness with joint appointments at the Centre of Regulatory Excellence and SingHealth Duke-NUS Global Health Institute. He is also a faculty at the Centre for Infectious Disease Emergency Response at Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore and an advisory board member of the Asia Pacific Oncology Alliance (APOA). Yoong Khean focuses on global health policy and health systems strengthening through policy development and capacity-building efforts. His areas of research and interests also include patient engagement in health systems, access to medicine and healthcare services and regulatory systems strengthening.

name: Khor Jen Lock Christopher
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Christopher Khor attended medical school at NUS, and completed advanced clinical fellowships in ERCP and in Endoscopic Ultrasound (EUS) at the Medical University of South Carolina and Indiana University Medical Center respectively. More recently, he trained in Endoscopic Submucosal Dissection in Japan. His main practice areas are in pancreato-biliary disease (ERCP and EUS), endoscopic resection and other advanced endoscopic therapies, and in general gastroenterology. Dr Khor has a keen interest in endoscopic quality and education, and in promoting cross-border co-operation among the regional endoscopy community. His regional work includes regular faculty invitations to demonstrate and teach endoscopy. He has more than 40 peer-reviewed papers to his name, and is a reviewer for endoscopy and GI journals. Dr Khor currently co-chairs an Asia-Pacific group focused on EUS education. He served as Vice-President of Asian-Pacific Digestive Week 2011 in Singapore, for which he directed Endoscopy programming, is a past President of the Gastroenterological Society of Singapore, and is the immediate past Chairman of the Chapter of Gastroenterologists, Academy of Medicine Singapore.

name: Kiang Lei (Jiang Lei)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Jiang Lei is a consultant spine surgeon in Singapore General Hospital. He was awarded the Singapore College of Surgeons Gold Medal for being the best performer in the orthopaedic surgery exit examinations and was appointed as a Fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons in 2021. He received fellowship training in spine surgery in Addenbrooke’s Hospital, Cambridge University Hospital Trust in 2021 with exposure to complex procedures in a tertiary spine centre. He was awarded the National Medical Research Council Research Training Fellowship and completed an MPhil in clinical neurosciences at the University of Cambridge in 2022, completing his thesis on novel neural interfaces for motor restoration in spinal cord injury. He is dedicated to translating recent advances in spinal cord neural interfaces from bench to bedside to restoring motor function in patients with paralysis due to spinal cord injury and other neurological disorders.

name: Kiew Sieh Yean
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Kiew Sieh Yean is a Consultant with the Cataract & Comprehensive Ophthalmology Department at Singapore National Eye Centre, and is also a subspeciality fellow in the Glaucoma Department under the Advanced Clinical Specialist Program. Her current practice includes the management of glaucoma, general and comprehensive ophthalmology, and cataracts. She completed her undergraduate medical training at the University of Aberdeen in Scotland, graduating with Honours and the Keith Medal in Surgery in 2011. She subsequently trained under the ACGME-I (American Council of Graduate Medical Education- International) accredited SingHealth Ophthalmology Residency Program, and obtained her Master of Medicine in Ophthalmology in 2017. She qualified as a Fellow of the Royal College of Ophthalmologists (London, United Kingdom) in 2018, attained Ophthalmology Specialist accreditation and was admitted as a Fellow of the Academy of Medicine, Singapore in 2019. Dr Kiew has received multiple academic and service awards, including the SingHealth Outstanding Resident in Ophthalmology award twice in 2016 and 2019, the Joint Commission for Specialist Training (JCST) gold medal award for her performance in the Ophthalmology exit examinations in 2019, as well as the Richard Fan Medal of Ophthalmology for outstanding residents in 2019. In 2014, she was the recipient of the prestigious Crombie Medal form the Royal College of Ophthalmologist in London. Throughout her career at SingHealth, Dr Kiew has been involved in various leadership and administrative roles: she represented Ophthalmology on the SingHealth Resident's Committee in 2016, served as the Chief Resident for SingHealth Ophthalmology from 2017 to 2019, and she is an alumnus of the Singapore Chief Residency Programme. Dr Kiew has authored two book chapters and multiple articles in peer-reviewed journals and presented her work at various regional and international conferences. Her current interest includes investigate of conjunctival wound healing, inflammation and fibrosis, including the search for novel therapeutic targets to modulate the post-surgical scarring response following glaucoma surgery. She is currently co-investigator of a study exploring the use of a novel antifibrotic drug in animal models of glaucoma filtration surgery, which was awarded the SNEC Health Research Endowment Fund (HREF) grant in 2019.

name: Kinjal Vidyut Mehta
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Kinjal Mehta is a Senior Consultant & Director of Foot and Ankle Surgery with Department of Orthopaedic Surgery, Changi General Hospital (CGH). Under Dr Kinjal Mehta, the service has been brought to greater heights and multiple initiatives and collaborations to improve the service have been implemented. Multidisciplinary care is undertaken to ensure optimal outcomes for patients. Dr Kinjal Mehta graduated from National University of Singapore and did her Orthopaedic Surgery training in Singapore. Dr Kinjal underwent Foot and Ankle fellowship training in Canada under Dr Mark Glazebrook in 2015. Dr Kinjal is Deputy Vice Chair (Research) of Musculoskeletal Academic Clinical Programme. She works with other key stakeholders to improve research avenues within SingHealth cluster. She is passionate about research and attends conferences on a regular basis to present her research. She has been invited as faculty in local and regional workshops, conferences and webinars. Dr Kinjal is active in teaching medical students from various medical schools in Singapore. She is part of core faculty for Orthopaedic residency teaching. She organises foot and ankle cadaveric course regularly to enhance the learning and surgical skills of junior doctors. Due to her commitment to education, Dr Kinjal won the Changi General Hospital Best Educator Award 2022 – the Guru.

name: Kiong Liqin Kimberley
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Kiong completed her undergraduate medical training in NUS and ENT Residency in Singhealth. She underwent further subspecialty training in Head & Neck Surgery at the prestigious MD Anderson Cancer Center, Houston, USA. She sees ENT, head & neck and thyroid conditions, with a special interest in endoscopic surgery for skull base tumours and robotic surgery for oropharyngeal tumours. She also participates actively in research with more than 30 publications in peer-reviewed medical journals

name: Ko Kwan Ki
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Pathology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Karrie is currently a consultant in Singapore General Hospital. She works in the department of Microbiology, and the department of Molecular Pathology. Karrie is a NMRC Research Fellowship Awardee and is working in the Laboratory of Metagenomic Technologies and Microbial Systems in the Genome Institute of Singapore. She is involved in routine clinical diagnostics in bacteriology and infectious disease molecular diagnostics.

name: Koh Fangju, Beatrice (Xu Fangju)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Koh Hong Xiang, Frederick(Gao Hongxiang)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Frederick Koh is a Consultant in the Department of Colorectal Surgery, Division of General Surgery, Sengkang General Hospital. Dr Koh graduated from the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore (NUS) in 2012 and received his formal surgical training in National University Health System (NUHS). He completed his membership of the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh (Surgery) in 2014, and obtained the Masters of Medicine (Surgery) from the NUS in 2016. He completed the General Surgery Residency Programme as the Chief Resident for General Surgery in 2019 and become a Fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh (General Surgery) in 2019. He now practices in Sengkang General Hospital's Department of General Surgery and is subspecialising in Colorectal Surgery. His clinical interests include the various aspects of colorectal surgical oncology, proctology as well as acute care surgery. Receiving training in both minimally invasive and open surgery, Dr Koh is well versed in both laparoscopic and conventional open techniques of surgery. Other than clinical contributions, Dr Koh places emphasis on the importance of Academic Surgery. He is published in numerous peer-reviewed journals and enjoys both clinical as well as basic science research, pertaining to the field of General and Colorectal Surgery. He has held grants and has experience in conducting both population-based and trial-based research and intends to continue his pursuit of Academia in order to continue to participate in the continuous improvement to clinical practice to serve his patients best. Dr Koh also believes in Clinical Education and is active in the development of future doctors and specialists. He was awarded the Dean's Award for Teaching Excellence AY 18/19 by NUS. His contributions to Clinical and Research Education have been recognised with the Role Model Certificate of Commendation and Wong Hock Boon Society, Singapore Medical Association Charity Fund Outstanding Mentor Award.

name: Koh Jit Hin, Adrian
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Adjunct Associate Professor
summary: Dr Adrian Koh Jit Hin is currently a Senior Consultant with the Department of Surgery as well as the current Director of Education Office at Changi General Hospital (CGH). He has a subspecialty interest in head and neck surgery, for which he has been appointed as director. Dr Koh graduated from the National University of Singapore (NUS) in 1993 and become a fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh and Glasgow after completing his advanced specialist training in general surgery. He then subsequently embarked on a head and neck surgery fellowship programme at the Queen Mary Hospital (Hong Kong University) under the Human Manpower Development Plan (HMDP) Award. Apart from clinical work, Dr Koh believes strongly in educating our future generation of doctors and has been appointed as the director of Undergraduate Medical Education in CGH and associate dean for the Yong Loo Lin School Of Medicine at NUS. His dedication for teaching and medical education has won him multiple awards which includes the Best Educator Award.

name: Koh Liang Khai Darren
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Darren Koh serves as a Senior Consultant Anaesthesiologist at Singapore General Hospital. He is the Education Director of the SGH Campus and holds pivotal roles as the Clinical Educator Lead (Medical) and Associate Dean at the NUS-Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine. Additionally, he maintains adjunct and clinical associate professorships at NUS-Medicine and Duke-NUS Medical School, respectively. His leadership is instrumental in steering the SGH educational pillar within the SGH’s Academic Medical Centre, guiding the direction and oversight of all undergraduate and postgraduate education and training for all professional groups. A/Prof Koh's professional journey commenced at the National University of Singapore, where he earned his Bachelor of Medicine & Surgery (MBBS) in 1993, followed by a Master of Medicine (Anaesthesia) in 1998. He was honoured with the HMDP award in 2000, subsequently completing an advanced fellowship in Neuroanaesthesia and Neuro-Intensive Care. His professional recognitions include a fellowship at the Academy of Medicine, Singapore, and a Graduate Diploma in Acupuncture. He is also duly registered with the Traditional Chinese Medicine Practitioners Board (Acupuncture) under the Ministry of Health. In his role as a clinician-academician, A/Prof Koh actively contributes to the academic sphere as a lecturer and tutor, engaging in problem-based learning, simulation-based learning, health-ethics law, professionalism, and educational leadership training programs.

name: Koh Shao Hui (Xu Shaohui)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Koh Shao Hui completed his medical undergraduate studies at the Yong Loo Lin School of medicine, National University of Singapore in 2009. He subsequently completed his training with the SingHealth Service Emergency Medicine Residency Programme in 2017. Dr Koh joined the Department of Emergency Medicine, Sengkang General Hospital as an Associate Consultant in 2017. He has a keen clinical interest in Trauma care and Clinical Acupuncture and is also actively engaged in Healthcare Informatics and ED Operative Improvement. He also practices as an Acupuncturist in SKH.

name: Koh Si Ya Natalie
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Natalie Koh is a Consultant from Department of Cardiology at National Heart Centre Singapore.

name: Koh Siyue Mariko
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Koh is a Senior Consultant in the Department of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine, Singapore General Hospital. She is also currently the Clinical Director of SingHealth Clinical Trials Coordinating Centre. Assoc Prof Koh‘s key clinical and research interests include Difficult- to-treat Asthma, identification and treatment of phenotypes in Severe Asthma. She established the Singapore General Hospital Severe Asthma Phenotype Study (SGH-SAPS) in 2011 and has >500 severe asthma patients in the registry who are being followed longitudinally. ORCID: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1230-0129

name: Koh Su-Mei Angela
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Angela Koh is a senior consultant and clinician scientist with the Department of Cardiology at the National Heart Centre Singapore. Her clinical interests are multimodality cardiovascular imaging and cardiovascular disease. She is thrice awarded by the National Medical Research Council, Singapore, as a Clinician Scientist and leads as principal investigator in several clinical research studies. ‬‬‬

name: Koh Suang Bee Joyce
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Dr Joyce Koh is a senior consultant orthopaedic surgeon at the Singapore General Hospital. She is from the Trauma Service and has subspecialty interests in Upper Extremity and OrthoGeriatric Trauma. She is usually referred complex trauma especially of the shoulder and elbow with a need for reconstruction or replacement and have also worked in multi-disciplinary efforts with the Plastic surgeons. Research interests include Geriatric trauma with specific interests in atypical femur fractures for which she has numerous publications with good citations, presentations and ongoing collaborative research. She also regularly reviews for established journals including Osteoporosis International, New England Journal of Medicine, JAMA Open, JAMDA and British Journal of Anaesthesia. Another area of interest and research is in novel emerging technologies. She and the local team work closely with Professor Bernard F Morrey and colleagues from the Mayo Clinic, Rochester, MN on the early safety and efficacy trials and clinical implementation of a device for image-guided minimally invasive ultrasonic tenotomy for recalcitrant tendinopathy, and has completed the long term follow-up of the technology which is accepted for publication. The technology has successfully translated into clinical use in the USA and the Asean region. Currently, Dr Koh is on the Shoulder Expert Taskforce of the AO Foundation, involved in the design of state-of-the-art upper extremity trauma devices. In December 2024, the team was awarded the AO Technical Commission Innovation Prize 2024 for development of the VA Clavicle System. Dr Koh is involved in numerous post-graduate skills training courses as local chair and frequently as regional faculty. She previously served as the Singhealth Surgical Skills Center Musculoskeletal Platform Director, and has been involved in the start-up of the skills laboratory to a level of international excellence, holding many musculoskeletal workshops since its inception to its amalgamation with the current academic pillars. She organized multiple pre-congress Workshops and the inaugural Singhealth-Mayo Symposium was successfully run with the S3 Congress in 2019. She previously served as Regional Program Contributor for Upper extremity trauma with AOTrauma from 2019 to 2022 and is also active as faculty in the Mayo Elbow Club and the Asian Morrey Elbow Club. Currently, she sits on the Residency Advisory Committee for Orthopaedic Surgery with the Joint Commission on Specialist Training in Singapore and also serves as the International Program Editor for Upper Extremity Trauma, representing the Asia-Pacific region for AO Trauma International. Dr Koh is the Vice-chair for Research of the Musculoskeletal Academic Clinical Program She is the Chair of the Singapore General Hospital Hip Fracture value-based care workgroup and works closely with Singhealth, Duke-NUS and the Ministry of Health, Singapore to drive value-driven care initiatives. She was previously on the MOH Total Hip Value-driven care and OneRehab committees. She is also on the Medical Device Advisory Committee of the Health Sciences Authority of Singapore and currently co-chairs the Medical Devices Committee of the Singapore General Hospital. Regionally, she served as the Secretary of the Asia-Pacific Orthopaedic Research Section, driving Orthopaedic research initiatives in Asean and the Asia-Pacific region. She is the President of the Asia-Pacific Orthopaedic Research Section of the Asia-Pacific Orthopaedic Association, a member organization of the International Combined Orthopaedic Research Societies, from 2021 to 2023. She is also a current member of the Osteoporosis ACE Clinical Guidance Expert Group with the Ministry of Health, Singapore. Dr Koh is the current Vice-President of the Singapore Orthopaedic Association.

name: Koh Tian Hai
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: ​Clinical Professor Koh Tian Hai is an Emeritus and Senior Consultant, Department of Cardiology, National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS). His sub-specialty interest is in interventional cardiology. Clin Prof Koh has extensive experience in interventional techniques, including the use of atherectomy and valvuloplasty procedures with a special interest in acute myocardial infarction interventions.

name: Koh Tong San
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Adjunct Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Koh Tong San’s research interest is on developing quantitative methods for functional oncologic imaging which include dynamic contrast-enhanced imaging (DCE) using CT or MRI, diffusion weighted imaging, dynamic PET and the computational analysis of such images. Functional oncologic imaging allows for the assessment of physiological information pertaining to the tumor microenvironment by imaging the transport/movement of molecular tracers within the tumor. He has developed two versions of the distributed parameter model (mammillary and capillary distributed) for DCE imaging which are physiologically more realistic in portraying tumor microcirculation than existing methods. He has also developed DCE methods for measuring molecular diffusivity within the tumor interstitium and for the assessment of splenic and splanchnic contributions to portal venous flow in liver cirrhosis.

name: Koh Tse Hsien
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Pathology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Dr Koh graduated from University of Aberdeen in 1991. He completed his specialist microbiology training at the Singapore General Hospital and the Royal London Hospital. He subsequently obtained a Master of Science (MSc) in Clinical Microbiology (London), as well as a Diploma in Tropical Medicine and Hygiene (London). He was conferred both FRCPath and FRCPA fellowships in 2000. In 2006 he did a HMDP Fellowship on the ‘Detection and Characterization of Novel Types of Antimicrobial Resistance in Gram-Negative Bacilli under the supervision of Dr Yoshikazu Ishii at Toho University, Tokyo, Japan. This was followed in 2008 by an attachment to Leiden University Medical Centre under the supervision of Dr Lenie Dijkshoorn to study the molecular epidemiology of Acinetobacter spp.

name: Koh Ye Xin (Xu Yexin)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Koh Ye Xin is a Senior Consultant at the Department of Hepatopancreatobiliary and Transplant Surgery in Singapore General Hospital and a member of the Singapore Hepato-Pancreato-Biliary Association (HPBA-S). Dr Koh serves as the Co-Chairman in the Quality Improvement Committee of the Surgery Academic Clinical Program. Dr Koh graduated from the Singhealth General Surgery Residency Programme and the Singapore Chief Residency Programme from the Healthcare Leadership College. Dr Koh is a Fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons, Edinburgh and holds a Master of Medicine (Surgery). He was the Asia-Pacific awardee of the Japan Hepatopancreatobiliary Society observership to Hokkaido University for training in advanced hepatopancreatobilary malignancy. He was a senior clinical fellow in Abdominal Transplantation at Cambridge University, Addenbrookes hospital, focusing on Multivisceral and Intestinal transplantation. Dr Koh has authored several scientific publications in regional and international journals and has delivered multiple scientific presentations at conferences worldwide. He is the recipient of the Surgery Academic Clinical Programme Nuturing Clinican Scientist Award and is currently collaborating with A Star scientists and NTU engineers to develop novel bioengineering techniques to create a perfusable pancreatic islet cell carriage device and recellularisation of dellularised of pancreatric parenchyma matrices. Dr Koh is passionate about passing on knowledge and contributes to education and training as an Assistant Professor in the National University Singapore, Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine and Duke NUS Medical School. He also and serves as a physician faculty to the Singhealth General Surgery Residency Programme and as a mentor to the general surgical chief residents of the Singapore Chief Residency Programme.

name: Koji Itahana
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Associate Professor
summary: I obtained B.S. and Ph.D. degree at the faculty of science, Kyoto University in Japan. I subsequently received the postdoctoral training at the laboratories of Dr. Judith Campisi, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory and Dr. Yanping Zhang, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill in US, where I worked extensively on ARF-MDM2-p53 pathway. I identified the nucleolar protein B23/NPM1 and the mitochondrial protein p32 as ARF-binding partners. I also reported the E3 ligase-deficient Mdm2 knock-in mouse model for studying the E3 ligase activity of MDM2 in vivo. I have been an Assistant Professor at Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School Singapore since Oct 2009. My laboratory published several papers related to the critical role of ARF-MDM2-p53 pathway in cancer. The current focus of my research is finding the novel non-canonical tumor suppressive functions of ARF and p53 and cancer metabolism.

name: Kong Tze Yean
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Koo Wen Hsin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr Koo Wen Hsin is a Senior Consultant (Division of Medical Oncology) at the National Cancer Centre Singapore (NCCS), as well as the Associate Programme Director for SingHealth Internal Medicine residency and SingHealth Group Director for Education. Dr Koo also holds academic positions in the Duke-NUS Medical School and the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine at the National University of Singapore. He also set up the Academic Medicine Education Institute (AMEI). As the chairman for the Medical Oncology Specialist Training Committee for several years, he successfully guided the various institutions in transiting to senior residency in medical oncology. It is also with this same collaborative spirit that Dr Koo helped set up and provided oncologic training to physicians at Tan Tock Seng Hospital, Changi General Hospital and KKH Women’s and Children’s Hospital. Dr Koo has also initiated many new educational programmes for the upgrading and training for those in the medical profession. He also plays an active role in the education of oncology nurses and lecturers for the Master of Nursing Course at NUS and the Palliative courses at Singapore Hospice Council.

name: Ku Chee Wai
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Obstetrics & Gynaecology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Assistant Professor

name: Kuan Kaibin Kelvin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Kelvin Kuan Kaibin is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Emergency Medicine at Changi General Hospital (CGH). He received his medical degree from the National University of Singapore (NUS), School of Medicine in 2006. Dr Kuan received his specialist training in the Department of Emergency Medicine at CGH and completed his specialist training in 2015.

name: Kuhan Venugopal
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: I completed my medical school in United Kingdom in 2011 and completed the Singapore Emergency Medicine Residency Programme in 2018. I joined SKH DEM as an associate consultant in the same year. My interest in critical and trauma care has allowed me to continue pushing the boundaries of patient care through evidence based medicine. As a keen medical educator, I use my role as the Duke-NUS SKH DEM Deputy Coordinator to engage medical students in their learning journey during their posting in EM as well as improve my education pedagogy.

name: Kui Swee Leng
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Kui Swee Leng, Michelle is a Consultant from the Department of Cardiology with the National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS). She subspecialises in cardiac magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and echocardiography.

name: Kularatna Muhandiramalage Sanjeewa Madur
department: Signature Research Programme in Health Services Research & Population Health
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Sanjeewa Kularatna is a health economist and researcher in health services, currently serving at the Programme in Health Services & Systems Research at Duke-NUS, as well as holding a position at the National Heart Research Institute Singapore and National Heart Centre Singapore. His expertise centres on evaluating patient-reported outcomes, focusing on quantifying utility measures and quality-adjusted life years (QALYs). His work also extends to the economic analysis of healthcare interventions and enhancing evidence-based decision-making in healthcare contexts. He has developed methodologies to assess patient and consumer preferences within healthcare service organisations through discrete choice experiments. He is committed to creating collaborative programs aimed at redesigning healthcare delivery models. A central objective of his work in Singapore involves devising collaborative strategies to reduce hospital admissions. He is an experienced PhD mentor and welcomes interested students. He has published over 100 manuscripts of international significance and mentored many clinicians in health services research.

name: Kuo Chung-Liang
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Kuo is a fellowship-trained Senior Consultant with the Department of Orthopaedic Surgery at Changi General Hospital (CGH). He has an interest in all aspects of shoulder and elbow surgeries including arthroplasty and arthroscopy. He manages various degenerative disease, sports related injuries as well as trauma around the shoulder and elbow joints on a regular basis.

name: Kurugulasigamoney Gunasegaran
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Assistant Professor
summary: ​Assistant Professor K Gunasegaran is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Cardiology and Director of the Cardiology Fellowship Programme at the National Heart Centre Singapore. His subspeciality interest is in echocardiography.

name: Kwa Lay Hoon, Andrea(Ke Liyun)
department: Centre for Clinician-Scientist Plus Development
title: Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Andrea Kwa graduated with a degree in Pharmacy from the National University of Singapore in 1996 and received her Doctor of Pharmacy Degree from the Albany College of Pharmacy and Health Sciences in New York in 2006. She then completed a two-year postdoctoral research fellowship at the University of Pittsburgh’s Division of Infectious Diseases, where she researched anti-fungal resistance and molecular diagnostics. Assoc Prof Kwa is currently a Pharmacy Clinician Scientist and Assistant Director, Pharmacy (Research), at the Singapore General Hospital. She is also a faculty member of the Emerging Infectious Diseases program at Duke-NUS Medical School. Assoc Prof Kwa specializes in critical care medicine, infectious diseases and antimicrobial resistance research. She helms a laboratory studying antimicrobial pharmacodynamics/pharmacokinetics and resistance. She also performs health services research involving antimicrobial stewardships, risk factors and outcomes of resistant infections. Assoc Prof Kwa has authored more than 100 publications and is a reviewer for many scientific journals. https://orcid.org/0000-0001-8981-4411

name: Kwek Jin Wei
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Kwek Jin Wei is a Senior Consultant with the Division of Oncological Imaging (DOI), National Can-cer Center Singapore (NCCS). He graduated from the National University of Singapore (MBBS) in 1996. Completing his training in Diagnostic Radiology, he became a Fellow of the Royal College of Radiology (FRCR, UK) in April 2001 and finished his Advanced Specialty Training in Diagnostic Radi-ology in Singapore in May 2003. In 2004, he was honored to receive a Human Manpower Develop-ment Plan Award by the Ministry of Health Singapore for Oncologic Body Imaging at MD Anderson Cancer Center, Houston, Texas, USA. Furthermore, he obtained an Advanced Diploma in Healthcare Management and Leadership from Singapore Management University and SingHealth Institute, where he graduated as the Valedictorian in 2013. Currently, he holds the position of Deputy Director of the Division of Cancer Education (DCE), On-cology ACP, overseeing the Faculty Development and Support Education Unit (FDSEU). Serving as a faculty member of the Singhealth Radiology Residency Program and as a Clinical Professor (Duke NUS Medical School), he finds fulfillment in educating medical students, radiology resi-dents, senior residents, and radiology fellows associated with the Division. From 2008 to 2022, he chaired the Disease Outbreak Response (DORS) Taskforce of the NCCS. In recognition of his contri-butions to managing the impact of COVID-19 in Singapore, he was awarded the National Day Public Administrative Medal (Bronze) (Covid-19) in 2023. With a portfolio of publications in international and local journals, he has also been a featured speaker at numerous international and local conferences.

name: Lai Fook Onn
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Lai Fook Onn is a Senior Consultant with the Dept of Anaesthesiology.

name: Lai Hwei Meeng Angeline
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Angeline Lai is a Senior Consultant in the Genetics Service, Department of Paediatrics in KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital (KKH). She is Adjunct Associate Professor, Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, and also serves as faculty in Duke-NUS Medical School, NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, and in the Singhealth Paediatrics Residency Program. In her clinical practice, she manages children and adults with chromosomal and genetic conditions. She co-directs the BRIDGES (Bringing Research Initiatives for the Diagnosis of GEnetic diseases in Singapore) programme in KKH, which aims to apply new genomic technologies to improve diagnosis and management of patients with genetic conditions.

name: Lai Shieh Mei
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Lai Shieh Mei is a Senior Consultant with the department of Emergency Medicine at Changi General Hospital (CGH). She received her medical education from the National University of Singapore (NUS) and completed specialist training in emergency medicine in 2008. Dr Lai's interest is in emergency cardiac care and critical care.

name: Laing Christopher James
department: Office of Innovation & Entrepreneurship
title: Associate Professor
summary: Since January 2020, Christopher Laing has been Associate Professor (Practice Track) and Vice Dean for Office of Innovation and Entrepreneurship at Duke-NUS, with responsibility for technology commercialization, industry collaborations, and innovation education. He is also the Co-Chair of the SingHealth Duke-NUS Academic Medicine Innovation Institute, and Co-Chair of the Joint Centre for Technology and Development. In addition to creating strategic partnerships for the School in the areas of innovation and entrepreneurship, he works directly with researchers and clinical innovators on developing and commercializing their projects, and in teaching early career researchers and professionals about innovation and commercialization. In 2022, on a year-long hiatus from Duke-NUS, Chris served as the inaugural Chief Executive of ClavystBio. ClavystBio is building Singapore’s life sciences ecosystem through direct investments in promising startups, through engagement of universities in ecosystem development, and through collaboration on the development of Singapore’s life sciences innovation district at Science Park 1. Prior to coming to Singapore, from 2017 to 2019, Chris was the inaugural Executive Director of Capital City Innovation, an organization that is coordinating the stakeholders of the innovation district emerging around Austin’s new health complex. Anchored by a collaboration among the University of Texas at Austin’s Dell Medical School, Ascension Health, and Central Health, and supported by the Downtown Austin Alliance, Opportunity Austin, and community stakeholders, Austin’s innovation district is committed to health innovation, inclusive place-making and healthy community. It has been recognized as an up-and-coming innovation district by the National League of Cities and the Association of University Research Parks. Previously Chris was a member of the senior management team of Philadelphia’s University City Science Center, the country’s oldest and largest urban innovation hub, where he managed relationships between the innovation district and more than 30 academic institutions and had specific oversight of commercialization programs, startup investment, and business incubation/acceleration. His programs engaged more than 300 academic, company, and investment innovators and projects. Chris became an Eisenhower Fellow in 2015, through which he had the opportunity to engage with government, academic, and business leaders in China’s emerging urban innovation centers. He is a member of the Philadelphia College of Physicians and the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons. Prior to joining the Science Center, Chris worked for a number of life sciences start-up companies, ran a research program at the University of Pennsylvania, and was a practicing companion animal veterinarian in the United Kingdom. He completed his veterinary training and his PhD (in endocrinology) at the University of Sydney, and his post-doctoral training (in molecular biology) at the University of Pennsylvania’s School of Medicine.

name: Lakshminarayanan Rajamani
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Lakshminarayanan is the Co-Head of the Ocular Infections & Anti-Microbials Group and Principal Investigator II at the SERI. He has a joint appointment as Associate Professor with the Department of Pharmacy, NUS and ACP in Vision and Ophthalmology, Duke-NUS. He received his PhD from the Department of Chemistry at the National University of Singapore in 2003. His group received the prestigious SingHealth Publish Award for the years 2017 and 2018 - an award recognizes basic research peer-reviewed articles with good translational values. He was one of the recipients of the prestigious Singapore Millennium Foundation - Post Doctoral Fellowship and continued in the same department till September 2005. From December 2005 - April 2009 he worked as Research Associate at the University Southern California before joining the Singapore Eye Research Institute as Senior Research Scientist in May 2009.

name: Lalit Kumar Radha Krishna
department: MD Programme Department
title: Professor
summary: I have been a Senior Consultant in Palliative Medicine at the National Cancer Centre Singapore. Over the last 18 years in Singapore I have focused upon the provision of holistic care of cancer patients with complex symptomology. I believe this is best accomplished through a combination of clinical skills, a team based approach and evidence based practice. To realize this i have sought to combine personal and professional development with effective support of my team through mentoring and peer support and guiding practice through clinical, ethics, professionalism, communications and education research. Thus i have complemented my educational pursuits in the form of my Masters in Ethics and Masters in Medical Education, PhD in Medical Ethics, MD in Palliative Medicine and my ongoing PhD in Medical Education with research into Professional identity Formation and its links with professionalism, communication, moral distress and compassion fatigue to better support staff and enhance patient care

name: Lam Joyce Ching Mei
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Joyce Lam is a Senior Consultant in the Haematology/Oncology Service and Head of the Haematology Laboratory and Blood Bank Service at KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital. She graduated from the National University of Singapore in 2000 with a basic medical degree and obtained her Membership from the Royal College of Paediatrics and Child Health, United Kingdom, and Master of Medicine (Paediatric Medicine) qualifications in 2004. She became a Fellow of the Academy of Medicine, Singapore in 2008 and obtained Fellowship of the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia in 2013. She is a practicing Paediatric Haematologist / Oncologist with special clinical and research interests in non-malignant haematological conditions, laboratory haematology and general paediatric oncology. She is also actively involved in undergraduate medical education and is an Adjunct Assistant Professor with the Duke-NUS Graduate School of Medicine.

name: Lam Shao Wei, Sean
department: Signature Research Programme in Health Services Research & Population Health
title: Adjunct Associate Professor
summary: A/Prof Sean Lam has a PhD and Master’s in Industrial and Systems Engineering, Operations and Business Analytics from the National University of Singapore. He is currently the Deputy Director and Head (Data Science) in the SingHealth Duke-NUS Academic Medical Centre. Sean is with the Signature Programme in Health Services and Systems Research and the SingHealth Duke NUS Global Health Institute. He is also a Faculty Fellow with the Singapore Biodesign Programme. He has more than 80 international publications and recipient of numerous local and international awards.

name: Lam Su Ping, Carolyn
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Professor
summary: Professor Carolyn Lam is a Senior Consultant from the Department of Cardiology and Director of Women's Heart Health at the National Heart Centre Singapore, having pioneered the first Women’s Heart Clinic in Singapore. Academically, she serves as a tenured Full Professor at the Duke-National University of Singapore, having also graduated from the Standford Executive Programme in 2015, and obtained a PhD at the University Medical Centre Groningen, the Netherlands in 2016. In the field of MedTech, Prof Lam is co-founder of Us2.ai, an award-wining startup dedicated to the automation of the fight against heart disease by applying artificial intelligence to echocardiography (ultrasound of the heart). Prof Lam is a world-renowned specialist in heart failure (particularly heart failure with preserved ejection fraction [HFpEF]). Her work in the PARAGON-HF and EMPEROR-Preserved trials led to the first FDA-approved treatment for HFpEF and the first robustly positive clinical outcomes trial in HFpEF to-date, respectively. She leads several multinational global and regional clinical trials, and has received numerous prestigious awards and global recognition for her work. Her recent appointments on the 2021 European Society of Cardiology Heart Failure Guidelines Task Force, and as International Honorary Fellow of the Heart Failure Society of America 2021, attest to her contributions in both cardiology and research fields. Prof Lam is a proud recipient of the National Medical Research Council (NMRC) Senior Investigator Clinician Scientist Award in 2020, and served as the Founding Programme Lead of A*STaR’s Asian neTwork for Translational Research and Cardiovascular Trials (ATTRaCT) and Principal Investigator of ASIAN-HF (a multinational study across 11 Asian countries). In 2019, She initiated the Asian Diabetes Outcomes Prevention Trial (ADOPT), an ongoing trial involving 5 countries and aimed at reducing the cardiovascular adverse outcomes of diabetes in Asian patients. She has published with over 350 articles in major high impact journals including New England Journal of Medicine, Journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA), Lancet, Circulation, and European Heart Journal; and has been recognised as a World Expert by Expertscape’s PubMed-based algorithms (top 0.1% of scholars writing about Heart Failure over the past 10 years; Heart Failure: Worldwide - Expertscape.com). Besides being an Associate Editor for Circulation (top Cardiology Journal) and European Journal of Heart Failure, she is also the lead author of the chapter on HFpEF for Braunwald’s Heart Disease: A Textbook of Cardiovascular Medicine, 12th Edition - the award-winning textbook trusted by generations of cardiologists for the latest, most reliable guidance in the field. Prof Lam serves as a consultant on several global advisory boards for cardiovascular disease, as a member of the Executive Committees of global heart failure trials, and as an Associate Editor for Circulation and the European Journal of Heart Failure. Finally, she is heard weekly on the global podcast "Circulation On The Run" and seen regularly on television as the Resident Doctor of the health programme "Body and Soul" by MediaCorp Singapore.

name: Lam Wee Leon
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: I graduated from the University of Edinburgh and did my basic surgical training in Leeds. Thereafter I completed my Plastic Surgery training in Manchester, followed by a microsurgery fellowship at Chang Gong Memorial Hospital, Taiwan, a hand fellowship in Sheffield, UK and a congenital hand fellowship at Great Ormond Street, London. I was a consultant Plastic and Hand Surgeon at the Royal Hospital for Children and Young People, Edinburgh and St John's Hospital, Livingston until 2023 when i returned to Singapore for a post as Senior Consultant at Singapore General Hospital. My interest is in both adult and pediatric hand surgery, particularly congenital hand surgery. I also have a keen intertest in the management of nerve-related injuries including stroke, cerebral palsy and other spasticity disorders. I have keen interest in research and currently the Editor-in-Chief of the Journal of Hand Surgery (European Volume). Internationally, I am the President-elect for the Bristish Society for Surgery of the Hand.

name: Lam Wing Chuen Winnie
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Lam has post-graduate medical training in both Nuclear Medicine and Diagnostic Radiology. She has also done clinical training in the Department of Nuclear Medicine at University Hospital Zurich. Dr Lam was the inaugural Program Director of the National Nuclear Medicine Residency Program in 2012-2018 as well as Clinical Physician Faculty Member of the Diagnostic Radiology and Endocrinology Residency Programs. Her clinical interests include oncologic and non-oncologic applications of PET/CT, SPECT/CT and radionuclide therapy.

name: Lam Yeng Po, Paula
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Adjunct Associate Professor
summary: I graduated from the University of Leeds in UK majoring in Molecular Biology and Biochemistry. Following which, I received postdoctoral trainings at St Jude Children’s Research Hospital and The Chinese University of Hong Kong where I gained valuable insights into the mechanisms of cancer development and the potential use of cells and viral vectors as delivery tools for gene therapy, which later became the focus of my research at Massachusetts General Hospital and the National Cancer Center in Singapore. Towards end of 2017, I joined a startup company where I lead the RnD team towards developing novel strategies to enhance viral productivity, while continuing to collaborate with academics to foster research in immunotherapies. Starting 2024, I was appointed the CSO of BioCell Innovations Pte Ltd., Singapore where our primary objective is to bring cell and gene therapy to patients-in-need, offering them effective, affordable, and personalized treatment options.

name: Lam Yun Rui Amanda (Lan Yunrui)
department: Faculty Advancement Department
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Amanda Lam is a Consultant Endocrinologist at Singapore General Hospital. She graduated from Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore in 2009 and later obtained her MRCP(UK) and M.Med (Internal Medicine) in 2012. She completed her specialist training in Endocrinology and Diabetes at Singapore General Hospital in 2017. She is currently a clinical lecturer for the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine.

name: Lamers Mart Matthias
department: Signature Research Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Mart Lamers is an Assistant Professor at Duke-NUS Medical School and group leader of the coronavirus lab in the Programme of Emerging Infectious Diseases. Mart received his BS in Biology from the University of Amsterdam in 2013 and his MS in Infection & Immunity from the Erasmus MC in 2015. In 2022, he completed his PhD at the Erasmus MC in the group of prof. Bart Haagmans on the topic of coronavirus pathogenesis and evolution. Mart is interested in virus-host interactions relevant to pandemic potential and studies these in organoid systems of humans and animals. He aims to identify animal coronaviruses that are able to infect humans, uncover the mechanisms by which they infect human cells, and investigate the susceptibility to these viruses at the population level. The planet’s current changes in human and domestic animal population size, urbanization, global connectivity, climate, wildlife trade, ecosystem health and land use, guarantee that humanity will face new, previously unrecognized animal viruses that can infect humans (zoonotic viruses) in the next decades. Among zoonotic viruses, coronaviruses pose a high risk to global health due to their abundance and diversity in wildlife and proven ability to adapt to infect and spread between humans. This is not only exemplified by the ongoing SARS-CoV-2 pandemic that has had a devastating societal and economic impact over the past three years, but also by the emergence of particularly virulent SARS-CoV in 2003-2004 and MERS-CoV since 2012. Therefore, there is an urgent need to better prepare for future outbreaks of zoonotic coronaviruses, and especially assess their likelihood of achieving human-to-human transmission. His lab aims to to identify animal coronaviruses that are able to infect humans, uncover the mechanisms by which they infect human cells, and investigate the susceptibility to these viruses at the population level. Such viruses will be identified through various experiments directed at assessing whether and how these viruses overcome barriers known to restrict viruses from infecting human cells. As viruses are obligate intracellular parasites that rely on a plethora of fine-tuned virus-host interactions, this research needs to be performed in cells that accurately mimic viral target cells in vivo. This can be achieved using organoid technology, in which differentiated, physiologically relevant human or animal cells are grown from self-renewing stem cell cultures to grow ‘mini-organs’. The application of organoid technology (alongside other technologies, e.g. CRISPR/Cas9, single cell omics, and antigenic mapping) in this research will lead to a better understanding of the molecular mechanisms that allow coronaviruses to cause pandemics. This will aid in the development of diagnostics, therapeutics and vaccines to coronaviruses that may cause future pandemics, and ultimately aid in predicting coronavirus pandemic potential based on genomic sequences. Our cutting-edge platforms and an approach on coronaviruses can be translated and used for other emerging pathogens.

name: Lao Zhentang
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Lao graduated from the National University of Singapore, School of Medicine in 2007. He obtained his haematology specialist accredited in 2017. His areas of interest are acute leukemia and genetics of myeloid malignancies. He was awarded NRMC Research Fellowship to support his research development in 2017 and has completed his PhD.

name: Lau Man Chun Jeffrey
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Lau is a Senior Consultant from Department of Cardiology at the National Heart Centre Singapore. He graduated with his Doctor of Medicine and Doctor of Philosophy from Washington University, St. Louis, USA and received his post-graduate internal medicine and non-invasive cardiology training at the Barnes-Jewish Hospital. He is a well-published expert in the area of Positron Emission Tomography/ Magnetic Resonance (PET/ MR) cardiac imaging. Clin Asst Prof Lau also specialises in using Echocardiography in diagnostic and preventive cardiology. He is also heavily involved in medical education and currently leads the SingHealth cardiology senior residency training programme at NHCS.

name: Law Yan Mee
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Law Yan Mee holds the positions of Senior Consultant at the Department of Diagnostic Radiology in Singapore General Hospital (SGH) and Clinical Assistant Professor at the Duke NUS Medical School. With a career spanning over two decades, Dr Law is a highly respected radiologist who has earned a strong professional reputation in Singapore and international medical communities in the field of abdominal imaging, particularly in genitourinary radiology. Upon receiving her postgraduate degree from the Royal College of Radiologists in 2005, she underwent advanced radiology training in the Singapore General Hospital (SGH). In 2008, she left for the USA to become one of the first local radiologists to be fellowship trained in the subspecialty of genitourinary imaging. In 2013, she was selected to collaborate at the National Institute of Health (NIH), USA in advanced prostate cancer MRI imaging, where she co-authored over 15 peer-reviewed scientific publications and 2 book chapters with leading world experts in the field. These collaborations to advance this critical field of cancer diagnosis with cutting edge research are on-going today. In the present day, Dr Law has been chosen as a key member of a global panel of radiologists participating in ground-breaking projects to validate the use of artificial intelligence (AI) deep learning for MRI prostate imaging. This initiative holds the potential to significantly enhance the quality of early prostate cancer diagnosis based on MRI. More than anyone else, Dr Law contributed much to magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of prostate in Singapore and today MRI is the gold standard for imaging of prostate cancer. Her expertise in genitourinary radiology is highly sought after and valued by her clinical colleagues in SGH. Urologists across the country and the region frequently seek her expert opinions on complex MRI prostate cases, highlighting her significant impact on patient care and safety, particularly in our aging communities. Her primary area of interest lies in prostate cancer imaging, with a particular focus on early prostate cancer and prostate cancer focal therapy, a rapidly evolving field. As the departmental clinical lead for genitourinary radiology, Dr Law chairs the weekly Multidisciplinary Team conference of the Departments of Urology and Diagnostic Radiology. The consistently high attendance at this conference stands as a testament to the exceptional quality of clinical discussions she facilitates. Recognized regionally for her outstanding work, Dr Law has been invited to lecture at various local and regional meetings, particularly on the subjects of prostate cancer and genitourinary diseases. On the research front, she collaborates with urologists, renal physicians and radiation oncologists and is a co-investigator in several National Medical Research Council grant-funded projects and participates in national and international clinical trials of genitourinary diseases. Dr Law boasts a remarkable portfolio of over 40 publications in peer-reviewed scientific journals, with a H-index of 17. Dr Law is also acknowledged to be an excellent educator and she is actively involved in the teaching of medical students, residents and fellows. She is the Course Director of the SGH Prostate MRI Teaching Course, a very highly subscribed regional educational programme.

name: Lee Cheng Jie Irene
department: Education Advancement Department
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Irene Lee is an Assistant Professor in the Duke-NUS Office of Education, recognized for her contributions across education, service, and research. As an educator, Dr. Lee has focused on advancing early medical education by designing courses for prospective and pre-matriculation medical students. She has co-led the primary pre-clinical course for MD Programme, “Foundations of Patient Care 1 (FPC1)” course, where her leadership has driven curriculum enhancements, including the integration of basic and clinical sciences, cognitive load management, and multi-modal educational approaches. Her commitment to professional growth is underscored by her completion of the Basic Science Medical Education Fellowship sponsored by Duke University School of Medicine and her current pursuit of a Master of Education in the Health Professions at Johns Hopkins University. In her service role, Dr. Lee serves as the Quality Assurance Lead in the Quality Assurance & Accreditation Department, where she collaborates with stakeholders to critically evaluate and advance the MD curriculum. She has co-developed initiatives to enhance the quality management system through data digitization, analytics, and visualization. Notably, she played a pivotal role in Singapore’s first external review of medical schools by leading Duke-NUS's self-assessment against the National Standards for Basic Medical Education. Dr. Lee’s research has transitioned from her early work as a basic scientist to a sophisticated focus on medical education, particularly in response to challenges brought by the COVID-19 pandemic. Her work evolved from rapidly adapting academic coaching and virtual Team-Based Learning (TBL) to implementing hybrid models and conducting a longitudinal study on student engagement in TBL formats. She is committed to improve the practice of education and fostering the next generation of doctors through her unique blend of scientific research rigor, educational experience, training and leadership skills.

name: Lee Chengjie
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Lee Chien Earn
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: I am currently the Deputy Group CEO of Singhealth overseeing the Regional Health System which seeks to enable the population in Eastern Singapore to Keep Well, Get Well and Live Well. My prior appointments included leadership positions in the Ministry of Health in Health Services, Healthcare Performance, Health Regulation and Health Services Finance in the Ministry of Health, Singapore.

name: Lee Chun Fan
department: Centre for Biomedical Data Science
title: Assistant Professor

name: Lee Chung Yin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Lee Chung Yin is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Cardiology at the National Heart Centre Singapore. Clin Asst Prof Lee’s sub-specialty interest is in echocardiography.

name: Lee Cia Sin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Lee Cia Sin is a Consultant Family Physician at the SingHealth Polyclinics. She received her medical degree from the University of Malaya (Malaysia) in 2008, her Master of Medicine (Family Medicine) from National University of Singapore in 2012, as well as her Fellowship of the College of Family Physician (FCFP) in 2017. Her clinical and research interests are in the area of diabetes, geriatric medicine, health services research, and education research. Dr Lee also published in respectable peer-reviewed journals (such as in the Annals of Family Medicine). Currently, she is the site principal investigator for the OPTIMUM trial (Optimizing care of Patients via Telehealth In Monitoring and Augmenting their control of Diabetes Mellitus), which is a randomized controlled trial funded by the MOH Office for Healthcare Transformation to design a new model of care for the future primary care setting. She also teaches medical students at Duke-NUS and Nanyang Technological University.

name: Lee Guat Lay, Caroline
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Associate Professor
summary: A/Prof Caroline Lee obtained her PhD in Genetics from Baylor College of Medicine and did her postdoctoral training with Michael Gottesman at the National Cancer Institute, National Institutes of Health (NCI/NIH). She returned to Singapore to become a faculty at the National University of Singapore with concurrent appointments as Assoc Professor at the Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School as well as Principal Investigator at the National Cancer Centre. She is currently the Deputy Executive Director (Admissions) at the NUS Graduate School of Integrative Sciences and Engineering and is in the Admissions Committee at Duke-NUS Medical School. Her research interests is in functional genomics of hepatocellular cancer, population genetics, and employing high throughput next generation sequencing strategies for understanding disease processes, biomarker discovery, diagnostics and metagenomics. She has more than 100 publications with several reviews and opinion articles.

name: Lee Haur Yueh
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Lee graduated from the National University of Singapore in 2002 and obtained his membership in the Royal College of Physicians (UK) in 2005. He completed his specialty training in dermatology in 2010. His areas of interest include cutaneous adverse drug reactions, autoimmune blistering conditions and medical dermatology. He has undergone further subspecialty training in these areas at the Hôpital Henri Mondor, Assistance Publique - Hôpitaux de Paris, France; Inselspital, Bern, Switzerland ; as well as King’s College Hospital, London, UK

name: Lee Jan Hau
department: Centre for Clinician-Scientist Plus Development
title: Associate Professor
summary: I am currently working as a senior consultant in pediatric critical care medicine at the Children's Intensive Care Unit, KK Women's and Children's Hospital, Singapore. I completed by basic and advanced pediatric training in Singapore. I was admitted into membership for the Royal College of Paediatrics and Child Health, United Kingdom in 2005 and obtained my pediatric specialist accreditation in Singapore in 2009. I did my advanced pediatric critical care medicine and research fellowship at Duke Children's Hospital, Durham and Duke Clinical Research Institute, Durham, respectively from 2011 - 2012. Upon return of my advanced clinical fellowship, I completed the Master’s in Clinical Investigation at the National University of Singapore in 2014. Over the past 5 years, I have focused on clinical research and have planned, executed and completed numerous clinical studies. My main research areas include pediatric critical care epidemiology, severe pediatric respiratory illness and critical care nutrition. Currently, I am leading a regional research network - PACCMAN (Pediatric Acute & Critical Care Medicine Network) to study important pediatric critical illness in the region. Over the past 5 years, I have demonstrated a consistent record of productive research projects, publications and scientific endeavour in pediatric critical care.

name: Lee Kheng Hock
department: MD Programme Department
title: Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Lee obtained his medical degree at the National University of Singapore in 1987. He holds the Master of Medicine Degree in Family Medicine (1993), Diploma in Practical Dermatology (UK) and the Fellow of the College of Family Physicians Singapore (2000). A/Prof Lee served continuously as a Council Member of the College of Family Physicians since 1993 and was the Censor-in-Chief (Chief Examiner) of the College of Family Physicians Singapore from 2005 to 2009. He is presently the Vice-President of the College. He serves in various national level committees that promote training and professionalism in Singapore. He is an examiner in the Master of Medicine (Family Medicine) Exams and the Chief Examiner of the Graduate Diploma in Family Medicine. His area of interest is in health assessment, physician training, complexity science and health services research. He is the Clerkship Director of the Family Medicine Clerkship and the College Master of the Sir Gordon Arthur Ransome Student Advisory College of the school. Assoc Prof Lee is active in promoting research and holds the appointment of Principal Investigator in the Family Medicine Research Network of the Singapore Clinical Research Institute. Before returning to the public sector in 2006, Assoc Prof Lee worked for 10 years as a clinician leader in a private group practice. Presently, he is a Senior Consultant of the Department of Family Medicine and Continuing Care in the Singapore General Hospital and Deputy CEO (Education and Community Partnerships) for SingHealth Community Hospitals. His clinical duties include providing care to hospitalized patients and patients at the specialist outpatient clinic of our hospital.

name: Lee Kong Hwee (Li Guanghui)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Lee Kong Hwee is a senior consultant with the Department of Orthopaedic Surgery at the Singapore General Hospital (SGH). He sub-specialises in knee preservation work, namely realignment osteotomies, cartilage, meniscal and ligament surgeries. Dr Lee is the Director of Sports service & President of Singapore Knee Preservation Society. He is spearheading SGH’s efforts in knee preservation surgeries, and helping patients retain their biologic knees Special Interest 1. Knee Preservation Surgeries a. osteotomy b. cartilage and meniscal transplants c. ligament repair and reconstruction 2. Total or partial knee arthroplasty

name: Lee Kwang Yang Aaron
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Lee is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Anaesthesiology, Singapore General Hospital. After completing his specialist training in Anaesthesiology in Singapore, he underwent fellowship training in neuroanaesthesia at the Royal Prince Alfred Hospital in Sydney, Australia. He has special interests in medical informatics and neuroanaesthesia. He is currently the Director of Clinical IT at Sengkang General Hospital.

name: Lee Kwok Kin
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Research Fellow
summary: I am a research fellow currently in Mei Wang-Casey's lab

name: Lee Lai Heng
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Prof Lee received her medical and advanced specialist training in haematology in Singapore and is currently a practicing haematologist, bestowed with multiple excellent service awards, outstanding clinician award and the national day public service award. Subspecialising in Thrombosis and Haemostasis, she was instrumental in the setting up of the anticoagulation consult and monitoring services to promote patient safety in anticoagulation therapy in SGH. She played a key role in clinical trials in novel anticoagulants in Singapore and the Asia Pacific. Prof Lee has been actively involved in graduate and post graduate medical education. Her interest in haematology research has resulted in more than 70 publications in peer reviewed journals. During her term as Head of Department, she has developed the Department of Haematology to acquire expertise and international repute in areas of haemato-oncology, thrombosis and haemostasis and laboratory haematology. Under her leadership as immediate past Chairman for Division of Research, Singapore General Hospital was awarded NMRC Centre Grant of $18 mil and she ensured a tight governance of research activities and high quality of research at SGH through sound policies which contributed to the successful JCI accreditation for SGH as an Academic Medical Centre.
