He holds multiple research grants including SGH Innovation Grant and NHIC I2A grant. His main research interests include Urinary Stone disease, Men's health and Subfertility, as well as Minimally Invasive Surgery. He has published many peer-reviewed manuscripts. He's also completed Professional Diploma in Smart Health Leadership at the National University of Singapore. Dr. Lu is down to earth, earnest and compassionate. He has received several commendations for his clinical service.

name: Lui Jeen Nee
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinical Associate Professor Lui Jeen Nee obtained her Bachelor of Dental Surgery at the National University of Singapore in 1996. She further obtained her Master in Dental Surgery (Endodontics) at the National University of Singapore and Membership in Endodontics from the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh in 2003. She was conferred a Fellow of the Academy of Medicine Singapore in 2005 and has been accredited as a Specialist in Endodontics by the Singapore Dental Council since 2008. Clinical Associate Professor Lui is currently the Deputy Chief Executive Officer, Clinical and Regional Health and Senior Consultant, Endodontic Unit, Department of Restorative Dentistry, National Dental Centre Singapore. She is actively involved in postgraduate education and is currently holding the position of Associate Programme Director of the Endodontic Postgraduate Residency Training Programme. She is a Council Member of the College of Dental Surgeons, Academy of Medicine and also serves on the Executive Committee of the Society of Endodontists Singapore.

name: Lum Pooi Ming
department: Signature Research Programme in Health Services Research & Population Health
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Elaine LUM BPharm, MClinPharm, PhD, FANZCAP Dr Elaine Lum is Assistant Professor, Health Services & Systems Research at Duke-NUS Medical School and visiting faculty at the Centre for Population Health Research & Implementation (CPHRI), SingHealth, Singapore. She is an implementation scientist and health services researcher whose work has been enriched by frontline experience as a clinical pharmacist in several countries and real-world implementation projects within healthcare systems in Australia and Singapore. Her current work in implementation research focusses on last-mile implementation of healthcare innovations — digital health, AI-assisted clinical decision-making, and new care pathways. Elaine’s research program on people-centred implementation investigates how to design for sustainment while right-sizing the process of implementation to efficaciously deliver desired outcomes. She believes in the value and power of collaborating with people of integrity who do things differently and fail fast, to bring about game-changing practices and innovations for better patient care.

name: Lum Wei Ming Vincent
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Vincent Lum Wei Ming graduated from the National University of Singapore (NUS) and trained under the Singhealth Emergency Medicine Residency Program. Dr Lum has an interest in the provision of palliative care, with the Accident and Emergency (A&E) department at Changi General Hospital (CGH), which led him to obtain a Graduate Diploma in Palliative Medicine. He is also a designated workplace doctor (compressed air works), lasting power of attorney certificate issuer and advance care planning facilitator. Apart from clinical work, he also teaches various life support courses such as BCLS AED, ACLS, ATLS and PHTLS. In addition, he obtained the WSQ Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment to further his interest in teaching and education.

name: Ma Haoran
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: A research fellow at Prof Patrick Tan's lab. Focus on Single-cell RNAseq analysis and spatial transcriptomic analysis.

name: Ma Wai Wai Zaw
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Maciej Piotr Chlebicki
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Piotr Chlebicki is a Senior Consultant in the Department of Infectious Diseases at Singapore General Hospital. After graduating from the Medical Academy of Warsaw, Poland, he completed his residency in Internal Medicine in the USA and then specialized in Infectious Diseases in Singapore. In addition to his clinical practice, he is also in charge of the Antibiotic Stewardship Program at Singapore General Hospital. He participates in research projects investigating the prevention and therapy of infections caused by multi-drug resistant bacteria. He also participates in undergraduate and postgraduate teaching at Singapore General Hospital.

name: Man Eyn Kidd, Ryan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Ryan Man is a Clinician-Scientist with the Population Health Research Unit at the Singapore Eye Research Institute (SERI), and a Junior Principal investigator in the Singapore National Eye Centre (SNEC) Optometry Service. He has a keen interest in assessing the impact of eye diseases and associated treatment modalities from a patient’s perspective using novel and sophisticated adaptive patient-reported outcome measures (PROMs) that tailors the questions administered to respondents to the participants level of visual ability at every step of the testing process using ‘smart’ technology (computerized adaptive testing or CAT). His current research focuses on the feasibility and clinical impact of implementing these novel PROM-CATs into the routine clinical workflow in specialty Ophthalmology clinics. He is also interested in quantifying and correlating the clinical, patient-reported, and economic outcomes of novel ophthalmic interventions with that of standard of care treatment. Assoc Prof Ryan Man is currently a principal investigator on 3 national level grants awarded by Singapore’s National Medical Research Council (NMRC) and has also published widely in other areas of health services and public health research including risk factors associated with the development and progression of eye diseases, randomized controlled trials evaluating novel ocular intervention strategies, as well as the effectiveness of behavioral interventions to improve patient outcomes in Ophthalmology.

name: Manisha Mathur
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Obstetrics & Gynaecology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Manisha Mathur is the Head of Ambulatory Service and Senior Consultant with the Department of Obstetrics & Gynaecology at KKH. She is also the Program Director of the SingHealth OBGYN Residency Programme. Dr Mathur graduated with her Bachelor of Medicine and Surgery and her Master of Surgery (Obstetrics & Gynaecology) from the S.M.S Medical College in India. She subsequently trained in the United Kingdom, advanced herself with Certificate of Completion of Training for Specialist from the General Medical Council, United Kingdom in 2010. She has been a Member of Royal College of Obstetrics & Gynaecology, London, United Kingdom since 2004, obtaining the Fellowship of Royal College of Obstetrics & Gynaecology, London, United Kingdom in 2017. She is also a fellow of the American Congress of Obstetrics & Gynaecology since 2016. Dr Mathur is highly-regarded as a caring and dedicated doctor who is very meticulous in ensuring that all her patients receive the best available care. Her excellent knowledge of clinical practice is up to date with local and international protocols that widely benefits women who seek care and treatment. Her main areas of interest are antenatal and peri-partum care and medical education. In recognition of her service to patients throughout the years, she was awarded the SingHealth Quality Service Gold Award in 2014, the SingHealth Quality Service Star Award in 2015 as well as the SingHealth Quality Service Superstar Finalist Winner in 2017. She was also awarded the prestigious AM.EI Golden Apple Award in 2019. Apart from the clinical commitment, her passion in teaching and educating junior doctors is unparalleled. As the Program Director of the SingHealth OBGYN Residency Programme, Dr Mathur has been patiently training and mentoring OBGYN residents. She is also actively involved in teaching and training of the medical students of Yong Loo Lin, Duke-NUS and Lee Kong Chian Medical schools. She is also active in clinical research with many publications in peer reviewed journals.

name: Manju Chandran
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: I currently direct the Osteoporosis and Bone Metabolism Unit under the Department of Endocrinology at Singapore General Hospital. I relocated to Singapore in 2003 from the USA after completing a 2 year Clinical fellowship in Endocrinology, Diabetes and Metabolism at the University of California, San Diego School of Medicine. Prior to that, I worked for 3 years at Kaiser Permanente Health Systems, Santa Clara California as a Hospitalist for 1 and a half years and in their Outpatient Medical Clinics at Milpitas, California, for 1 year. I completed my Residency in Internal Medicine at Maricopa Medical Centre, Phoenix, Arizona (affiliated with the University of Arizona and Mayo Clinic, Scottsdale) in 1998. My Medical School training was at Medical College, Trivandrum, University of Kerala, India from where I graduated in 1992. Subsequently, I moved to the US to do my higher studies. When I started work at Singapore General Hospital, the field of Bone Metabolism was in its infancy in Singapore. I set up the Osteoporosis and Bone Metabolism Unit at SGH in 2007. From a small one person led clinic then, it has grown into a powerful unit within the Department of Endocrinology with a team of 3 Endocrinologists, 2 trained Nurse Clinicians, Physiotherapists and Dietitians. The unit has been able to contribute significantly to the advancement of the field of Osteoporosis and Bone Metabolism in Singapore in the past several years. To date, we continue to be the only such unit in Singapore devoted exclusively to the field of Osteoporosis and Bone Metabolism. I was involved in setting up the Ministry of Health funded Secondary Fracture Prevention Program (OPTIMAL- Osteoporosis Patient Targeted and Integrated Management for Active Living) in Singapore in 2008. The program specifically at Singapore General Hospital has attracted international attention for its meticulous auditing and capturing of data and is now recognized globally on the Map of Best Practice of the Capture the Fracture Campaign of the International Osteoporosis Foundation (http://capturethefracture.org/map-of-best-practice). Despite being in a full time clinical role and having an extremely busy clinical practice and teaching schedule, I have still managed to invest a significant amount of personal time in Clinical Research in the field of Osteoporosis and Bone Metabolism and have published more than 40 significant and clinical practice changing articles in this area in International Peer Reviewed Journals ( I am the first/senior/second author in 25 of these (3 more such manuscripts are awaiting final acceptance) as well as more than 30 published abstracts (in 27 of them, I am the first/last author). I have 1604 citations credited to my publications, my h-index is 16 and i-10 index is 22. With all humility, I consider this achievement as quite significant given that my primary role is clinical and not research. I have mostly till now devoted my time and energy to non-funded research given the wealth of data that was available for retrospective study in the field of Bone Metabolism and that had not been explored in Singapore, however I have still managed to be directly involved either as a PI or Co-Investigator in local funded trials amounting to close to $100,000. This is in addition to the multi-million dollar trials such as the Veterans Affairs Diabetes Trial, the NAVIGATOR trial etc in which I was one of the investigators. I am on the FRAX ® Position Development Task Force of the International Society of Clinical Densitometry (ISCD) and International Osteoporosis Foundation (IOF) and was also involved in developing the FRAX® model for Singapore. I was elected into the very prestigious Council of Scientific Advisors (CSA) of the International Osteoporosis Foundation (IOF) in 2012. This is an honour given to only physicians who have contributed significantly to the field of Bone Metabolism and I consider myself doubly privileged since, to date I am the only physician from ASEAN to be elected into this august body. I currently serve on the Fracture Working Group, Diabetes and Bone and Rare Bone Disorders Working Groups of the CSA and am actively involved in developing international consensus guidelines on Fragility Fracture Prevention as well as evaluation and management of Bone Loss in Diabetes. My work on Pharmaco- Compliance amongst patients recruited into the Secondary Fracture Prevention Program-OPTIMAL at Singapore General Hospital won the Young Investigator Award at the European Society for Clinical and Economic Aspects of Osteoporosis and Osteoarthritis and International Osteoporosis Foundation Congress in Rome in March 2013. I also served as President of the Endocrine and Metabolic Society of Singapore (EMSS) from 2012-2014. During my term, I was able to organize the largest Clinical Course in Endocrinology in Singapore to date with sequential courses in Osteoporosis and Bone Densitometry, Advanced Endocrinology and Thyroid and Parathyroid Imaging held over a week in March 2014. I also am on the Executive Committees of the Osteoporosis Society of Singapore OSS), Asia Pacific Panel of the International Society of Clinical Densitometry (ISCD) and the Association of Women Doctors Singapore (AWDS). In November 2014, I was invited by the Australia’s National Health and Medical Research Council (NHMRC) to review a case for action paper that is being developed about optimising Older Australians’ musculoskeletal health, primary prevention of falls and fractures, and secondary prevention of fractures. The NHMRC is Australia's peak body for supporting health and medical research; for developing health advice for the Australian community, health professionals and governments; and for providing advice on ethical behaviour in health care and in the conduct of health and medical research. I consider this a singular honour since this invitation is solely based on my work in the field of Osteoporosis and Secondary Fracture Prevention and I am not aware of any other physician from Asia who has been invited to perform this. I have now been appointed in an Ex-Officio Capacity by the International Osteoporosis Foundation (IOF) to be a Board member of their Asia-Pacific Regional Advisory Council (RAC). I was awarded the very prestigious International Osteoporosis Foundation Committee of National Societies (IOF-CNS) medal in March 2015 at the World Congress on Osteoporosis-IOF-ESCEO in Milan, Italy (http://www.iofbonehealth.org/news/iof-committee-national-societies-medal-awarded-dr-manju-chandran). There are 172 National Societies in the International Osteoporosis Foundation, and this award was given in recognition of my work in promoting the cause of Osteoporosis globally and in Singapore. I consider this award as the biggest honour in my career so far. In July 2015, the Capture the Fracture Campaign of the International Osteoporosis Foundation awarded the OPTIMAL program at SGH a Gold Star with a 4.5/5 rating (http://www.capture-the-fracture.org/map-of-best-practice). There are only 2 other hospitals in the world (in the UK) which have a similar Gold 4.5/5 rating. We are the only hospital in the whole of Asia-Pacific with this prestigious rating. I am now regularly invited to speak and to conduct webinars on setting up similar Secondary Fracture Prevention Programs globally. Mine and the work of the Osteoporosis and Bone Metabolism Unit at Singapore General Hospital is now well known to almost all experts in the field of Osteoporosis and Bone Metabolism on both sides of the Atlantic as well as in the Asia Pacific region. This has led to invitations for me to speak at more than 30 (almost all non-pharmaceutical company initiated) regional as well as international Endocrine and Osteoporosis professional society meetings in the last few years. I have just been invited to be on the Editorial Board of the Indian Journal of Endocrinology and Metabolism- the official journal of the Endocrine Society of India and to be on the Reviewer Panel of the Journal of Bone and Mineral Research (the most prestigious Journal in the Bone Metabolism Field). I have also recently been invited to be one of 2 main authors for a review paper on Epidemiology, Pathophysiology and Treatment of Diabetic Bone Disease in NATURE REVIEWS ENDOCRINOLOGY (IMPACT FACTOR IN 2014: 13.28). As can be seen, the major focus of my career in the last few years have been along an entirely self- guided Clinician Researcher Track. However, I am also deeply and passionately interested in teaching and education and though I have not been particularly assigned any educational leadership roles in the Singhealth Institutions, I have without any hesitation accepted the teaching duties assigned to me as a member of the DUKE-NUS and YLL School of Medicine Faculty. In addition I have voluntarily assumed responsibility and have organized in addition to the very large international EMSS-MAYO Course in Advanced Endocrinology, AACE Thyroid and Parathyroid Imaging Course and the IOF-ISCD Bone Densitometry Course mentioned above, multiple workshops and symposia on Osteoporosis for General Practitioners, Medical Officers, Allied Health Care Professionals and the Public in Singapore. As current Chairperson of the Chapter of Endocrinology, I have personally initiated and am overseeing the setting up of a Staff Registrar Scheme for specialized training of foreign trained Medical Officers in Endocrinology in the various public hospitals of Singapore and I am organizing a first ever Parathyroid, Kidney and Bone Seminar in Singapore in May 2016. I have also just been invited ( Invitation sent to me in October 2015) to be Co-Chairperson alongside the world renowned Scientist in Bone Metabolism- Prof Serge Ferrari from University of Geneva, Switzerland for the 6th International Osteoporosis Foundation Asia Pacific Congress on Osteoporosis to be held in Singapore in November 2016.

name: Mara Catherine McAdams
department: Education Advancement Department
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Mara McAdams received her Bachelor’s Degree with Distinction in History of Art from Yale University before attending medical school at New York University. She completed medical residency training in Internal Medicine - Primary Care at NYU, developing her clinical acumen alongside knowledge and skills in medical education. After obtaining board certification in Internal Medicine, she moved to Singapore and joined Duke-NUS as a founding faculty member. Since joining Duke-NUS in 2007, Dr McAdams has held numerous and varied roles. She co-directed the preclinical doctoring course developing frameworks for teaching and evaluating history taking, interpersonal skills and professionalism. She created and managed the school’s standardized patient programme to facilitate training and testing of students’ communication and clinical skills. She blueprinted, developed and administered the school’s high-stakes clinical skills exams managing all aspects of the assessment as well as logistics for multi-day, multi-site examinations. In 2013, she was appointed Assistant Dean, Student Affairs. She worked closely with student leaders to facilitate the development of a greater sense of community and connection within the student body, and to develop a trusting collaboration between the students and the school. In 2016 she expanded her support and development portfolio as Associate Dean, Student Affairs and Alumni Relations. With the alumni leadership, Dr McAdams established the Duke-NUS Medical Alumni’s signature event, the Duke-NUS Dialogues. These oversubscribed discussions are held twice-yearly, connecting alumni and students with prominent clinicians, top scientific researchers and government leaders. Currently, Dr McAdams is Assistant Professor in the Office of Education where she trains medical students’ emotional intelligence, team dynamics and adaptive resilience, champions the incorporation of the medical humanities in the curriculum, and advances coaching services for students, faculty and staff in the academic medical centre. She is passionate about developing people and teams and creating a resilient and supportive organisational culture. Dr McAdams lives in Singapore with her husband and their three children.

name: Marco Aurelio de Anselmo Peres
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Programme
title: Professor
summary: Marco A Peres is a dentist who graduated from the University of Sao Paulo (USP) Faculty of Dentistry, Brazil with a PhD in Epidemiology from USP and University College London. After almost 20 years at the Federal University of Santa Catarina, Brazil, in 2012, he was appointed as a Professor in Population Oral Health and Director of the Australian Research Centre for Population Oral Health (ARCPOH), School of Dentistry, University of Adelaide, Australia. In 2018 he took a position as a Professor of Dental and Oral Health Research, School of Dentistry and Oral Health, Griffith University, Australia. Currently, he is a Research Director and Senior Principal Investigator at the National Dental Research Institute Singapore and Professor and Director of Academic Clinical Programme Oral Health, Health Services and Systems Research Programme, Duke-NUS Medical School, Singapore. His research areas of interest include the effectiveness of oral health-related policies, health services research, big data linkage, oral health surveillance, fluorides, inequalities in oral health, global oral health, life course epidemiology and the relationship between oral health and general health. Currently, Marco is the President of the International Association for Dental Research Global Oral Health Inequalities Research Network and a member of the Lancet Commission on Oral Health. Marco has been cited in a paper published in 2016 in Community Dentistry and Oral Epidemiology (doi: 10.1111/cdoe.12249) as the seventh most productive researcher in Public Health Dentistry in the world in the last half-century. Marco has been cited among the top 2% of scientists in the world in 2020 and 2021 (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.3000918 and https://doi.org/10.17632/btchxktzyw.3). He was the recipient of the 2017 International Association for Dental Research (IADR) Distinguished Scientist Award for Global Oral Health Research. Since 2002 Marco has had continuous funding grants from Brazilian, Australian, and Singaporean agencies totalling over S$ 10 million. Marco has supervised 40 postgraduate students, authored three books, 15 book chapters and more than 270 peer-reviewed papers. His work has received more than 22,000 citations and an H index of 80.

name: Maria Binte Rahmat
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Marielle V Fortier
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: I enjoyed teaching both medical students and radiology residents during my tenure as Assistant Professor at the University of Western Ontario in Canada. In early 2009, I decided to challenge myself by moving to Singapore to experience health care delivery in South East Asia and to expand my experience in research as well as education. Over the last five years, I have grown as a health care professional through the many opportunities I have been afforded. In addition to having an evidence-based practice, I now contribute to the design of educational activities such as multidisciplinary as well as radiology-tailored workshops. Some of my greatest contributions as Head of Department include promoting a truly academic culture, mentoring junior colleagues and developing research infrastructure.

name: Mark Koh Jean Aan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clin Assoc Prof Mark Koh is a dermatologist, paediatric dermatologist and dermatopathologist. He is head and senior consultant of the Department of Dermatology, KK Women’s & Children’s Hospital (KKH), as well as visiting consultant at Singapore General Hospital, Sengkang General Hospital and National Cancer Centre. After completing his specialty training in dermatology in Singapore, A/Prof Koh embarked on a 1-year fellowship in paediatric dermatology and dermatopathology at the Children’s Memorial Hospital and Northwestern Memorial Hospital, Chicago from 2010-2011. In 2016, he embarked on a 2-month fellowship in complex vascular anomalies at Boston Children’s Hospital. During his tenure at KKH, Dr Koh helped to set up several multi-disciplinary clinics (Vascular Anomalies Clinic, Genodermatoses clinic, Chronic Eczema Clinic, Immunodermatology Clinic, Rare Skin Conditions Clinic, Neurofibromatosis Clinic). He also helped to set up the Cutaneous Lymphoma Clinic, Adult Genodermatoses Clinic, and Vascular Anomalies Clinic at SGH, and Skin Cancer Syndrome and Neurofibromatosis Clinic at NCC. His sub-specialty interests in dermatology include atopic dermatitis, genodermatoses, vascular anomalies, histiocytic disorders, cutaneous lymphomas, and skin cancer syndromes. A/Prof. Koh is also involved in teaching of medical students, residents and fellows, and in involved in several research projects and has published widely in many peer-reviewed journals and dermatology textbooks.

name: Martin Linster
department: Signature Research Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Eager to learn about topics at the interface of biology and medicine, Martin studied at the ‘Centre Universitaire, Luxembourg’ and the ‘Universität Münster, Germany’. He enrolled in the Master in Molecular Medicine programme and subsequent PhD studies at the ‘Viroscience department, Erasmus Medical Center, Rotterdam, the Netherlands’ with projects including the evasion of innate immunity by the only recently discovered virus HMPV, as well as mammalian adaptation and airborne transmission of zoonotic influenza A virus subtypes H2N2 and H5N1.

name: Marton Adriana-Sanda
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Adjunct Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Adriana Marton is a Visiting Senior Research Fellow in the Cardiovascular and Metabolic Disorders Programme at Duke-NUS Medical School. She also holds an appointment as a Nephrology Consultant and Principal Investigator at Klinikum Nuremberg, Germany. Before joining Duke-NUS, Dr Marton was a postdoctoral research fellow in Clinical Pharmacology at Vanderbilt University Medical Center, where she developed expertise in translational research, bridging the gap between basic science and clinical applications. Dr Marton's research focuses on understanding the interplay between body water conservation mechanisms and energy metabolism, and how this contributes to the development and progression of chronic disease and metabolic aging. As a clinician scientist, she combines established clinical investigation with innovative methods such as 23NaMRI imaging and metabolomic analysis in order to understand pathophysiological processes and translate these insights into better patient care. She conducts mechanistic randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trials to study the effect of pharmacological intervention (SGLT2 inhibitors, mineralocorticoid receptor modulators) on body fluid homeostasis in patients with heart or kidney disease, and observational studies that characterize the relationship between body fluid, blood pressure, and energy homeostasis in the ageing population during the onset of essential hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus.

name: Mathew Jose Chakaramakkil
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Mathew Chakaramakkil Jose is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Cardiothoracic Surgery at National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS). He is also the Director of the Extracorporeal Membrane Oxygenation (ECMO) Programme, and Cardiothoracic Intensive Care Unit at NHCS. Clin Asst Prof Jose's sub-specialty interest is in cardiac surgery (adult).

name: Matthias Gotthard Maiwald
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Pathology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinical Assoc Prof Matthias Maiwald is a Senior Consultant and the Head of Service, Microbiology, at KK Women's and Children's Hospital and an Adjunct Associate Professor at the Department of Microbiology and Immunology, National University of Singapore and at Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School. He graduated with an MD from the University of Heidelberg, Germany. He previously worked at the University of Heidelberg, Germany, subsequently at Stanford University, USA and subsequently at Flinders University in Adelaide, Australia. He holds a German board certification in medical microbiology, a Fellowship of the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia and is a Diplomate of the American Board of Medical Microbiology. He has three main areas of interest, microbiological aspects relevant to women’s and children’s health, molecular detection and characterization of fastidious and emerging pathogens, and infection control with an emphasis on hand hygiene and skin antisepsis. He has a publication record of more than 120 articles, including journal articles and medical textbooks chapters.

name: Meagan Goh Yijing
department: Centre for Ageing Research & Education
title: Research Assistant
summary: Meagan graduated with a Bachelor of Social Science (B.Soc.Sci) in Sociology from the National University of Singapore. Her first taste in research was as an undergraduate research assistant focusing on children and women’s health in lower-middle income countries before joining the CARE team. Her main responsibilities are with THE SIGNS Study and her personal research interests include health, ageing, gender and social policies.

name: Micah Alan Luftig
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Professor
summary: Dr. Micah Alan Luftig is Professor and Vice Chair in the Department of Molecular Genetics and Microbiology at Duke University School of Medicine, with secondary appointments in Medicine, Cell Biology, and Integrated Immunobiology. He is an internationally recognized leader in viral oncology, focusing on Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) and its role in lymphoid as well as epithelial cancers. Dr. Luftig’s research integrates molecular virology, immunology, genomics, and metabolism, with significant translational impact in biomarker discovery and therapeutic target identification. He has published over 80 scientific articles, is an elected AAAS Fellow, and has received numerous awards for scientific excellence. He is committed to collaborative research and training, with active partnerships in Southeast Asia and a vision to expand virus-associated cancer research and mentorship at Duke-NUS.

name: Michael Lucas James
department: Dean's Office
title: Professor
summary: With training in neuroanesthesia, neurointensive care, and stroke and a special interest in translational and clinical research aspects of intracranial hemorrhage and brain trauma, I have the experience, expertise, and desire to participate in and fully complete my commitments to the proposed work. After completing residencies in neurology and anesthesiology with fellowships in neurocritical care, neuroanesthesia, and vascular neurology, I developed murine models of intracranial hemorrhage and brain trauma in the Multidisciplinary Neuroprotection Laboratories and Brain Injury Translational Research Institutes at Duke University. After model optimization, I began to pursue translatable mechanisms of modifying neuroinflammation after brain hemorrhage and trauma to improve long-term neurological recovery. My collaborators and I have used the models to evaluate the potential of several novel therapeutics for translation into human clinical trials, including apoE-mimetic peptides, gonadal hormones, and microglial modulatory agents. We have now begun to move certain translatable concepts into clinical evaluation. Through my position with Duke Clinical Research Institute and Assistant Vice Chancellor for Duke-NUS Research, my team is leading the effort to translate compounds and devices with high potential for therapeutics and monitoring in acute brain hemorrhage and trauma using multisite and multinationals translational clinical studies. I have also been privileged to be hold leadership positions on steering committees for national brain hemorrhage observational and genomic studies. My involvement has led to a volume of work defining previously unknown clinical aspects of and translatable therapeutics for acute brain hemorrhage and trauma.

name: Michaela Seng Su-Fern (Xin Sufen)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Michelle Chow Tian Nee
department: Lien Centre for Palliative Care
title: Research Assistant
summary: Michelle is a research assistant with Lien Centre for Palliative Care, Office of Research at Duke-NUS Medical School. She obtained her Bachelors in Psychological Science (BSc Hons) from Monash University. Working with Prof Eric Finkelstein and Dr. Soye Shin, she manages several health behavioural interventions aimed at improving physical activity and diet quality in Singapore and in the Kingdom of Bahrain. Since 2024, she has also been working with Dr. Mihir Gandhi on the "Quality of Care for Patients With Advanced Illnesses 2.0 (QCPAI 2.0)” project, which aims to build upon prior QCPAI work by testing the validity and reliability of the QCPAI measure among patients with advanced illnesses in Singapore.

name: Mihir Ananta Gudi
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Pathology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: I have been practising pathology for the past 3 decades and in 3 different countries namely India, UK and currently Singapore. My areas of specialist interest are female genital tract pathology and cytology. I am passionate about incorporating recent advances and testing in pathology within diagnostic pathology reporting to provide patients the best treatment options possible. I have been involved in education of undergraduate and post graduate students both in UK and Singapore since 2000. I am an examiner for the FRCPath 1 and 2 exams organised by the Royal College of Pathologists. Being involved in training committees at both the local and national level in UK as well as at the cluster level in Singapore has given me the opportunity to be able to understand students and trainee needs and aspirations and help them when they are struggling.

name: Mishra Alok
department: Office of Innovation & Entrepreneurship
title: Adjunct Associate Professor
summary: Alok Mishra is a prominent figure in the healthcare and medical technology industry, currently serving as the CEO of Value Addition, where he focuses on helping MedTech companies build capabilities in strategy and marketing. He recently retired as Vice President, Strategic Capabilities, Johnson & Johnson Medtech Asia Pacific, after a 20-year tenure with the company. During his time at Johnson & Johnson, he held various significant positions, including Area Vice President for the South Asia Region, Managing Director for both Singapore and India, and Regional Vice President for Marketing & Business Development in the Asia Pacific region. Alok holds a Master’s Degree in Pharmacy and an MBA from the Indian Institute of Management (IIM) Ahmedabad. He began his career in marketing with multinational companies in India and the Middle East, including Boots Pharmaceuticals (now Abbott) and Burroughs Wellcome (now part of GSK). He is also a member of MENSA, reflecting his high intellectual capabilities. In academia, Alok is an adjunct faculty member at the MBA Program of Singapore Management University and has previously taught at the National University of Singapore (NUS) and Nanyang Technological University (NTU). He is also associated with the Asia School of Business in collaboration with MIT Sloan School of Management and has been a business coach there. Additionally, he has been recognized as the "Most Valued Industry Mentor" by the Singapore Stanford Biodesign Program in 2014 and 2016. Alok has extensive experience in the healthcare industry, covering Pharmaceuticals, Consumer Pharmaceuticals, and Medical Devices. He has served as a Council member of the Singapore Manufacturing Association and chaired the Medical Technology & Life Sciences Industry Group of the Singapore Manufacturing Federation. He has also held leadership roles in various industry committees, including the Medical Technology Standards Committee of the Standards Council and the Medical Technology Group of the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII).Currently, Alok is on the Board of Directors of the Health Science Authority in Singapore, further contributing to the healthcare sector with his vast experience and expertise.

name: Moey Hui Lin Tammy
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Tammy Moey is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Diagnostic & Interventional Imaging at the KK Women's and Children's Hospital and the Department of Diagnostic Radiology at the Singapore General Hospital

name: Mohamad Farid Bin Harunal Rashid
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Mohamad Farid Bin Harunal Rashid is a medical oncologist by training and a member of the pediatrics/ sarcoma/ melanoma workgroup for NCCS Cancer Service Line Development. He was integral in setting up the sarcoma/ GIST clinical database at NCCS. His clinical interests lie in using clinicopathologic correlative studies to enhance understanding of sarcoma biology to guide therapeutic decisions, and exploring novel strategies with established agents to enhance outcomes of systemic therapy in sarcoma.

name: Mohammad Ashik Bin Zainuddin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Mohammad Ashik obtained his basic medical and postgraduate qualifications from the National University of Singapore and the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh in the UK. Dr Ashik is a Senior Consultant in the Department of Orthopaedic Surgery, KK Women's and Children's Hospital. He is also an Adjunct Assistant Professor in Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, DUKE-NUS Medical School and Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine. He was awarded the SingHealth Human Manpower Development Program in 2013, allowing him to pursue a 1-year clinical fellowship in Germany. Dr Ashik is instrumental in advocating minimally invasive surgical approaches (small incisions/keyhole surgery) with the aims of achieving less patient discomfort, shorter hospital stays and faster recovery. Dr Ashik's areas of interests are; 1. Sports injuries in children and adolescents 2. Arthroscopic (keyhole) surgeries of the hip, knee, ankle, shoulder and elbow and wrist 3. Patella dislocation and reconstructive surgery 4. Paediatric trauma 5. Lower limb malalignment and deformity correction 6. Femoroacetabular impingement (FAI) and Hip preservation

name: Mohammad Taufik Bin Mohamed Shah
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Mohamad Taufik is a Senior Consultant in the Department of Radiology, Sengkang General Hospital. His specific areas of interest include musculoskeletal sports imaging and intervention, and emergency and trauma radiology. He also has a strong interest in undergraduate and post-graduate medical education.

name: Mohammed Rizwan Amanullah
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Mohammed Rizwan Amanullah is a Senior Consultant from the Department of Cardiology at National Heart Centre Singapore. He is subspecialised in the field of advanced echocardiography.

name: Mok Chi Wei
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Mok Chi Wei is a Consultant Breast Surgeon at Changi General Hospital (CGH), specializing in both oncologic and reconstructive breast surgery. He is dedicated to advancing minimally invasive techniques, including endoscopic and robotic-assisted procedures, to enhance surgical outcomes for patients with breast cancer and other breast conditions. Dr. Mok graduated with a Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) with Dean’s List honors in 2010 before completing his postgraduate training in General Surgery through the SingHealth Residency Programme. He became a specialist in General Surgery in 2017 after earning his Fellowship with the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh. He then pursued subspecialty training in breast surgery, with a focus on innovative oncoplastic and minimally invasive techniques. Recognizing the importance of both oncological safety and aesthetic outcomes in breast surgery, Dr. Mok has pioneered new surgical techniques, including the 3D Endoscopic Subcutaneous Mastectomy for gynecomastia treatment and the Endoscopic-Assisted ICG (EASI) Technique for sentinel lymph node biopsy in breast cancer. Beyond clinical practice, Dr. Mok is actively involved in medical education, serving as a Clinical Assistant Professor at Duke-NUS Medical School and mentoring students from the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine and Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine. An avid researcher, he has published extensively on breast cancer and surgical innovations, contributing as a peer reviewer for leading journals, including the Journal of Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery (JPRAS). He is also a co-author of the first textbook on minimally invasive (endoscopic and robotic) breast surgery, published by Elsevier in 2020. Dr. Mok is passionate about pushing the boundaries of breast surgery to provide patients with safer, more precise, and cosmetically superior outcomes.

name: Mok Yee Hui
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Mok Yee Hui is a senior consultant pediatric intensivist with the children’s intensive care unit (ICU) at KKH and has interests in pediatric resuscitation, use of simulation in healthcare, narrative medicine and reflective practice in healthcare. She graduated from National University of Singapore in 2000, obtained her Masters in Paediatrics and Child Health in 2005 and completed advanced specialist training in Paediatric Medicine in 2008. As a paediatric intensivist, she understands that therapeutic relationships with families occur when relationships are built on trust, empathy and compassion. Empathy is positively reinforced when patients are viewed as people with lives and stories to tell, not just diseases to treat or problems to solve. This led to her current interest in narrative medicine and reflective practice to augment the humanistic values of our profession. As clinical head of Children’s ICU, she advocates for patient and family-centred care as a core value for the Unit, leading to positive changes in interactions with patients and families. She is currently undertaking a Certificate in Professional Studies in Narrative Medicine from Columbia University and hopes to incorporate elements of narrative medicine into medical education and clinical practice.

name: Mong Rupeng (Huang Rupeng)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Mong Rupeng is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Emergency Medicine at Changi General Hospital (CGH). He completed his medical degree in 2005 at the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore, and obtained his specialist accreditation in emergency medicine in 2014. His area of interest includes clinical toxicology and observation medicine. Dr Mong provides toxicology consultation service. He is also the co-director of the emergency department's Short Stay Unit (SSU). Additionally, Dr Mong is a senior clinical lecturer at the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore, a physician faculty member of the SingHealth Emergency Medicine Residency Programme, and Clinical Instructor (advanced cardiac life support course and hazmat medical life support course) at the SingHealth academy.

name: Muhammad Bin Idu Jion
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Muhammad Bin Idu Jion is a Consultant from the Department of Cardiology with the National Heart Centre Singapore and the Deputy Head at the Department of Cardiology at NHCS@SKH (Sengkang General Hospital). He has experience managing a wide variety of general cardiology conditions, and is trained in Interventional Cardiology procedures as well as Cardiac Nuclear Imaging. Clin Asst Prof Idu graduated from the National University of Singapore in 2003. He completed his Basic Specialist Training in Internal Medicine in 2008 and completed his Advance Specialist Training in Cardiology in 2014. He believes in a patient-centric approach to Cardiac Health and working towards optimizing the health of his patients.

name: Muhammad Raihan Jumat Bin Md TJ
department: Education Advancement Department
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Raihan Jumat is an educationalist and the lead educator for the Master of International Translational Medicine (MITM) program at Duke-NUS. With a background in molecular virology, he has transitioned into education research, focusing on grit, burnout, and collaborative learning. Passionate about fostering innovation in medical education, he designs engaging learning experiences that bridge scientific knowledge with real-world application.

name: Muhammad Syakir Bin Mohamed Tajudin
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Research Assistant

name: Mukesh Kumar S/O Chimanlal Shah
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Murni Tio
department: Signature Research Programme in Neuroscience & Behavioural Disorders
title: Adjunct Assistant Professor

name: Nah Jie Min
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: Jiemin Nah is a post-doctoral research fellow in the Endogenous Peptide Lab (EPL) at the Cardiovascular and Metabolic Disease department in Duke-NUS. During her PhD, Jiemin was trained in the field of RNA biology where she focused on studying ribosome heterogeneity, and the role of specialized ribosomes in translational regulation during erythropoiesis. She joined the Endogenous Peptides Lab in 2021 with an interest to identify and study SEPs involved in mitochondrial translation and cell metabolism.

name: Nah Sek Suan Giselle
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: Laboratory of Hematological Malignancies Prof. ONG Sin Tiong

name: Naik Madhava Janardhan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Naik Madhava Janardhan is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Cardiothoracic Surgery at the National Heart Centre Singapore. He had his basic training in India and his cardiac surgical training from various Hospitals in the United Kingdom (UK) and Singapore. He then proceeded with his advanced Cardiac surgical training in the John Radcliffe Centre, Oxford, UK and the University Hospital of Wales, Cardiff, UK. His sub-specialty interests are in adult cardiac surgery, coronary artery bypass surgery particularly the beating heart or off-pump bypass surgery and valvular heart surgery with special interest in minimally invasive cardiac surgery.

name: Nakao Masakazu
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Nakao Masakazu obtained his medical degree from the Kyoto Prefectural University of Medicine, Japan in 1998. He completed his training in cardiothoracic surgery and also pediatric cardiothoracic surgery in Singapore and was awarded FRCS-CTh in 2011. He completed his fellowship at Great Ormond Street Hospital for Children, London in 2013. He was then appointed as the head of Heart Centre, Al Jalila Children’s Hospital, Dubai in 2018, following his service as a consultant in pediatric cardiothoracic surgery, KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital, Singapore. Currently, he is a Senior Consultant with Cardiothoracic Surgery Service, KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital since January 2020. His research interests are in the areas of congenital heart surgery, tracheal surgery, right ventricular outflow tract reconstruction and regenerative medicine in congenital heart disease. Since 2010, he has been awarded several grants from SingHealth Foundation and National Medical Research Council. His career goal is to establish an international-standard unit for pediatric cardiology and pediatric cardiac surgery in Singapore.

name: Narasimhan Kannan Laksmi
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Prof Narasimhan has been in KK Women's and Children's Hospital from 2008. He has experience in managing surgical problems in children from 1987. He has special interest in posterior urethral valves, stricture urethra, epispadias and exstrophy. Prof Narasimhan also has experience in managing ano-rectal malformations, Hirschsprung’s disease, undescended testis and hypospadias. He has keen interest in bladder and nervous control of the bladder. The evaluation of newborn and post-operative bladder by urodyanmics is an area which he is actively involved.

name: Narayanan Gopalakrishna Iyer
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Professor
summary: Dr Iyer graduated top of his medical school class at NUS in 1998, with Honours. He subsequently went on to complete a PhD in molecular carcinogenesis at the University of Cambridge under Prof Carlos Caldas. He trained as a general surgeon at Singapore General Hospital and then went on to two separate head and neck surgical fellowships at Sydney Head and Neck Cancer Institute at Royal Prince Alfred Hospital, Australia and Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Centre, New York, USA. At the end of his fellowship at MSKCC, he was awarded the Michael Burt award for being the best fellow in the surgical division both in terms of operative skill and research output. Currently, Dr Gopal Iyer works as a head and neck surgeon in National Cancer Centre and Singapore General Hospital. He is also Senior Consultant of the SingHealth Duke-NUS Head and Neck Centre. Dr Gopal has extensive experience in the surgical management of head and neck cancers, as well as surgery for benign diseases in the head and neck (including thyroid, salivary gland and skin lesions). He performs complex resections and reconstructions of cancers involving the oral cavity, oropharynx, larynx, hypopharynx, nasopharynx, paranasal sinuses and neck. He is involved in the robotic and endoscopic head and neck surgical program having performed a number of these procedures locally and mentored the first ever robotic thyroidectomy in Australia in Feb 2012. He is actively involved in research and leads a number of research programs that study various clinical and translational aspects of head and neck cancer prognostication and therapeutics. He is the principal investigator of the Cancer Therapeutics Research Laboratory (National Cancer Centre) where the over-arching theme is the identification of novel biomarkers that guide therapeutic decisions, identification of novel therapeutic targets and modification of conventional therapy for head and neck cancers for precision oncology. This is achieved through several different approaches. Clinical research is achieved through the establishment and enrichment of good clinical databases to answer specific clinical questions and dilemmas in head and neck oncology. Biomarker discovery is focused on established and state-of the art technologies from next-gen sequencing, immunohistochemistry, flow cytometry to single cell genomics. Functional analyses to modify therapeutic paradigms involve the establishment of patient-derived 2D and 3D cell cultures, stem cell models, as well as modelling the immune micro-environment ex vivo. Apart from using these to test the efficacy of existing compounds, they provide valuable insight into novel mechanism of disease that determine the therapeutic response. The latter includes understanding the role of long non-coding RNAs and alternative splicing. These have been published in a number of high impact journals including Nature Medicine, Nature Communication, Oncogene and Genome Medicine. The overall objective is to translate this knowledge into clinical trials and patient specific outcome, even in the era of shifting focus in the therapeutic spheres.

name: Nausheen Edwin Doctor
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Nausheen Doctor is a Senior Consultant Emergency Physician at Sengkang General Hospital. She obtained her medical degree from the National University of Singapore in 2000 and subsequently worked and received her training in Singapore General Hospital's Emergency Department since 2004. She is passionate about maintaining high standards of patient care in the department and hospital and is currently involved in quality assurance and quality improvement processes in both domains. In addition, she also teaches medical officers and residents from various residency programmes, nurses in the department, medical students from the 3 medical schools in Singapore and is an instructor for ACLS, ATLS and other courses. She strives to empower nurses in her department to take on more active clinical roles with enhanced clinical decision-making capacity. Her other area of interest is in Cardiac Arrest Care, both by first responders (lay-persons) as well as EMS/medically trained staff. She was involved in training EMT crews in Dispatcher-assisted CPR as well as High-Performance CPR and has seen the improvement in survival these interventions have made. She hopes to be able to bring HP-CPR to the in-hospital setting as well. She partakes in some EMS research and is a member of the PAROS clinical research network. As Director of Prehospital Emergency Care, and in her capacity in MOH, she oversees the training and professional development of paramedics in Singapore, both in the public and private sector, and works to elevate emergency care provided in the field beyond international benchmarks as well as to enhance the capabilities and profession of paramedicine in Singapore and the region.

name: Neo Hui Shan, Shirlyn
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Shirlyn Neo graduated from the National University of Singapore in 2007 and obtained her MRCP (UK) and MMed (Internal Med) in 2010. She completed her specialist training in Palliative Medicine in National Cancer Centre Singapore (NCCS) in October 2015 and joined NCCS as an Associate Consultant in November 2015. She is currently working as a Senior Consultant at the NCCS in the Division of Supportive and Palliative Care. Alongside clinical work, Dr Shirlyn Neo is also interested in the education of students, doctors, nurses and allied health. She participates as tutor in various courses and symposiums. She is also actively involved in research activities and joined the Khoo Scholars Program in 2021. She has various publications as first, second or last author; as well as grants as Principal Investigator including most recently- a Health Services Research New Investigator Award awarded by the National Medical Research Council in 2021. Her over-arching research interest is that of discovering novel ways of provision of supportive and palliative care- through investigating innovative care models, drugs and technology.

name: Neo Soek Hui Patricia
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Neo graduated from the National University of Singapore in 1997, after which she completed her basic speciality training in Internal Medicine and advanced speciality training in Palliative Medicine in Singapore. She obtained her Masters of Public Health from Lund University, Sweden and subsequently went on HMDP training in Massachusetts General Hospital, US. She previously served as Clinical Director at Assisi Hospice and is currently Senior Consultant/Head at the Division of Supportive and Palliative Care, National Cancer Centre Singapore.

name: Ng Chau Hsien Matthew
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Matthew Ng is the Head of GI Oncology at the National Cancer Centre Singapore and obtained his PhD from the Institute of Cancer Research (London, UK). He is the lead for Upper GI cancers, Director of the Investigational Medicine Unit for early phase trials. His research interest is in developing novel treatments for GI cancers.

name: Ng Chee Chin David
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr David Ng is a Family Physician by training and currently serves as the Chief Executive Director of SingHealth Polyclinics and Academic Chair of SingHealth Duke−NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme. Through that role, he oversees the clinical, educational and research aspects of SHP in his drive towards excellent primary care. Over his 20 years of service in public healthcare and particularly in the past two years as Deputy CEO, Dr Ng has been a stalwart in advancing the development and implementation of organisation and clinical endeavours, and deepening collaborations with community partners and patients to deliver SHP’s primary care and population health mission. In 2017, Dr Ng successfully led in conceptualising, designing, planning and building SHP–Punggol, SingHealth’s largest Polyclinic to date, as a future-proofed facility to meet anticipated patient care needs over the longer-term national healthcare landscape. To provide better care delivery and outcomes, Dr Ng spearheaded the enhancement and integration of SHP data management across domains and systems for enhanced data governance and analytics. A strong believer in cultivating a happy and engaged workforce, Dr Ng chairs the SHP Employee Engagement Taskforce with the aim of establishing SingHealth Polyclinics as the Best Place to Work In. During the current COVID-19 pandemic, Dr Ng, in his capacity as the Deputy Chair of the institution’s Disease Outbreak Taskforce, has directed various multi-disciplinary teams to respond to national and cluster requirements, including external operations at Tampines Dormitory and the Community Care Facility at D’Resort, and supporting the National Vaccination Programme. In his new role as CEO, Dr Ng will build on the foundation laid by Dr Ee to strengthen the value proposition of “A Better SHP” for staff and patients. As Academic Chair, FM ACP, he will provide strategic direction to propel the ACP’s academic medicine agenda to greater heights.

name: Ng Chee Eng David
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: After obtaining my medical degree, I completed post-graduate medical training programmes in Internal Medicine and Nuclear Medicine, becoming a Member in the Royal College of Physicians (MRCP UK) in 1996, a Fellow of the Academy of Medicine in Nuclear Medicine (FAMS) in 1999 and Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians (FRCP Edinburgh) in 2012. In 1999, I completed an overseas Fellowship in Nuclear Medicine and Clinical PET Imaging at the Clinical PET Centre, Guy’s, St Thomas’ and Kings’ Hospital, University of London and in the Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania, Philadelphia, USA. My educational background also included a BSc in Mathematics and a MSc in Biomedical Engineering, with a thesis on renal haemodynamics. I am currently Head and Senior Consultant of the Department of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging, Singapore General Hospital, a Clinical Senior Lecturer with the National University of Singapore Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, a Clinical Associate Professor at the Duke-NUS Medical School and Vice Chair (Faculty Affairs) of Singhealth DUKE-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Program (ACP). I led and served the Department of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging, SGH as Head of Department since 2014 and helped to develop it as a regional centre of excellence for Nuclear Medicine and Theranostics. During my headship, I intergrated Nuclear Medicine Department into, and also assisted in the conceptualization and development of, the Singhealth-DUKE NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Program (ACP) and also a new Division of Radiological Sciences SGH. As part of my work, I helped to co-develop and implement a new system for cluster-wide based PETCT service across the Singhealth cluster as a Radiological Shared Service. I assisted to design, develop and implement new clinical services for Nuclear Medicine in Seng Kang Hospital and for the new National Cancer Centre Singapore. I was immediate past President of the Nuclear Medicine Society (Singapore) (2010-2020) and past Chair of the Chapter of Nuclear Medicine in the College of Radiologists, Singapore. I also chair 2 Ministry of Health Committees relating to health-care policies pertaining to Nuclear Medicine and Radiation Issues – overseeing Nuclear Medicine Specialty training and development (as Chairman of the Nuclear Medicine Residency Advisory Committee (RAC) under the Specialist Accreditation Board) and as Chairman of the MOH Advisory Committee on Nuclear Medicine, co-developed Nuclear Medicine Safety Standards under the new HCSA in conjunction and consultation with the Regulatory Compliance & Enforcement Division MOH. I also contributed as a member of the ACUITY Guidelines Committee to the development of new guidelines for the appropriate use of radiological imaging for various conditions such as low back pain, headache and chest Xray in asymptomatic individuals. I also had the privilege of contributing as a member of the National Environment Agency Advisory Committee for Radiation Safety under the Radiation Protection and Nuclear Science Division. In conjunction with Agency for Care Effectiveness (ACE) MOH, I was involved in developing and implementation of PETCT subsidy indications. My field of sub-expertise is in the treatment of thyroid cancers, radioimmunotherapy, SIRT and other radionuclide therapy, as well as PET/CT imaging, molecular diagnostic techniques and physiological mathematical modelling and collaborated with various other researchers and clinicians, with over 106 papers and 10 book chapters, including in the Journal of Nuclear Medicine, International Journal of Radiation Oncology Physics, Clinical Nuclear Medicine. I have given talks and lectures at regional and international conferences on my areas of specialty, particularly in nuclear theranostics and partition modelling of Y90 microspheres therapy.

name: Ng Chin Teck
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: A/Prof Ng Chin Teck is Head & Senior Consultant in the Department of Rheumatology and Immunology, Singapore General Hospital (SGH). He obtained his basic medical degree with honours from Trinity College Dublin in 2000. After obtaining his MRCP(UK) in 2003, A/Prof Ng entered Rheumatology and General Medicine specialist training under the Royal College of Physicians Ireland, during which he was trained in University Hospital Limerick (University of Limerick), St. Vincent's University Hospital (University College Dublin), Beaumont Hospital (Royal College of Surgeons Ireland) and Tan Tock Seng Hospital in Singapore. Having a subspecialty clinical and research interest in rheumatoid arthritis, A/Prof Ng worked as a clinical research fellow in Rheumatology Translational Research Group of Dublin Academic Medical Centre between 2006 and 2009. His research work on the role of hypoxia in inflammatory arthritis was presented in European and US conferences and was published in top peer-reviewed journals of his field, including Annals of Rheumatic Diseases and Arthritis & Rheumatology. A/Prof Ng joined SGH in 2015 and is a recipient of the NMRC Transitional Award for his research efforts. Besides research, A/Prof Ng also started the department’s Early Arthritis Clinic service. He also took on an active role in teaching the department and at Duke-NUS Medical School.

name: Ng Chirk Jenn
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Prof Ng Chirk Jenn is a Clinical Professor at Duke-NUS and a Senior Consultant at SingHealth Polyclinics, Singapore. His research expertise is in the field of shared decision making, implementation science, digital health and men’s health. He has published 165 ISI-indexed papers in peer-reviewed journals, which received more than 2000 citations. He was the Editor-in-Chief of the Malaysian Family Physician and currently sits in several editorial boards. Before, he joined SingHealth-Duke NUS AMC, he was the Deputy Chair of the University of Malaya Medical Centre Medical Research Ethics Committee and the Deputy Dean of Faculty of Medicine, University of Malaya, Malaysia.

name: Ng Choon Ta (Huang Chunda)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clin Asst Prof Ng Choon Ta is a Senior Consultant from the Department of Cardiology at National Heart Centre Singapore, where he specializes in both general cardiology and several advanced subspecialties, including cardio-oncology, advanced heart failure, cardiomyopathy, and echocardiography. In recognition of his expertise, Clin Asst Prof Ng is fully accredited as a cardiologist by the Ministry of Health Specialist Accreditation Board in Singapore and is also certified by the Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates (ECFMG) in the USA. His pursuit of excellence led him to the Mayo Clinic in Rochester, USA, where he was awarded a Ministry of Health Singapore scholarship to undergo specialized training in cardio-oncology and cardiomyopathy. Clin Asst Prof Ng holds board certification in cardio-oncology from the International Cardio Oncology Society (ICOS) and is accredited in echocardiography by both the American Society of Echocardiography (ASE) and the European Association of Cardiovascular Imaging (EACVI). Beyond his clinical practice, Clin Asst Prof Ng is deeply committed to medical education and the nurturing of future healthcare professionals. He serves as an Adjunct Assistant Professor at Duke-NUS Medical School, Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, and the Alice Lee Centre for Nursing Studies. Clin Asst Prof Ng is also an active researcher with a focus on advancing treatment paradigms in heart failure, cardio-oncology, echocardiography, innovation and cardiomyopathy. He contributes to global clinical trials and is dedicated to pushing the boundaries of medical science through ongoing research.

name: Ng Chung Wai
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Ng Chung Wai, Mark is a Senior Consultant at SingHealth Polyclinics. Besides general practice, he is the Associate Programme Director and Core Faculty for the SingHealth Family Medicine Residency Programme, as well as Clinical Tutor at Duke-NUS Medical School. His areas of interest in Family Medicine include Medical Pedagogy, Infection Prevention, Vaccination, Travel Medicine, and Dermatology. He is the lead of Infection Prevention and Infectious Diseases Workgroup at SingHealth Polyclinics.

name: Ng Foo Cheong
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Ng Foo Cheong is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Urology at Changi General Hospital (CGH). He is the director of the Robotic Surgery Programme in CGH and chairman of the Robotic Surgery Credentialing Committee. He also is a member of the Medical Board, the deputy chairman of the Medical Audit Committee and served as the chief for the Department of Urology till 2019. Additionally, Dr Ng holds academic appointments as adjunct associate professor at the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, Imperial College London and Nanyang Technological University, as well as clinical senior lecturer at the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore. He is a member of the Urology Residency Advisory Committee (Ministry of Health) and a fellow of the Chapter of Urology (College of Surgeons, Academy of Medicine). Dr Ng has had fellowship training in reconstructive urology and subsequently, in minimally invasive urology including robotic surgery. He has participated as principal investigator in several international multi-centre clinical drug trials and various clinical and basic research projects. Currently, his research includes a collaboration with an academic tertiary institution in the area of biomedical engineering. He also has a number of publications in peer-reviewed journals and presentations at conferences.

name: Ng Heng Joo
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Prof Ng received his early medical training at the National University of Singapore and then served in Malaysia before qualifying as an internal medicine specialist. He then joined the Department of Haematology for his advanced specialty training in haematology before pursuing subspecialty training in coagulation, a branch of haematology dealing with blood clotting and bleeding disorders. He is currently the Head of the department. Prof Ng has a special interest in thrombotic and bleeding disorders. His research activities cover clinical aspects of managing these patients and laboratory diagnostics into coagulation disorders.

name: Ng Jing Ting
department: Centre for Ageing Research & Education
title: Research Assistant
summary: Jing Ting is a Research Assistant at the Centre of Ageing Research and Education. She graduated with a Bachelor of Science and fourth-year studies in Psychology at Monash University and James Cook University, Singapore. Jing Ting had previously been a project officer at Nanyang Technological University and Rehabilitation Research Institute of Singapore and has a research background ranging from psychosocial quality of life of first-time stroke patients and their caregivers to mental health promotion through art gallery-based interventions. Her area of interest includes health psychology and gerontology.

name: Ng Kee Chong
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Clin Prof Ng Kee Chong is currently the Chief Executive Officer of Changi General Hospital (CGH) and Senior Consultant with the Children’s Emergency at KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital (KKH). He is concurrently Deputy Group Chairman Medical Board (Disease Outbreak and Preparedness), SingHealth, spearheading strategy development for disease outbreak and pandemic preparedness for the SingHealth cluster. A pioneer in paediatric emergency medicine, Clin Prof Ng is an advocate for clinical education, advancement and patient safety. He previously served as Chairman, Medical Board and Senior Associate Dean at KKH from May 2017 to April 2023. With keen insight on medical leadership and governance, clinical services and infrastructure capacity, Clin Prof Ng chairs and serves in numerous institutional, national and international programmes and committees that advance medical research and education, and enhance patient outcomes.

name: Ng Kok Pin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Neuroscience Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Ng Kok Pin is a Senior Consultant at the Department of Neurology, National Neuroscience Institute. After graduating from the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore in 2006, he obtained his MRCP(UK) in 2011 and was accredited as a Neurologist by the Ministry of Health Singapore in 2015. In 2016, he obtained his Master of Clinical Investigation (NUS). Assoc Prof Ng subspecialises in dementia and his research interest is PET imaging in neurocognitive diseases. He completed his 1-year clinical research fellowship at the McGill University Research Centre for Studies in Aging, Montreal, Canada in 2017.

name: Ng Lay Guat
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Ng Lay Guat graduated from National University of Singapore in 1992 and obtained her post-graduate degree in Surgery FRCS in 1996, and FAMS (Urology) from the Academy of Medicine, Singapore in 2001. She obtained her Female Urology & Adolescent Urology Fellowship after spending one year at the Institute of Urology, London, UK from 2002 to 2003. Dr Ng is the immediate past Head of Department, Senior Consultant and Director of Female Urology & Transplant, Department of Urology, Singapore General Hospital. Dr Ng was the award winner of the Chapter of Surgeon’s Gold medal in 1996 and winner of Yahya Cohen medal in 1996. She has numerous publications in local and international journals as well as actively involved in research. Dr Ng’s clinical and research fields of interest include Female Urology & Incontinence, Reconstructive Urology, Adolescent Urology, and Renal Transplantation.

name: Ng Lok Pui
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Ng Lok Pui is a Family Physician at SingHealth Polyclinics (SHP). She is currently the Director, Regional Clinical Services (East). Prior to her current appointment, she was the Clinic Director, SHP Bedok from 2018 to 2021. She has a keen interest in preventive health. She has led many initiatives for both staff and patients, with the intent to empower them to take charge of their health e.g. SHP staff health screening program, SHP staff vaccination programs, smoking cessation program.

name: Ng Quan Sing (Huang Guanxin)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Ng is a Senior Consultant Medical Oncologist at the National Cancer Centre Singapore, sub-specializing in genitourinary, thoracic, head and neck, and nasopharyngeal cancers. He completed his medical training at Imperial College, University of London, and is a member of the Royal College of Physicians in the United Kingdom. He is a clinician scientist, and is a current recipient of the National Medical Research Council research grant to develop new drugs for the treatment of cancer. He was awarded the Doctor of Medicine degree by University College London for his research into novel vascular targeting anti-cancer drugs. He is also actively involved in research to develop functional imaging as a biomarker in oncology. He was awarded the Cancer Research UK research fellowship as well as the British Oncological Association Young Investigator Award for his work. His research has been widely published in both oncology and radiology journals. He is actively involved in medical education. He is clinical senior lecturer at the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore, as well as research mentor to Duke-NUS Medical School students.

name: Ng Sen Ark Charles
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Obstetrics & Gynaecology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Since 1965 after my return from my medical education in Cambridge, UK, I have devoted my professional life to public service. My professional training as a medical specialist in O&G has been done at KKH, SGH and St Mary’s Hospital in London. I have served in SGH after leaving NUS in 1986 to start the Department of Obstetrics & Gynaecology at the invitation of MOH. Apart from professional services in O&G, I have also discharged my administration duties as Head of Department, Chairman of Division of Surgery, Chairman of Medical Board and Associate Dean. Professionally I have been elected President of O&G Society of Singapore, Chairman of O&G, Academy of Medicine and Chairman of Singapore Representative Committee of Royal College of O&G. I have returned to clinical practice since 2002 in SGH. Apart from clinical service, I have been a teacher for medical students and postgraduate doctors and been involved in research projects especially when I was in NUS. Currently I provide gynaecology service (part-time) at SGH and as Advisor to the Department of O&G.

name: Ng Si Rui
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Ng Si Rui is a Consultant at the Singapore National Eye Centre's Glaucoma Department with a special interest in cataracts and glaucoma. Dr Ng completed his undergraduate medical education at the National University of Singapore and received several awards including books prizes and placing on the Dean's List. He underwent his postgraduate training at SingHealth's Ophthalmology Residency where he was awarded the Outstanding Resident Award as the top trainee throughout most years of his training. During the course of his training, he obtained his Masters of Medicine (Ophthalmology) and became a member of the Royal College of Ophthalmology (London). Dr Ng's main clinical interest include the treatment of cataracts, reduction of spectacles dependence in refractive cataract surgery, treatment of complex cataract conditions such as subluxed or dislocated lenses as well as the medical and surgical treatment of glaucoma.
