He currently practices at the Singapore National Eye Centre's (SNEC) branches at the Outram Campus, Changi General Hospital and Sengkang General Hospital. He is passionate about teaching future ophthalmologists and clinical fellows in clinics and in the operating theatre. He was awarded SingHealth Residency's Outstanding Faculty Award for his contributions in education. Dr Ng has served multiple roles in SingHealth including being the Lead Resident during his Residency and was part of the SingHealth Residency Leadership Program. He was also appointed as the program lead for the medical Transcription Service SNEC, Secretariat of the Forward Planning Commitee at SNEC. Dr Ng is the current Deputy Chief Medical Informatics officer at the Singapore National Eye Centre. He is the Principal Investigator of a few studies and holds several grants for research in clinical innovation.

name: Ng Su Lyn Adeline
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Neuroscience Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Assoc Prof Adeline Ng is a Senior Consultant Neurologist and Director, Special Projects, Office of Academic Affairs at the National Neuroscience Institute (NNI@TTSH). She has a keen interest in cognitive and behavioural neurology, and completed a one-year fellowship at the Memory and Aging Centre, University of California, San Francisco, focusing on Young-Onset Dementias (YODs). Her main interests in clinical research are in neurodegenerative diseases and cognitive neurology, with a focus on genetics and biomarkers.

name: Ng Suah Bwee Agnes
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Agnes Ng is the founding member of the Department of Paediatric Anaesthesia. Her interests lie in education, and she is heavily involved in undergraduate, postgraduate, and simulation based education. She is also the founding member of the Asian Society of Paediatric Anaesthesiologists, formed with the intention to improve networking amongst Asian paediatric anaesthesiologists. She constantly develops equipping workshops for this society, to improve standards of paediatric anaesthesia in Asia. At KK Women's and Children's Hospital, her involvements include promoting Patient Safety in Sedation. She also sits on the board for the Research and Ethics Committee, and the Transplant Ethic Committee. In her free time, Dr Agnes Ng enjoys music, food and is an avid reader.

name: Ng Wai Hoe
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Neuroscience Academic Clinical Programme
title: Professor
summary: Professor Ng Wai Hoe is currently the Group Chief Executive Officer (GCEO) at SingHealth and Senior Consultant with the Department of Neurosurgery at National Neuroscience Institute (NNI). He was previously Deputy Group CEO (Strategy & Planning) of SingHealth, CEO of Changi General Hospital, Medical Director of NNI, Academic Chair of the SingHealth Duke-NUS Neuroscience Academic Clinical Programme as well as Deputy Chairman of the SingHealth Medical Board. Prof Ng is a neurosurgeon with clinical interests in neurosurgical oncology (brain tumours), stereotactic and functional neurosurgery and epilepsy surgery. He was a Fellow of the Royal Australasian College of Surgeons (FRACS) and underwent residency training in Singapore (NNI) and Australia (Royal Melbourne Hospital and Royal Children's Hospital). He underwent subspecialty training at the Royal Melbourne and Royal Children's Hospital in Neuro-Oncology, Toronto Western Hospital in Stereotactic and Functional Neurosurgery and Hospital for Sick Children (Toronto) in Paediatric Neurosurgery. He was also a Sugita Fellow and Visiting Scholar at Nagoya University.

name: Ng Wei Yan (Huang Weiyan)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Ng Wei Yan obtained his Medical Degree from the National University of Singapore in 2010 and completed his Ophthalmology residency training in Singapore. He obtained his post-graduate Ophthalmology qualifications in 2016 and subsequently underwent a further 2 years of advance surgical training. He attained his specialist accreditation in 2020 and is currently pursuing his paediatric ophthalmology subspecialty training at the Singapore National Eye Centre. His research interest includes retinopathy of prematurity, artificial intelligence and blockchain in healthcare. He has published several papers during his training years.

name: Ng Xiang Long, Louis (Huang Xianglong, Louis)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Louis Ng graduated from NUS Medical School in 2005 and obtained his Anaesthesiology Specialty Board accreditation locally in 2014. He was admitted as fellow of Australia and New Zealand College of Anaesthesiology in 2017 and trained at the Intensive Care Unit of The Austin Hospital in Australia from 2016 to 2017. Dr Ng also actively pursues his interest in trauma anaesthesiology and point-of-care device guided blood management clinically and academically. Currently he leads the Operating Theatre Blood Services and coordinates simulation and education in Surgical Intensive Care Unit.

name: Ng Yee Sien
department: Student Affairs Department
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Ng’s sub-specialties and special interests are Spasticity Management including Botulinum Toxin Injections, Traumatic Brain Injury Rehabilitation, Neuro-Rehabilitation, Robotics and Interactive Technology in Rehabilitation.

name: Ng Yeong Huei (Huang Yonghui)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Ng obtained his medical degree from National University of Singapore in 2005. He was the pioneer batch of SingHealth Orthopaedic Surgery Residency Program and completed his specialty training in Orthopaedic Surgery in 2017. He was elected a Fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh in 2017. He underwent further training in spine surgery at Kanazawa University Hospital, Japan after he was awarded the Health Manpower Development Plan award 2019/2020. He has key interests in oncological spine surgery and degenerative spinal conditions. Dr Ng is also involved in teaching medical students and residents.

name: Ng Yong Hong
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: I completed my medical undergraduate education in 1999 and subsequently joined the Department of Paediatrics as a Basic Paediatric Trainee in May 2002. My interest has been in Paediatric Nephrology all along. My current appointment as Head, Nephrology Service has allowed the opportunity to consolidate the service further in terms of service delivery and also forge collaborations for education and research within KKH and with our partners in NUH. My role in education started early during my registrar days subsequently expanded to include that of Training Director for the seamless BST/AST program and then the residency program as Associate Program Director (Senior Residency). I strongly believe that patient care should not center on care of body parts but each individual must be cared for as a whole. Thus, a solid and good foundation in General Paediatrics is vital for individual growth and subsequent development in any trainee in Paediatrics. My current administrative role includes HOD for Department of Paediatrics and Academic Vice-Chair, Paediatrics Academic Clinical Program.

name: Ngeow Yuen Yie Joanne
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Joanne Ngeow, MBBS, MRCP, MPH is Senior Consultant, Division of Medical Oncology at the National Cancer Centre Singapore and Associate Professor, Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, Nanyang Technological University. Dr Ngeow currently heads the Cancer Genetics Service at the National Cancer Centre Singapore with an academic interest in hereditary cancer syndromes and translational clinical cancer genetics. She was awarded consecutive fellowships by the National Medical Research Council and the Ambrose Monell Foundation to complete formal clinical and bench training in Cancer Genomic Medicine at the Genomic Medicine Institute, Cleveland Clinic, Ohio. Dr Ngeow’s research focuses on understanding how a gene-environmental interaction predisposes to cancer initiation and progression. Her team seeks to identify, characterize and understand genes which cause susceptibility to traditional and complex heritable cancer syndromes, to determine their role in sporadic carcinogenesis. The Cancer Genetics Service led by Dr Ngeow is the go-to for clinical cancer genetics in Singapore and the region. Dr Ngeow is funded by the National Medical Research Council, Terry Fox Foundation and Lee Foundation. Her work is published in over 70 peer-reviewed top scientific journals and she is the recipient of consecutive scientific awards from the American Society of Clinical Oncology and Endocrine Society in recognition of her contributions to the field of cancer genomic medicine.

name: Ngo Nye Thane
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Pathology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Ngo read Medicine at the Royal Free and University College London Medical School before completing histopathology training in London, U.K. She was appointed Consultant in Histopathology at Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust in 2011, based at St Mary’s and Charing Cross Campuses. Currently, she is appointed as Senior Consultant in Histopathology at Singapore General Hospital. Her subspecialty interests include Gastrointestinal pathology and Uropathology.

name: Ngu Chi Yong (Wu Qiyong)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr James Ngu Chi Yong graduated from the National University of Singapore in 2003 and was awarded a fellowship by the Royal College of Surgeons (Edinburgh) in 2013. He is currently a senior consultant colorectal surgeon in the Department of Surgery at Changi General Hospital (CGH). Dr Ngu’s subspecialty interest lies in robotic and minimally-invasive surgery, including single-port and natural-orifice surgery for treatment of colorectal cancers and other benign inflammatory conditions that require operation. Dr Ngu participates actively as a clinical instructor and speaker at advanced minimally-invasive colorectal surgery workshops in Singapore, Vietnam, India and Taiwan. He has also been appointed as a founding board member of the Robotic Surgery Society of Singapore and the honorary treasurer of the Singapore Society of Colorectal Surgeons.

name: Nicolas Daniel Marie Foin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Adjunct Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Nicolas Foin is the Founder and Managing Director at Dhenin Management, and an Adjunct Associate Professor at the Duke-NUS Medical School Singapore. Passionate about accelerating new ventures, he brings 15 years of experience in leadership and advisory board roles across healthcare innovation. He has worked with companies including Medtronic, Abbott, Biosensors, Biotronik, and Philips on product, business development strategy and venture investment. He has also advised and supported start-ups in Devices and SaaS in their development and financing. Nic started his career with the Imperial College Cardiology research unit. In 2013, he joined the National Heart Centre Singapore, setting up and leading, as a Principal Investigator of the Medical Technologies theme to translate research and innovation from the bench to the bedside. In 2017, he joined the Image Guided Therapy team at Philips Healthcare, focusing on Market Development Strategy and BD Venture Investment. Then, as Vice President of Business Development and Strategy at Wondr Medical, he led the company's BD Strategy and first equity financing preparation. Nic graduated with a MSc in Engineering in Medicine and a PhD fellowship from Imperial College London. He has authored several patents and contributed to over 150 publications, editorials, and book chapters in the field of cardiovascular interventions and received several awards for his innovation and research contributions. He recently joined Medinbox, a leading remote connectivity solution for hospitals and medical education, as Deputy Managing Director. He serves on the executive board of CoreAalst, a clinical research incubation company, and with Dhenin, his mission is to bring to market and accelerate disruptive value propositions that reduce costs and inefficiencies.

name: Nicole Keong Chwee Har
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Neuroscience Academic Clinical Programme
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Keong is a Malaysian-born, Cambridge-trained academic neurosurgeon with an interest in reversible brain injury. In addition to obtaining a Certificate for the Completion of Training (CCT) in the UK, she was awarded MPhil and MD degrees from the University of Cambridge. For her MD, she received the Raymond Horton-Smith prize (founded 1900) for best thesis. Prior to relocating to Singapore, Dr Keong successfully applied for a Transitional Award Grant from the National Medical Research Council of Singapore to fund a longitudinal MR imaging study, SERENDIPITI, in which cross-correlations between normal pressure hydrocephalus (NPH) and secondary hydrocephalus (post-trauma and intracranial haemorrhage) are being sought. She has research interests in clinical interventions to modify disease outcomes. She was the Principal Investigator of the SILVER trial, a double-blind randomised controlled study, which produced Class I evidence for the reduction of infection using silver-impregnated external ventricular drains. Her focus is on acute-on-chronic reversible brain injury and developing neuroimaging biomarkers using Diffusion Tensor Imaging (DTI) methodology to characterise white matter injury patterns remediable with surgical intervention. She is involved in scoring strategies for risk stratification, early diagnosis and prognostication of mild-to-moderate Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI), as well as translating cognitive neuroscience into learning micro-interventions.

name: Nina Le Bert
department: Signature Research Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Nina Le Bert earned her undergraduate degree in Biology from the Freie Universität Berlin (Germany), with studies abroad at the University of Swansea (UK) and the Université Pierre et Marie Curie (Paris, France). She was awarded a prestigious Marie Curie Early-Stage Research Fellowship, which facilitated her pursuit of a Ph.D. from the Division of Infection & Immunity at University College London (UK) from 2006 to 2011. In 2012, Dr. Le Bert relocated to Singapore, where she joined A/Prof Stephan Gasser’s lab at the National University of Singapore as a Research Fellow. In 2014, she transitioned into Prof Antonio Bertoletti’s lab, initially as a Research Fellow at the Singapore Institute for Clinical Sciences, A*STAR, and later as a Senior Research Fellow at Duke-NUS Medical School from 2017 onward. Since 2023, Dr. Le Bert is an Assistant Professor at Duke-NUS Medical School within the Emerging Infectious Diseases Program.

name: Nishiyama Jun
department: Signature Research Programme in Neuroscience & Behavioural Disorders
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Jun Nishiyama is an Assistant Professor in the Program in Neuroscience and Behavioral Disorders at Duke-NUS Medical School. His research focuses on understanding the molecular regulation of synapses and their disruption in neuropsychiatric disorders, such as schizophrenia, autism, and Alzheimer’s disease. Dr. Nishiyama obtained his MD and PhD from the University of Tokyo. As a physician-scientist trained in psychiatry, he has over ten years of clinical experience. He is qualified as a designated psychiatrist in Japan and served as an assistant professor in the Department of Neuropsychiatry at the University of Tokyo. As a neuroscientist, he has specialized training in neurobiology with specific expertise in the molecular biology of synapses, gained from Keio University, Duke University, and the Max Planck Florida Institute for Neuroscience (MPFI). He pioneered CRISPR/Cas9-mediated in vivo genome editing in the brain (Cell in 2016, Neuron in 2017) and has received prestigious awards, including the Japan Neuroscience Society Young Investigator Award in 2018 and the Singapore National Research Foundation (NRF) Fellowship in 2020.

name: Nongpiur Monisha Esther
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: A/Prof Monisha Nongpiur, MD PhD, is a Clinician Scientist and Principal Investigator at the Singapore National Eye Centre, and Head of the Clinical Research/Trials Research Platform at the Singapore Eye Research Institute. She balances both clinical and research related work in Glaucoma. She currently holds academic appointment at Duke-NUS Medical School, Singapore as Associate Professor, Ophthalmology and Visual Science; and is also a member of the World Glaucoma Association (WGA) Education committee and the WGA Communication and Technology committee. She was a member of the Associate Advisory Board of the World Glaucoma Association for a term of 5 years. Her main clinical research interest is primary angle closure glaucoma with a focus on a better understanding of the clinical course, risk factors/disease mechanisms, genetics, and also to develop risk assessment models for the condition. Her research integrates clinical, imaging and genetics. A/Prof Nongpiur has achieved international recognition achieved within her discipline, and is noted to be within the top 3 researchers worldwide into Angle Closure Glaucoma on expertscape.com (https://expertscape.com/ex/glaucoma, angle-closure).

name: Nur Diana Binte Zakaria
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Oh Choon Chiat
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Oh Choon Chiat is a Senior Consultant with the Dermatology Department, he has keen clinical interests in skin cancers and skin care in transplant recipients.

name: Olivia Wijeweera
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Olivia Wijeweera is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Pediatric Anesthesia, KK Women's and Children's Hospital. She graduated from the Faculty of Medicine, National University of Singapore in 2004 and won the Best Houseman award in 2005. She went on to complete her five year specialty training in Anesthesiology and obtained her Master of Medicine Anaesthesiology in 2009. Dr Wijeweera has been a Pediatric Anesthetist with our department since 2009 and was awarded the Health Manpower Development Programme in 2011. She completed her fellowship in Pediatric Cardiac and Neuroanaesthesia in Birmingham Children's Hospital, United Kingdom in 2012. Being a mother of two girls, Dr Olivia was inspired to do Pediatric Anesthesia because of her desire to work with children. She enjoys helping children through the perioperative process of anesthesia and surgery and seeing them recover well. Her subspecialty interests in the field of Pediatric Anesthesia include pediatric cardiac anesthesia and neuroanaesthesia. She is also an Adjunct Assistant Professor with Duke-NUS Medical School, Clinical Senior Lecturer with Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, and a Clinical Faculty Member of the SingHealth Anesthesia Residency Program.

name: Ong Biauw Chi
department: MD Programme Department
title: Professor

name: Ong Boon Hean
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Ong Boon Hean is a Senior Consultant and Deputy Head (Thoracic) of the Department of Cardiothoracic Surgery and the Director of Thoracic Surgery at the National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS). He graduated from the Faculty of Medicine, National University of Singapore in 2006. He subsequently completed his training in cardiothoracic surgery at the NHCS and was admitted as a Fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons (Edinburgh) in 2014. He was then awarded the Singapore Ministry of Health-SingHealth HMDP Award in 2014 to pursue an advanced clinical fellowship in general thoracic surgery at Brigham and Women’s Hospital, Harvard Medical School in the United States of America. In 2016, Asst Prof Ong returned from fellowship training to practice cardiothoracic surgery with clinical interests in minimally invasive thoracic surgery, thoracic oncology and lung transplantation at the NHCS. Currently, he is the service chief at the Singapore General Hospital campus for the SingHealth Duke-NUS Lung Centre, and a visiting consultant at KK Women's and Children's Hospital and Sengkang General Hospital. Asst Prof Ong presently serves as the vice-chairman of the Chapter of Cardiothoracic Surgeons, Academy of Medicine Singapore. Aside from his clinical work, he is also actively involved in education and research in thoracic surgery. He is a core clinical faculty member of the SingHealth Cardiothoracic Surgery Residency programme, a clinical senior lecturer with the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore and an examiner for the Joint Committee on Specialist Training / Royal College of Surgeons (Edinburgh) Joint Specialty Fellowship Exit Examination in Cardiothoracic Surgery. Asst Prof Ong is also a co-investigator for several studies related to lung cancer screening and novel treatment strategies for lung cancer.

name: Ong Chiou Li
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Ong Chiou Li is a diagnostic radiologist by training. She served as Head of Radiology from 1990-2003 and a second term from 2010-2013. She is currently the Chairperson of Clinical Support Services. She has an interest in women’s imaging with extensive experience in obstetric and gynaecologic ultrasound. She is also experienced in screening mammography having taken part in the Breast Screen Singapore program from 2002-2011. She is actively involved in teaching medical students and radiology residents.

name: Ong Choo Phaik Caroline
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Caroline Ong is a general paediatric surgeon with special interest in Paediatric Urology. After graduating from National University of Singapore, she completed Paediatric Surgery training in Singapore followed by subspecialty training in Paediatric Urology at the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne. Her special interests in Paediatric Urology are congenital abnormalities of the urinary system (CAKUT) and neurogenic bladder. Her other area of interest is medical education where she holds several educational leadership positions in postgraduate surgical training at institutional and national level. Her education interests are in surgical skills training and faculty development.

name: Ong Choon Kiat
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Ong Choon Kiat is the Head of the Division of Cellular and Molecular Research and the Principal Investigator of Lymphoma Translational Research Laboratory, National Cancer Centre Singapore (NCCS). Concurrently, he also holds an appointment as an Associate Professor at the Duke-NUS Medical School. Recently, Dr. Ong is appointed as the Project Director for a National grant at the Fundeni Clinical Institute (FCI), Center of Excellence in Translational Medicine (CEMT), Romania. Dr. Ong obtained his BSc (Hon) in 2000 and PhD in 2006 from the National University of Singapore. During his post-doctoral training, Dr. Ong has led the genomic research in various cancers prevalent in Asia including lymphoma, cholangiocarcinoma and fibroepithelial tumors of the breast. He was also representing Singapore, leading the whole genome sequencing of T and NK-cell lymphoma in the International Cancer Genomic Consortium (ICGC). His research interest is to improve the clinical outcomes of cancer patients through the understanding of cancer genomics and translating the findings into clinical applications. Dr Ong, along with other collaborating scientists in the field of cancer research, was awarded the prestigious “AACR Team Science Award 2018” by the American Association for Cancer Research (AACR), recognising the outstanding interdisciplinary team’s work in furthering the knowledge of Asian prevalent cancers, contributing to the progress of cancer detection, treatment and prevention. He was also recently awarded the Outstanding Researcher Award (SingHealth GCEO Excellence Awards 2024).

name: Ong Eng Hock Marcus
department: Signature Research Programme in Health Services Research & Population Health
title: Professor
summary: Marcus ONG Eng Hock MBBS, FRCS (A&E) EDIN, MPH (VCU) Professor and Director Health Services and Systems Research (Signature Research Program), Duke-NUS Medical School Senior Consultant and Clinician Scientist, Dept of Emergency Medicine Singapore General Hospital Director, Health Services Research Institute, SingHealth-Duke NUS Academic Medical Center Director, Health Services Research Center, SingHealth Services Director, Unit for Pre-hospital Emergency Care, Ministry of Health Senior Consultant, Ministry of Health, Prehospital Emergency Care Vice-Chair, SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme (EM ACP) Prof Ong is a Senior Consultant, Director of Research, and Clinician Scientist, at the Department of Emergency Medicine in Singapore General Hospital. He is also the Director of Health Services Research Center (HSRC), Singapore Health Services; Professor and Director, Health Services and Systems Research (HSSR), Duke-NUS Medical School. Prof Ong also serves as Medical Director, Unit for Prehospital Emergency Care (UPEC) and Senior Consultant, Ministry of Health, Hospital Services Division. He is also Chairman, Pan Asian Resuscitation Outcomes Study (PAROS). Prof Ong has obtained more than S$47 million in research grants for his studies, which include geospatial diseases mapping, clinical drug trials, resuscitation and cardiovascular sciences, pre-hospital emergency care, and biomedical engineering. He is the Principal Investigator for an international, multi-centre cohort study of out-of-hospital cardiac arrest across the Asia-Pacific. This clinical research network has published more than 36 articles in peer-reviewed journals since 2009. In total, Prof Ong has published more than 250 articles in international and local journals, such as Journal of the American Medical Association, Lancet, American Journal of Medicine, Critical Care Medicine, Resuscitation, Annals of Emergency Medicine, and etc. In 2014, Prof Ong started a Data Science team at SingHealth Health Services Research Centre (HSRC) and was appointed Head of Data Science at SingHealth. He is the SingHealth Co-Principal Investigator of the AI Singapore Grand Challenge addressing diabetes, hypertension and hyperlipidemia. He is considered a leading proponent of Data Science in Healthcare in Singapore. He is also a Scientific Advisor for Global Healthcare, a start-up company providing medical cooling solutions and TIIM Healthcare, an AI start-up.

name: Ong Eng Koon (Wang Yongqin)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Ong Eng Koon is a specialist palliative medicine senior consultant at the Division of Supportive and Palliative Care, National Cancer Centre Singapore and Assisi Hospice. Dr Ong has keen interests in medical education, especially in the field of humanities, ethics, and professionalism. He has special interests in the humanities and is Co-lead, SingHealth Duke-NUS Medical Humanities Institute; Director, Office of Medical Humanities, SingHealth Medicine ACP, and Deputy Director, Medical Humanities Education Unit (MHEU), Division of Cancer Education (DCE), National Cancer Centre Singapore. Through medical education, he hopes to nurture professional identity formation for both medical students and postgraduate colleagues, improve clinical communications, achieve education excellence in interprofessional education and collaboration, and foster wellbeing for all staff of the academic medicine institute. Dr Ong is currently Clinical Asst Professor at the Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School and is member of various educational and clinical committees. He completed his Master’s in Medical Education at the University of Dundee, UK in June 2024.

name: Ong Gek Kim Sharon
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: A/Prof Ong is an Anaesthesiologist. Her sub-specialty is in Intensive Care Medicine. She was awarded her Fellowship in Intensive Care Medicine and did her training in the United Kingdom. She obtained her Diploma in Intensive Care Medicine and is now a consultant to the Surgical, Neurosurgical and Burns Intensive Care Unit at the SingaporeGeneralHospital. She also has an interest in the complementary role of Traditional Chinese Medicine in Western Medicine. She has obtained a Diploma in Acupuncture and is a licensed acupuncturist with the Traditional Chinese Medicine Practitioner’s Board.

name: Ong Hock Soo
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr Ong is Senior Consultant & Head of Department for Upper Gastrointestinal & Bariatric Surgery. He is a graduate of the Faculty of Medicine, National University of Singapore and is a Fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh, the Academy of Medicine of Singapore and the American College of Surgeons. His sub-specialty interests are in Upper Gastrointestinal Surgery, Surgical Oncology, Minimally Invasive Surgery, Gastrointestinal Endoscopy, Surgical Nutrition and Trauma.

name: Ong Hon Shing
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Ong Hon Shing is a senior consultant at the Singapore National Eye Centre and the deputy clinical director of the Singapore Eye Bank. He obtained his medical degree from Barts and the London School of Medicine, University of London, United Kingdom. In medical school, he was awarded a total of 16 prizes and awards, the highest number achieved by any individual in his alumnus. He was overall best in his cohort and was proxime accessit to the University of London Gold Medal. He subsequently completed his Ophthalmology Specialist Training at the world-renowned Moorfields Eye Hospital in London, UK. During his specialist training, he continued to receive a number of awards including the prestigious Elizabeth Hunt Medal from The Royal College of Ophthalmologists, UK. He attained his medical postgraduate qualification from The Royal College of Ophthalmologists and a Masters of Science in Cataract and Refractive Surgery from the University of Ulster, UK. He subsequently completed three subspecialised training fellowships in Corneal External Diseases and Refractive Surgery at The Western Eye Hospital London, Moorfields Eye Hospital / The UCL Institute of Ophthalmology London, and the Singapore National Eye Centre / Singapore Eye Research Institute. He is a fellow of the Royal College of Ophthalmologists and the Academy of Medicine Singapore. He is also a member of the Association for Research in Vision and Ophthalmology, the American Academy of Ophthalmology, the International Society of Refractive Surgery, the European Society of Cataract and Refractive Surgery, the Asia Corneal Society, the Asia Dry Eye Society, and the Society of Transplantation Singapore. His clinical areas of expertise include corneal and external diseases (including ocular surface diseases, dry eyes, blepharitis, allergic eye diseases, pterygium surgery, corneal infections), advanced techniques in corneal transplantation, cataract surgery (including femtosecond laser assisted cataract surgery and the use of premium intraocular lenses), and refractive surgery (including femtosecond LASIK and other refractive surgical options). Dr Ong has been conferred a Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in ocular surface inflammation and scarring by University College London (UCL). He has won awards from the Royal College of Ophthalmologists, the Association for Research in Vision and Ophthalmology, the Asia-Pacific Association of Cataract and Refractive Surgeons, and the Asia-Pacific Academy of Ophthalmology for his research interests, which span several different aspects of corneal and external eye diseases, corneal transplantation, and refractive surgery. Through competitive application, he has also been awarded, as principal investigator, research grants from various funding bodies including the National Medical Research Council (NMRC) Singapore. His research work incorporates basic science, and clinical translation. He has numerous first author publications in peer-reviewed international medical journals and he has co-authored in various book chapters in Ophthalmology reference texts. He is regularly invited to present his work at international scientific conferences. Beyond his clinical and research responsibilities, Dr Ong has volunteered in humanitarian missions, providing ophthalmic healthcare to needy individuals in developing countries. Dr Ong is also active in medical education and training. He has been a facilitator in various ophthalmology skill transfer courses and is regularly invited to be a faculty member for instruction courses delivered at international Ophthalmology meetings.

name: Ong Meng Ann Marianne
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Marianne Ong obtained her Certificate & Master of Science in Periodontics from the University of Michigan in 1997 upon completion of a 3-year residency program in the USA. She is a Fellow in the Academy of Medicine, Singapore and a Diplomate of the American Board of Periodontology. Dr Ong has been involved in clinical dental education of the oral health care team (dental assistants, oral health therapists, dental officers, residents, registrars, associate consultants) since 2000 whilst maintaining an active clinical practice in periodontics in National Dental Centre Singapore (NDCS). She attended and completed the SingHealth Duke-NUS Academy of Medicine Education Institute (AMEI) Fellows programme in 2014, the AMEI Education Leadership Programme (AMLead) in 2015 and the NHG College, Partners HealthCare International and Harvard Macy Institute Health Professions Educators’ Essentials (HaPEE) Course (Oct 2016 – Apr 2018) to further develop her teaching skills to be a more effective health professions educator. She held several education leadership appointments between August 2014 to July 2023 as NDCS Director Education, Academic Vice-chair Education SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Programme (ORH ACP), Director Graduate Education SingHealth Duke-NUS ORH ACP, Chair College of Clinical Dentistry (CCD) SingHealth Academy and Vice-chair Graduate Dental Education CCD SingHealth Academy. Dr Ong is presently a Senior Consultant Periodontist in the Department of Restorative Dentistry at NDCS and is Clinical Associate Professor & Programme Associate Director to the Master of Dental Surgery (Periodontology) Residency Training Programme, Faculty of Dentistry, NUS. She has been involved in research with over 30 peer-reviewed publications. She has also contributed to the dental profession through her active involvement in professional bodies in various capacities and in providing continuing education courses both locally and regionally over the years.

name: Ong Ming Liang, Andrew
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Andrew Ong graduated with MBChB (Honours) from the University of Edinburgh and completed his postgraduate degrees in Internal Medicine, MRCP (UK). He then obtained specialist accreditation in the field of Gastroenterology & Hepatology. He completed his fellowship training at Alfred Hospital & Monash University, Melbourne, where he learnt advanced integrated medical, psychological and dietary management of Irritable Bowel Syndrome and other functional gastrointestinal and motility disorders. From this fellowship, he also was trained in intestinal ultrasound and became one of the pioneering gastroenterologists in Singapore to use intestinal ultrasound in the non-invasive evaluation of bowel disorders. His areas of interest include Irritable Bowel Syndrome, Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease, Pelvic Floor Disorders and Gastrointestinal motility disorders such as swallowing disorders. As such, his practice often includes challenging cases of chronic abdominal pain, constipation and bloating. He runs the Gastrointestinal Function Unit (GIFU) in SGH and is trained to perform oesophageal high resolution manometry, pH studies and anorectal manometry studies. He has a particular interest in medical education and has received several teaching awards for his contribution to medical education. He was previously the Associate Programme Director in SingHealth Internal Medicine Residency programme from 2017 to 2019, and is currently the Programme Director of Singhealth Gastroenterology Residency Programme. His other leadership positions include Deputy Director, Faculty Development, Singhealth Medicine Academic Clinical Program and he serves as a part time consultant for faculty development to Ministry of Health, Singapore.

name: Ong Shi Chee
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Research Assistant
summary: I am a recent graduate currently working as a Research Assistant with a strong interest in research and experimentation. I am eager to contribute to advancing knowledge through rigorous investigation and to further develop my skills in scientific research.

name: Ong Sin Tiong
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Professor
summary: I completed my undergraduate and medical training at Cambridge University in the UK, and subsequently trained as a haematologist/oncologist at the University of Chicago, where I also began my laboratory research with Michelle Le Beau. My independent research career started in 2001, when I began my studies in cancer drug resistance at the University of California at Irvine. While our studies focus on chronic myeloid leukemia, the mechanistic implications of our work have been extended and validated in other human cancers, including EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer, and more recently, glioblastoma and breast cancer. In 2007, I joined Duke-NUS to help build the first American-style post-baccalaureate medical school in Asia. Arising from my practice as a haematologist and medical oncologist, my research is focused on the discovery and elucidation of novel mechanisms of resistance to cancer targeted therapies, and thereby the development of therapeutic strategies to overcome clinical drug-resistance. In addition, I have been intimately involved in training the next generation of clinician scientists comprising the MD-PhD students at Duke-NUS. In my role as the Director of the Duke-NUS MD-PhD Programme, the only such dedicated programme in the country, I have established and overseen a unique curriculum that specifically caters to the clinical needs of our students during their PhD years. Our past and current service activities include membership of the Singapore Medical Council, as well as NMRC, NIH and ASH Award review panels. Our research teams have also received international awards and recognition, including career development awards from the American Society of Clinical Oncology, European Society of Hematology John Goldman Award, and election to the American Society of Clinical Investigation.

name: Ong Thun How
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Ong Thun How is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Respiratory & Critical Care Medicine. She did her Health Manpower Development Plan training in sleep medicine at Brown University, Rhode Island. She is also the immediate past Director, Sleep Disorder Unit (SDU), under her lead the SGH Sleep Disorder Unit was the first adult sleep unit in Singapore to achieve international accreditation by The Thoracic Society of Australia and New Zealand. Dr Ong led the unit to a successful re-accreditation and she played a significant role in formalising sleep medicine as part of the residency curriculum.

name: Ong Yee Siang
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: I graduated from Cambridge Medical School, UK in 1996. Having made up my mind that I wanted to work in Singapore, I came back immediately after graduation to complete my housemanship in Singapore. This was followed by Basic Surgical training and Advanced Specialty Training in Plastic Surgery. I did my fellowship in Duke University, Durham specialising in Reconstructive Microsurgery under Dr Scott Levin. I am currently the Head and Senior Consultant in Plastic Surgery and the new Duke- Singhealth Head and Neck Disease Center. I am also serving as a Program Director in SingHealth Plastic Surgery and Surgery- in- General. I have been the coordinator for Plastic Surgery for Musculoskeletal elective and Plastic Surgery elective for Duke students.

name: Ooi Eng Eong
department: Signature Research Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases
title: Professor
summary: Eng Eong trained in medicine at the University of Nottingham and then completed his PhD studies at the Department of Microbiology, National University of Singapore. He is a Professor in the Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases and Associate Dean (Early Research Career Development) in the Office of Academic Medicine, Duke-NUS Medical School. He holds a joint Professorship at the Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health, at the National University of Singapore. He received the Clinician-Scientist (Senior Investigator) Award by the National Medical Research Council of Singapore, in 2010, 2014 and 2019 and the Singapore Translational Research (STaR) Award in 2023. He was one of six to be named Straits Times Asians of the Year and a member of the team that was named Straits Times Singaporean of the Year, both in 2020. He is a member of the Scientific Advisory Board of Science Translational Medicine and an editorial board member of PLoS Biology

name: Ooi Peng Jin London Lucien
department: Admissions & Alumni Affairs Department
title: Professor
summary: Professor Ooi is a senior surgical oncologist with specialisation in hepatobiliary and pancreatic (HPB) cancers. He started as a general surgeon with FRCSEd and FRCSG and exit certification in General Surgery before receiving advanced training in HPB surgery and liver transplantation as Senior Fellow in Australia. He was instrumental in establishing the combined HPB service at SGH and NCCS which now has become the Department of Hepato-pancreato-biliary and Transplant Surgery. He has a Doctorate in Medicine (MD) based on his work on liver cancer (HCC) and is involved extensively in various aspects of liver cancer research and clinical trials. He has trained many HPB surgeons in the region. He was also previously Chair of Surgery SGH, Deputy Director National Cancer Centre, inaugural Chair of Surgery ACP at SingHealth and founding Director SingHealth Transplant as well as being a pioneer at Duke-NUS. He has won numerous awards including the Yahya Cohen Award (1998), PB Desai-UICC Silver Jubillee Oration (2007), Asian Surgical Association Lecture (2012), SingHealth Excellence Awards, Visionary Leader Award (2012), Arthur Li Oration (2014), Honorary Fellowship College of Surgeons of Hong Kong (2014), MOH Long Service Medal (2014), Asian Surgical Association Lecture (2015), National Outstanding Clinician Mentor, MOH National Medical Excellence Awards (2019), MOH Distinguished Senior Clinician Award (2020), Duke-NUS Master Academic Clinician Award (2023).

name: Ooi Yaw Shin
department: Signature Research Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Yaw Shin Ooi, PhD , is an Assistant Professor in the Emerging Infectious Diseases (EID) Programme at Duke-NUS Medical School. He received his PhD from Albert Einstein College of Medicine in New York, under the mentorship of Dr. Margaret Kielian, and completed his postdoctoral training with Dr. Jan Carette at Stanford University. His research focuses on identifying host cellular factors that influence susceptibility to viral infection—specifically, host dependency factors that viruses hijack to enter cells and replicate, as well as restriction factors that constitute the host's innate antiviral defenses. These virus-host interactions determine whether infection proceeds or is halted by the immune response. Currently, his lab investigates the molecular mechanisms underlying infections by medically important RNA viruses, with a particular emphasis on mosquito-borne viruses (including Alphaviruses and Flaviviruses ) and respiratory viruses (such as Paramyxoviruses and Orthoreoviruses ). For more information, please visit the lab website.

name: Oon Lin Ean, Lynette
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Pathology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: A/Prof Lynette Oon is a Senior Consultant clinical microbiologist with a special interest in molecular microbiology. She is currently Head of the Department of Microbiology in the Singapore General Hospital and also holds the position of Clinical Associate Professor at the Duke-NUS Medical School. She was the first Head of the Molecular Laboratory Section in the Department of Microbiology in the Singapore General Hospital from 2006-2025 where she helped to develop its range of molecular tests in microbiology, detection of mutations in somatic cancers as well as pharmacogenomics molecular tests. She was Head of the Department of Molecular Pathology, Singapore General Hospital from 2016-2023 when she led the department to respond to the COVID-19 pandemic and was Academic Vice-Chair (Clinical Quality and Innovation) in the Pathology Academic Clinical Programme, SingHealth Duke-NUS Academic Medical Centre from 2017-2023. In addition to her duties in the field of clinical microbiology, she is actively involved in the field of biological safety. She is the Director of the SingHealth Office of Biosafety and chairs the SingHealth Institutional Biosafety Committee which oversees biological safety in research within SingHealth.

name: Ou Yang Youheng
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Ou Yang Youheng is an Orthopaedic Surgeon with a subspeciality interest in Spinal Surgery, Lower Limb Arthroplasty and Reconstructive Surgery, as well as Trauma and Acute Care. He believes in holistic care for patients suffering from musculoskeletal conditions to promote a better quality of life. He is heavily involved in Undergraduate and Postgraduate Medical Education, Medical Research and Mentoring. He was Chief Resident for the Singhealth Orthopaedic Residency in the years 2018-2019 and was awarded the “Distinguished Chief Resident Award” in 2019. He has won multiple awards in Education including the Inspiring Resident Educator Award (2016) as well the National University of Singapore, Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine (NUS YLLSOM), Junior Doctor Teaching award (2016, 2017). He has published multiple peer-reviewed articles as well as presented extensively at local and international orthopaedic conferences. His research work has attracted competitive grants amounting to just under 1 million SGD. Dr Ou Yang takes a mentoring role in supporting the next generation of future leaders by co-facilitating the SingHealth Residency Leadership Programme.

name: Owen John Llewellyn Rackham
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Adjunct Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Rackham is a world expert in the development of computational approaches for cell reprogramming and disease-gene association. His group works on ways to identify key regulators that can control cell fate in order to find novel routes for cell conversion, as well as identifying drug targets. He has extensive expertise in the analysis of high-throughput sequencing data, biological network analysis and statistics.

name: Pang Hee Nee
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Pang Hee Nee completed his specialist training in May 2012. He went on to subspecialize in hip and knee surgery- primary, complex primary and revision joint replacement surgery by completing a clinical fellowship in London, Ontario, Canada. This is a large volume tertiary centre where he trained under various designer surgeons of widely used implants. Dr Pang is familiar with the various hip approaches in joint replacement, including the direct approach to total hip replacement. He is interested in conducting research and has more than 20 publications currently. He is also actively involved in teaching.

name: Pang Junxiong, Vincent
department: Centre for Outbreak Preparedness
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Pang is an epidemiologist who is passionate about the epidemiology, surveillance and prevention of emerging infectious diseases, in particular, zoonotic diseases. Dr. Pang was previously Deputy Director and then Director for Centre for Infectious Disease Epidemiology and Research in Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health, leading a multi-disciplinary team to strengthen infectious disease surveillance, risk assessment, research and outbreak investigations in military services. Dr. Pang had served as a consultant to the World Health Organization (WHO) MERS-CoV Epidemiology Workgroup in Geneva, Switzerland, and the Ministry of Health (MOH) COVID-19 Epidemiology Workgroup in Singapore. Dr. Pang's public health expertise also involves pandemic preparedness and outbreak management. He had also served as a consultant to the WHO Indonesia, South-East Asia and Western Pacific Offices, the Ministry of Manpower (Singapore) ACE Medical Unit and the Singapore Armed Forces Biodefence Unit on pandemic preparedness and outbreak management. Dr. Pang is a member and focal point of the WHO Global Outbreak and Alert Response Network (GOARN). Dr. Pang was actively involved in One Health research, publication and teaching, collaborating with experts from the other disciplines, to further understand the epidemiology, transmission, and risk factors of hosts, vectors, pathogens, and environment resulting in zoonotic disease transmission. Dr. Pang has taught and guided medical, nursing, and non-medical undergraduates and postgraduates (Master in Public Health) in lectures, tutorial, and research projects in NUS. Dr. Pang is one of the trainers in the Singapore Field Epidemiology Training Programme, an initiative co-led by the National Centre for Infectious Diseases and Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health since the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic, to build up a pool of trained field epidemiologists in the government agencies to support outbreak investigations.

name: Pang Shiu Ming
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Pang Shiu Ming is a senior consultant in the Department of Dermatology in Singapore General Hospital. He graduated from the University of Hong Kong and trained in dermatology in Social Hygiene Service (Sexually Transmitted Diseases and Dermatology) in Hong Kong and St John’s Hospital of Skin Diseases in London, United Kingdom. He was the Director of the Dermatology Unit of Singapore General Hospital from 2005 to 2014 during which the Burns Centre and Dermatology Unit became the national centre for patients with Stevens-Johnson Syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis. In addition to seeing patients in general dermatology, he runs the Immunobullous Diseases Clinic, Generalised Exfoliative Diseases Clinic and Skin Transplant Clinic. The Skin Transplant Clinic caters for the needs of the solid organ recipients in the SGH Transplant Centre who require surveillance and treatment of skin complications from immunosuppression. He is Adjunct Assistant Professor of the Duke NUS Graduate School and Clinical Senior Lecturer of NUS YLL School of Medicine and is a mentor of medical students, medical officers and residents. He was a member of the Dermatology AST committee, member of the Residency Advisory Committee (Dermatology) and Associate Program Director (Dermatology) involved in the planning of the national training program of dermatology and monitoring the progress of dermatology trainees. He has published and co-authored over 40 peer reviewed scientific publications. His research interest includes pharmacogenetics of severe cutaneous adverse drug reactions, HPV and skin cancer in solid organ transplant recipients, bacteremia in generalized exfoliative dermatitis, Stevens-Johnson Syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis and epidemiology of onychomycosis.

name: Pang Yi Kit Philip
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Philip Pang is a Senior Consultant and Deputy Head (Cardiac) at the Department of Cardiothoracic Surgery, National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS). He completed his specialist training in cardiothoracic surgery in 2014 and was a recipient of the SingHealth Health Manpower Development Plan award in 2015. He completed a 1-year Advanced Cardiac Surgery Fellowship at Yale University and Yale-New Haven Hospital, USA, under the mentorship of renowned cardiac surgeons Dr John Elefteriades and Dr Sabet Hashim. He graduated from the National University of Singapore in 2007 and was awarded the SingHealth Best House Officer award in 2008. He obtained his postgraduate qualifications, MRCS (Edin), MMed (Surg) and FRCS (CTh) (Edin) in 2009, 2010 and 2015 respectively. As a researcher, his research interests include clinical outcomes of surgery for valvular heart disease (in particular mitral valve repair), coronary artery bypass surgery, heart failure surgery and extracorporeal life support. In 2013, he was awarded a SingHealth Foundation Startup Grant as principal investigator for the study of “Therapeutic Hypothermia in Adult Patients receiving Extracorporeal Life Support”. As the main or co-author, Clin Asst Prof Philip Pang has published 26 papers in peer-reviewed medical journals.

name: Patrick J Casey
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Professor
summary: Professor Patrick Casey, PhD, joined Singapore’s Duke-NUS Medical School (Duke-NUS) in 2005 as the Senior Vice Dean of Research, serving in this capacity until June 2023. He is now the Senior Advisor at Dean’s Office; and Professor of Cancer & Stem Cell Biology Programme at Duke-NUS, and the James B. Duke Professor of Pharmacology and Cancer Biology at Duke University, USA. Prof Casey received his PhD in Biochemistry from the Brandeis University in 1986 and carried out his postdoctoral work at the University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center in Dallas. Following this, he joined the Duke University Medical Centre as an Assistant Professor of Molecular Cancer Biology and Biochemistry in 1990. He tenured in 1995 and became full professor in 1999, before being named James B. Duke Professor—Duke University’s most distinguished professorship—in 2002. In 2005, Prof Casey relocated to Singapore to spearhead the establishment of Duke-NUS Medical School—a joint venture between Duke University and National University of Singapore—and has continued serving as Senior Vice Dean (Research) of the school since then. A global leader in the field of cell signaling, Prof Casey’s pioneering initiatives and strategic insights were invaluable in establishing the School’s Office of Research, and building a team of world-class scientists across its five Signature Research Programmes. Under his leadership, Duke-NUS has flourished into a research powerhouse, known for its ground-breaking discoveries and innovations. But Prof Casey’s impact extends well beyond the bounds of science, research administration and leadership. He also fundamentally shaped Duke-NUS culture, where people of all stripes work harmoniously to translate their their next big scientific idea into an impactful outcome. Through his illustrious scientific career, Prof Casey has won numerous prestigious awards and accolades, including the Established Investigator Award from the American Heart Association in 1992, the Amgen Award from the American Society of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology in 2000, and the American Association for the Advancement of Science Fellowship in 2012. More recently, he received the Distinguished Faculty Award from the Duke University Medical Alumni Association in 2018 for his instrumental role in establishing the Duke-NUS Medical School and mentoring the doctors, researchers, and educators of tomorrow, and the prestigious National Day Award from the Singapore government in 2020, for his exemplary contributions to shaping the nation’s biomedical landscape.

name: Pavithra Shyamsunder
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: I hold a Ph.D. in Biotechnology from the Indian Institute of Technology Madras (IITM), following which I joined Cancer Science Institute of Singapore (CSI) as a Postdoctoral Research Fellow and focused on the regulatory elements controlling CAAT Enhancer Binding Protein Epsilon (Cebpe). Currently as a postdoctoral research fellow in Prof. Ong Sin Tiong's lab, I study transcriptional circuits and mechanisms that govern tyrosine kinase inhibitor resistance in Chronic Myeloid Leukemia (CML). I use multiomics approaches to combine advanced genomic techniques with innovative therapeutic strategies to advance our understanding of the biology of CML.

name: Pek Jen Heng
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Pek Jen Heng completed his medical undergraduate studies at Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore in 2009. He subsequently completed his training with the SingHealth Emergency Medicine Residency Program in 2016. Dr Pek joined the Department of Emergency Medicine of Sengkang General Hospital in 2016 and led the workgroup in developing the paediatric emergency service. He served as the Chairman of Junior Doctors Well-Being Committee of Sengkang General Hospital from 2020 to 2023, and the Commander for Disaster Site Medical Command under Ministry of Health from 2021 to 2024. Dr Pek has a keen clinical interest in Disaster Medicine, Paediatric Emergency Medicine and Trauma. He is a strong proponent for the use of simulation in medical education and interprofessional learning.

name: Pengiran Hishamuddin P Badaruddin
department: Centre of Regulatory Excellence
title: Adjunct Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Hisham is currently the Founder and CEO of Noviu Health. He is a medical doctor and public health specialist who has over 25 years’ experience in the healthcare industry. He was previously the Director and Lead for Public Healthcare in South-East Asia at Deloitte Consulting. Before joining the private sector, he worked with the Ministry of Health in Singapore and Brunei. He is also an advisor to several digital healthcare companies and has consulted for various companies and organisations in healthcare and life sciences. Dr Hisham studied medicine at the University of Nottingham, UK as a Government of Brunei scholar. Upon graduating from medical school, he worked in emergency medicine in various hospitals in the UK and Brunei, and subsequently specialised in Public Health and Epidemiology. He obtained hisMaster of Public Health from Emory University as a Fulbright Scholar. Dr Hisham is also a Fellow of the Academy of Medicine Singapore and an Adjunct Assistant Professor at Penn State University, USA.

name: Petretto Enrico Giuseppe
department: Centre for Biomedical Data Science
title: Professor
summary: Dr Enrico Petretto obtained his degree in Physical Chemistry in 1998 (University of Sassari, Italy), and then moved into computational data modeling, specializing in Statistical Genetics during his MSc in Molecular Medicine and Genetic Epidemiology awarded in 2000 (The University School for Advanced Studies IUSS Pavia, Italy), and then during his PhD, which was awarded in 2003 (University of Sassari, Italy). He then moved to Imperial College London, Faculty of Medicine, where he worked as Research Fellow. In 2007, Dr Petretto was awarded a 5-years competitive fellowship by Research Councils UK (RCUK), and at the same time he was promoted to Lecturer in Genomic Medicine. In 2009, he obtained a Career Development Award from the MRC London Institute of Medical Sciences (LMS), Imperial College London, and becomes Senior Lecturer in Genomic Medicine at Imperial College London. From 2009 until 2013, Dr Petretto was Member of the Academic Advisory Group for IT & Computing of MRC-LMS, Imperial College, leading and managing the core-Bioinformatics support service and IT & Computing department for the MRC-LMS of Imperial College. In 2012 he was promoted to Tenured Track Group Leader at the MRC London Institute of Medical Sciences of Imperial College. In 2014 Dr Petretto moved to Duke-NUS Medical School in Singapore as Associated Professor with tenure where he leads a research program in Systems-Genetics. From 2020, Dr Petretto is also Theme Leader for Bioinformatics and Systems Genetics at the Institute of Molecular and Translational Cardiology (IMTC) for the study of Brugada Syndrome and Sudden Death, Policlinico San Donato University and Research Hospital in Milan (Italy), and Adjunct Professor at the Institute of Big Data and Artificial Intelligence, China Pharmaceutical University (CPU) in Nanjing (China). From 2021, Dr Petretto took the position of Director of the Centre for Computational Biology (CCB). Enrico Petretto has developed a research programme in Systems Genetics to study the genetic regulation of cellular pathways and gene networks that drive human disease. His lab uses Systems Biology and integrative genomics approaches to identify new drug targets for various complex disease, including cardiometabolic (e.g., cardiomyopathies, Brugada Syndrome) and fibrotic disease, diabetic nephropathy, cancer and important brain disorders such as epilepsy and Alzheimer’s disease. The identified drug targets are then followed up experimentally in Petretto’s lab using several cellular and pre-clinical disease models. Enrico Petretto has published more than 130 papers in top tier journals such as Nature, Nature Genetics, Nature Communications, Nature Neuroscience, Nature Immunology, Cell, Circulation, European Heart Journal, etc., with more than 10,000 citations to date (see Google Scholar). Selected papers include: Integrated genomic approaches implicate osteoglycin (Ogn) in the regulation of left ventricular mass (Nature Genetics 2008); A trans-acting locus regulates an anti-viral expression network and type 1 diabetes risk (Nature 2010); Systems-genetics identifies Sestrin 3 as a regulator of a proconvulsant gene network in human epileptic hippocampus (Nature Communications 2015); Systems genetics identifies a convergent gene network for cognition and neurodevelopmental disease (Nature Neuroscience 2016); A predictive computational framework for direct reprogramming between human cell types (Nature Genetics 2016); IL11 is a critical determinant of cardiovascular fibrosis (Nature 2017); A systems-level framework for drug discovery identifies Csf1R as an anti-epileptic drug target (Nature Communications 2018); Changes in macrophage transcriptome associate with systemic sclerosis and mediate GSDMA contribution to disease risk (Annals of the Rheumatic Diseases 2018); WWP2 regulates pathological cardiac fibrosis by modulating SMAD2 signaling (Nature Communications 2019); A single-cell atlas of entorhinal cortex from individuals with Alzheimer's disease reveals cell-type-specific gene expression regulation (Nature Neuroscience 2019); Whole-genome sequencing of Finnish type 1 diabetic siblings discordant for kidney disease reveals DNA variants associated with diabetic nephropathy (Journal of the American Society of Nephrology 2020); Wnt-regulated lncRNA discovery enhanced by in vivo identification and CRISPRi functional validation (Genome Medicine 2020); Brugada Syndrome Genetics is Associated with Phenotype Severity (European Heart Journal 2020); EpiMogrify models H3K4me3 data to identify signaling molecules that improve cell fate control and maintenance (Cell Systems 2020); Transcriptional signature in microglia associated with Aβ plaque phagocytosis (Nature Communications 2021); WWP2 regulates pro-fibrogenic monocyte infiltration and activity in heart fibrosis (Nature Communications 2022).

name: Phua Ghee Chee
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Phua is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine at the Singapore General Hospital, which is the national tertiary and quaternary referral centre in Singapore. The department provides comprehensive and state-of-the-art clinical care to patients with complex pulmonary and critical-care conditions. He obtained his medical degree from the National University of Singapore in 1997, and completed his postgraduate training at the Singapore General Hospital and Duke University Medical Centre in USA. His key interests are in Intensive Care Medicine, Advanced Lung Disease, Lung Transplantation, Pulmonary Hypertension and Medical Education. He serves in a number of hospital and national committees leading the field of Respiratory Medicine, Intensive Care Medicine and Medical Education.

name: Phua Kong Boo
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Clinical Professor Phua is actively involved in clinical service, research and both undergraduate and postgraduate teaching and examinations. He was officially appointed by NUS as a clinical teacher in 1972. He was the Paediatric ACP Education Vice Chairman from 2011 to 2018. Following which he became the Advisor to Paediatric ACP. He chaired KKH Pharmacy and Therapeutic Committee from 2001 to 2014 and acted as an advisor from 2014. He is now co‐chairing the MAF plus. He is a medical advisor for Club Rainbow. He also serves in the Research and Residential Home (Sunbeam Place) Subcommittees of Singapore Children Society.

name: Pierce Chow Kah Hoe
department: Graduate Studies (PhD & Accredited Programmes) Department
title: Professor

name: Png Meng Ai
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Png Meng Ai is a specialist accredited diagnostic radiologist practicing at the Diagnostic Radiology department at Singapore General Hospital (SGH) since 1987. She is concurrently Clinical Asst. Prof. Duke-NUS GMS. She graduated from the National University of Singapore in 1984 and obtained her post-graduate degree in Radiology in 1991. She was admitted to the Fellowship of the Royal College of Radiologists (London, UK) the same year. She completed a fellowship in Musculoskeletal Radiology at the Nuffield Orthopaedic Centre at Oxford, United Kingdom in 1993. She is currently Senior Consultant Radiologist at Singapore General Hospital with a special interest in Musculoskeletal Radiology, Ultrasound and Radiology IT solutions. From 1995-2002, she was chief of Musculoskeletal section at SGH. She was previously Honorary Consultant to Sports Medicine and Research Centre at Singapore Sports Council, Visiting Consultant at Changi General Hospital and Deputy Head, Diagnostic Radiology SGH. She has been actively involved in information technology projects and implementations at SGH and Singhealth since 1993 and is currently Director, Singhealth RISPACS workgroup. Previously senior lecturer at NTU (2001-2008) and tutor cum lecturer in the National radiology training program since 2000 and also lecturer in the Duke-NUS medical student training program. She is author and co-author of scientific publications mainly in topics with a musculoskeletal and computing-information systems focus.

name: Poon Choy Yoke
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Cl A/Prof Poon graduated from the Faculty of Dentistry, National University of Singapore in 1984. She went on to pursue her Postgraduate Clinical Fellowship at Glan Clwyd Hospital (North Wales) in UK from 1984-1988. In 1993-1994, she was awarded the Health Manpower Development Programme Fellowship in Advanced Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery, Glan Clwyd Hospital in UK. She specialises in Dentoalveolar Surgery and Orthognathic Surgery. Cl A/Prof Poon is currently a Senior Consultant at the Department of Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery, Clinical Associate Professor of the NUS Faculty of Dentistry as well as Adjunct Associate Professor of SingHealth Duke-NUS Oral Health Academic Clinical Program.

name: Poon Shi Hui
department: Signature Research Programme in Neuroscience & Behavioural Disorders
title: Adjunct Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Poon Shi Hui received her undergraduate medical training from the National University of Singapore in 2009 and obtained her Membership of the Royal College of Psychiatrists in 2013. She has been on the Specialist Accreditation Board of Psychiatrists (Singapore) since 2015. She is currently a Consultant Psychiatrist n Mount Elizabeth Hospital. She is also the Medical Director of Neuropsychiatry Associates, and Adjunct Assistant Professor in Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School. Her specialty clinical interests are sleep medicine, neuropsychiatry, complex brain disorders and neurostimulation.

name: Poongkulali D/O Anaikatti
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Poongkulali is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Emergency Medicine at Changi General Hospital (CGH). She completed her fellowship training in pre-hospital care and has a special interest in education. She is involved in teaching medical students and training paramedics in pre-hospital emergency care.

name: Pratibha Keshav Agarwal
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Agarwal is a Senior Consultant in the Department of Child Development at KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital, Singapore. The department offers specialized diagnostic and intervention services for preschool children with developmental or behavioural concerns. Her area of interest is in developmental screening for children and is currently involved in evaluation, implementation and optimisation of developmental screening tools in community health in Singapore. She is also keenly interested and involved in the long term follow up of high risk preterm babies. She was heading the hospital’s “Very Low Birth Weight (VLBW) High Risk Follow Up programme” till Dec 2014 during which she coordinated that programme and maintained a structured longitudinal database over the last decade which has been invaluable in providing data for the department, hospital and Ministry of Health for purposes of research, Quality Improvement and guidelines for care and counselling. She was previously working as senior consultant in the Department of Neonatology and was also the appointed Head of Special Care and Level 1 Nursery since January 2006 till Dec 2014. She is actively involved in perinatal ,VLBW and developmental screening research and has several research projects, presentations and publications in peer-reviewed journals.

name: Prema Raj S/O C Jeyaraj
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinical Assoc Prof Jeyaraj Prema Raj is the Head of Liver Transplant Program in Singapore General Hospital, the Director of SingHealth Transplant and the Deputy Director of the National Transplant Program, Ministry of Health. He started the hepatobiliary service in both Tan Tock Seng and Singapore General Hospital in 1993 And 1995, respectively, the Cadaveric Liver Transplant Program at SGH in 2004 and the Living Donor Program and Liver Transplantation Service at Mount Elizabeth Hospital in 2008 where he was also the Director of Liver Transplantation Programme and Chairman of the Medical Advisory Board. His primary research interests are tolerance induction in liver transplantation, and bioprinting & 3D printing of the liver.

name: Priscilla Phoon Hui Yi (Priscilla Pan Huiyi)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: ​Dr Priscilla Phoon is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Anesthesiology, Singapore General Hospital and a Head and Senior Consultant with the Department of Cardiothoracic Anesthesia, National Heart Centre Singapore.

name: Pronyk Paul Michael
department: Centre for Outbreak Preparedness
title: Professor
summary: Professor Pronyk is the Director of the Duke-NUS Center for Outbreak Preparedness (COP) and the Deputy Director of the SingHealth Duke-NUS Global health Institute. As an infectious disease and public health physician, he has spent much of his career supporting infectious disease control programs in low and middle-income countries in Africa and Asia. He held senior positions at UNICEF Headquarters and UNICEF Indonesia and was the technical lead for the UN Commission on Life Saving Commodities. He led the Ebola response for UNICEF in Sierra Leone, and has directed cross-sectoral research programs for Columbia University and the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine working on HIV, TB, malaria and vaccine-preventable disease across Africa. https://www.linkedin.com/in/ppronyk/

name: Pua Yong Hao
department: Centre for Clinician-Scientist Plus Development
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Pua Yong Hao is a Physiotherapist at the Singapore General Hospital (SGH), Singapore. He has served as a research mentor to several students and allied health professionals. He is an Assistant Professor at Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School, an Associate Editor for the Proceedings of Singapore Healthcare, and Research Lead at the SGH Allied Health Division. His research focuses on using technology and analytics to assess, predict, and improve physical function – particularly in patients with knee osteoarthritis. He has over 130 peer-reviewed publications (H-index, 36) and he is a recipient of the NMRC (i) Transition Award in 2018, (ii) NHIC I2D grant in 2022, and (iii) HPHSR Clinician Scientist Award in 2024.

name: Puar Hai Kiat
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Troy Puar is a Senior Consultant endocrinologist at Changi General Hospital (CGH). Dr Puar received his medical degree from the National University Singapore School of Medicine, and completed his specialist training with the Department of Endocrinology at CGH. Dr Puar received the prestigious HMDP award and completed a year-long fellowship at Radboud University Medical Centre, Nijmegen, the Netherlands. He has an interest in looking after patients with adrenal and resistant hypertension. He runs an Adrenal Subspecialty Clinic, and has also initiated a Resistant Hypertension Clinic in CGH. He is currently a clinician scientist funded by NMRC Singapore.

name: Quah Boon Long
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinical Associate Professor Quah graduated from the National University of Singapore in 1987 and was trained in Ophthalmology at the Singapore General Hospital, Tan Tock Seng Hospital and Singapore National Eye Centre (SNEC). From 1998 - 1999 he was an International Fellow at The Hospital For Sick Children in Toronto, for producing the best research work amongst Ophthalmology Fellows at the University in 1999. In his formative years, Clinical Associate Professor had also won various awards for his research writings including the 1st prize in Institute of Biology National essay writing competition on "Ethics of Bioengineering" (1981) and SNEC Merit Award for Research (1996). Clinical Associate Professor Quah is a Fellow of the Academy of Medicine (Singapore), Fellow of Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh, and a Council Member of Asia-Pacific Strabismus and Paediatric Ophthalmology Society. His Clinical areas of interest includes Paediatric Ophthalmology, Squints (Strabismus), Cataracts in adults and children, and General Ophthalmology. He is actively involved in training of doctors at all levels from medicals students, medica officers to ophthalmology trainees and fellows. Clinical Associate Professor Quah has participated as invited speaker at many local and international meetings. He has published book chapters and numerous research papers. His research interests include strabismus, retinoblastoma and paediatric eye disorders. He derives energy from an active and sporting lifestyle and still indulges in the occasion competitive swimming and running.

name: Quah Hui Min Joanne
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Joanne Quah is a Family Physician by training, Senior Consultant and Assistant Director, Education at SingHealth Polyclinics, as well as the Program Director for the SingHealth Family Medicine Residency Program. She is Adjunct Assistant Professor, and Director for the Health Promotion and Preventive Care Research and Research Talent Development, and a key appointment holder for the SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Program. Dr Quah graduated from the National University of Singapore in 2001 with MBBS, Master of Medicine (Family Medicine) in 2006, Fellow College of Family Physicians Singapore FCFP(S) and Master of Clinical Investigation MCI (NUS) in 2010, and completed a HMDP Fellowship in Integrated Diabetes Care in the United Kingdom in 2016.
