He is trained as a console-surgeon in performing transoral robotic surgery (TORS) using the da Vinci robot and Medrobotic system for obstructive sleep apnea. He and his team have done the most cases of TORS for sleep apnea and have the most experience in Transoral Robotic Surgery for Obstructive Sleep Apnea in Singapore and Southeast Asia to-date. He is often invited to share his experience in this surgical technique in Asia. Dr. Toh has published various scientific articles in notable peer reviewed journals as well as book chapters. He is volume editor of the March 2019 issue of Sleep Medicine Clinics on Screening, Prevention and Treatments of Obstructive Sleep Apnea: Beyond Positive Airway Pressure. He is an invited reviewer for international scientific journals including Sleep Medicine, The Laryngoscope, Archives Otolaryngology-Head and Neck Surgery and International Journal of Pediatric Otorhinolaryngology. He held SingHealth Foundation Grant and Singhealth Academic Clinical Program Seed Grant to explore innovative surgical technique in the treatment of sleep apnea. He is often invited as an expert speaker at local, regional and international scientific meeting and conferences on obstructive sleep apnea and its’ surgical management.

name: Tolwinski, Nicholas Stanislaw
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Associate Professor
summary: Nicholas Tolwinski, PhD is an Associate Professor in the Cancer and Stem Cell Biology Program at Duke-NUS Medical School and Yale-NUS College. Dr. Tolwinski received his B.A. from the University of Colorado in Boulder, majoring in Molecular, Cellular, and Developmental Biology. He received a PhD from Princeton University in Molecular Biology in 2004 under the mentorship of Professor Eric Wieschaus. After completing his PhD, he started his own laboratory when he became the first Frank A. Howard Scholar in the Developmental Biology Program at Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Center. He moved to Yale-NUS College as a founding member of the faculty in 2012 and joined Duke-NUS Medical School in 2022.

name: Tong Khim Leng Anna
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinical Associate Prof Tong Khim Leng is the current director of Echocardiography Service and senior consultant with the Department of Cardiology at Changi General Hospital (CGH). Her subspecialty interests include echocardiography and sports cardiology. She obtained her MBBS degree from the National University of Singapore in 1992, and completed two years of fellowship training 2001 – 2003 at the University of Virginia, USA, with focus in contrast echocardiography. In 2009, she completed her second HMDP in sports cardiology at the Institute of Science and Sports Medicine in Italy, under the Italian National Olympic Committee. She is a member of various national and regional committees and taskforces, and works closely with Singapore Sports & Exercise Medicine Centre @ CGH.

name: Too Chow Wei
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: ​Too Chow Wei graduated from the Faculty of Medicine, National University of Singapore in 2003. He subsequently trained in various hospitals in Singapore in the Diagnostic Radiology training program and attained specialisation accreditation in 2012. He is currently a senior consultant at the Department of Vascular and Interventional Radiology and Director of Interventional Services. He has keen interest in the realm of Interventional Oncology and palliation, with experience in the ablation of liver, lung, kidney and bone tumours. Outside of work, he wishes he has more time to go diving.

name: Tow Lee Choon Sharon
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinical Associate Professor Sharon Tow completed her medical training at the University of Sydney. She underwent basic surgical training in ophthalmology in Edinburgh, Scotland where she attained the FRCSEd and continued her advanced surgical training in ophthalmology at the Singapore National Eye Centre, Singapore. She then embarked on the subspecialty of Neuro-ophthalmology, completing advanced Fellowship training in Neuro-ophthalmology at the Wilmer Eye Institute, Johns Hopkins Hospital, Baltimore in 2001. On her return to SNEC, she helped develop the Neuro-ophthalmology department, and since 2006, has been the head of this busy service. Clinical Associate Professor Tow is actively involved in teaching undergraduate students and trainees in Ophthalmology. She is a regular examiner both for the Singapore College of Ophthalmologists and the Royal College of Surgeons, Edinburgh. Clinical Associate Professor Tow’s research interests include epidemiologic studies of neuro-ophthalmologic conditions in Singapore, optic neuritis, ischaemic optic neuropathy and ocular motility disorders. She has been invited to speak at local, regional and international ophthalmology and neuro-ophthalmology meetings, and continues with this busy schedule. She has contributed to book chapters in the Neuro-Ophthalmology Section of Ocular Manifestation of Systemic Diseases Atlas, and the Neuro-Ophthalmology section of Clinical Ophthalmology – An Asian Perspective.

name: Tran Nguyen Tuan Anh
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Tran completed his undergraduate education at University of California, Los Angeles, United States with a focus in Biochemistry and Mathematics. He completed his medical education in Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School, Singapore, and Radiology training under SingHealth Clinician Scientist track. He is a radiologist in SGH Department of Diagnostic Radiology, subspecialized in Body Imaging. Prior to a career in medicine, he worked at a Neuro-Oncology lab at UCLA, studying genetics, epigenetics and radiogenomics signature of Glioblastoma. During his Radiology residency, he led a study to investigate vascular normalization in Hepatocellular carcinoma using advanced MRI imaging technique and mouse xenograft. He enjoys problem-solving via computer programming and discovering hidden patterns in data. His research interest is in leveraging Artificial Intelligence to improve a radiologist’s efficiency and accuracy.

name: Troeung Prom Lakkhina
department: Centre for Ageing Research & Education
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: Lia joins CARE as a quantitative researcher with experience across academia, industry research and consulting, and research commercialisation. Her expertise is in clinical and cognitive neuroscience, biostatistics, epidemiology, clinical trials, program evaluation, and real-world evidence studies. Before joining CARE, Lia worked as an industry consultant in Australia from 2020-2024 and led multiple large-scale clinical quality improvement projects in the areas of aged care and neurorehabilitation for the government and private sector leveraging national and enterprise-scale real-world data (RWD) to improve patient outcomes and optimise operational efficiency. Prior to this, she held academic positions at the University of New South Wales (UNSW) Medicine, University of Western Australia (UWA) School of Population and Global Health, and University of Notre Dame Australia (UNDA) School of Medicine, where she underwent postdoctoral training in clinical trials, implementation science, and advanced statistical and epidemiological analysis, across the fields of cancer epigenetics, pharmacoepidemiology, and general practice (GP) medicine. She has expertise in the analysis of large-scale RWD including real-time clinical data warehouses, hospital, emergency department, cancer registry, infectious diseases, pharmaceutical claims, medicare claims, and mortality data. Lia has authored 45 publications and been awarded A$6.5m in research funding as Principal Investigator. She is the principal developer of TARB-Ex, an electronic screening tool for identifying risk of familial hypercholesterolaemia using GP EMRs, which was implemented for routine clinical screening across Australia in 2021. She currently serves as a member of the Australian National Health and Medical Research Council (NHMRC) Grant Assessment Committee as an early career expert in public health, neurological and ageing research.

name: Tsai Fung Chen
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Tsai Fung Chen is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Anesthesia & Surgical Intensive Care at Changi General Hospital (CGH). She graduated from University of Malaya (MBBS) and started her internship in Singapore in 2008. She then went on to complete her M. Med (Anesthesia) exams in 2013 and joined Changi General Hospital (CGH) back in 2017. Dr Tsai has an interest in neuro-anesthesia, airway management and regional anesthesia.

name: Tsai Shih Hsiang Andrew
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Andrew Tsai is currently a Senior Consultant Ophthalmologist and Adult and Pediatric Vitreoretinal surgeon at the Singapore National Eye Centre and Clinical Associate Professor at DUKE NUS Medical School. He served a term as lead Resident during his third year of residency. He obtained his Master of Medicine in Ophthalmology in 2014 and qualified as a Fellow of the Royal College of Ophthalmologists in 2015. He completed his training in 2016 and was appointed as Chief Resident in SNEC in 2016/2017. He graduated with a Master of Clinical Investigation in 2019. He completed his subspecialty training in Medical and Surgical Retina in SNEC in 2019. Dr Tsai was awarded the HMDP fellowship in Paediatric Retinal Diseases and Advanced Vitreo-retinal Surgery Techniques, and completed a clinical fellowship in pediatric retina in Illinois Eye and Ear Infirmary, University of Illinois at Chicago, as well as vitreo-retina fellowship in Linkou Chang Gung Memorial Hospital. In terms of committee work, he served as Clinical Director, Pharmacy, SNEC and chair of the SNEC pharmacy and therapeutics committee. He is currently the program lead for Surgical Retina in the fellowship coordination program in SNEC, and Program Director SNEC Fellowships. Dr Tsai has a strong interest in research and published his first paper as an undergraduate. To date, he has published first-author papers in local, regional and international journals, and has presented at various conferences. He has co-authored a book chapter on ophthalmic surgery, an atlas on retinal imaging and he also serves as a peer reviewer for ophthalmic publications. Dr Tsai also received grants from NUS and the SNEC Health Research Endowment Fund. His current research interests are in adult surgical retinal outcomes and pediatric retinal diseases. He aims to provide excellent quality clinical care to adult and pediatric retina patients, conduct impactful research into retinal diseases and continue to contribute to the ophthalmology community and educate the next generation of retina surgeons.

name: Tse Wan Lung Derek
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Family Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Derek Tse is Deputy Chief Executive Officer at Singhealth Polyclinics(SHP). He is a Family Physician by training and has attained the professional grade of Senior Consultant. He also holds the academic appointment of Clinical Assistant Professor at the Duke-NUS Medical School. Prior to his current leadership appointment, he held many key leadership appointments, including the most recent as Director, Regional Clinical Services, East, following the re-organisation of SHP to align the three regions of SingHealth’s Regional Health System in January 2020. Dr Tse was first appointed to the role of Director, Clinical Services, SHP in April 2019 providing oversight for the entire spectrum of clinical services in SHP. Prior to this, he was the assistant Director, Clinical Integration and Clinical Support, where he has oversight in care transitions, dental as well as allied health services. Dr Tse built a Dental Management team to optimize management of dental services and strategize for future development, and provided strategic directions for allied health services to grow in support of clinical services. He strongly believes in the importance of inter-sectorial collaboration in healthcare to smoothen care transition and has anchored an extensive effort to establish referral and discharge criteria between SHP and hospitals. He has also initiated the link-up with social agencies in the community to facilitate health and social care integration, and set up the Community Health Centres in the rollout of the primary care masterplan version 1 to support GPs with nursing and allied health services. His academic profile includes preceptorship for the Continuity Clinic Module of Duke-NUS Family Medicine Clerkship and membership at the Centralized Institutional Review Board. He is currently the Director (Primary Care) in FM ACP since 2017.

name: Tyebally Arif
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Tyebally is a senior consultant working in Paediatric Emergency Medicine. Aside from treating patients with acute illnesses and injuries, he is passionate about post graduate medical education, injury prevention and global health. He is a faculty member in both the Paediatric and Emergency Medicine residency programmes. As a champion of injury prevention, he has worked with many stakeholders like ActiveSG and the Singapore Police Force. He helped prepare several national technical references in water safety and sports safety for the Singapore Standards Council. His interest in global health has given him the opportunity to work in many counties like Indonesia, Myanmar, Philippines, South Africa and Pakistan. Dr Tyebally was awarded the Outstanding Young Persons of Singapore Award 2009 – Merit for his contribution to children.

name: Umapathi N Thirugnanam
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Neuroscience Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Dr Umapathi N Thirugnanam is the Senior Consultant with Department of Neurology at the National Neuroscience Institute (NNI) and Department of Neuro-Ophthalmology Singapore National Eye Centre. He is Adjunct Associate Professor at Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine and Clinical Associate Professor at both Duke-NUS Medical School and NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine. He is visiting consultant at Dover Park Hospice, Singapore. He is also Visiting Professor Yangon University, Myanmar, Honorary Visiting Faculty of Aravind Eye Hospital & Postgraduate Institute of Ophthalmology, Visiting Professor (Neurology and community health) in S.E.A.R.C.H. (an NGO in Maharashtra, India) and Honorary Professor of Training Department of the Viet Nam Neurology Association. He was also awarded Honorary Membership to Association of Sri Lanka Neurologists. He was recently elected member of the Hong Kong Neuro-ophthalmology fraternity and the Pan Arab electrophysiology faculty. He is an active exco member in the International Peripheral Nerve Society, Asia Ocean Myology Congress and AOCN-World Congress of Neurology educational and training committee co-chair. He specializes in Neurology and his sub-specialty interests include Neuromuscular Disorders, Electrophysiology, Eye movements , Autonomic Nervous System and Neuro-immunology. He has been active in research through-out his career, the main focus of which is Guillain Barre syndrome, diabetic neuropathy and neuro infections. Dr Umapathi Thirugnanam’s local and international awards, include 2003 National Day Award-Pinkat Kepujian (SARS “Courage Award”) and 2022 30-year-long service award (Local-SingHealth). He was awarded the SingHealth Distinguished Mentor Award in 2023; and over the last 20 years, a number of teaching awards from NUS, NHG and SingHealth. He has been invited to speak at various international meetings as well as Grand Rounds of prestigious international universities. He has also been actively participating in teaching and service at various hospitals in South East Asia, South Asia, Middle East and Africa over the last 15 years and is recognised for his expertise in Global Health, Neurology.

name: Uthayanan Nilanthi
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Research Assistant

name: Valchanov Kamen Petkov
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: I am currently a Senior Consultant in Anaesthesia in Singapore General Hospital and Clinical Associated Professor for Duke-NUS University. Prior to this I was appointed as a Consultant in Anaesthesia and Intensive Care at Royal Papworth Hospital, Cambridge, UK for 14 years. From 202 to 2022 I worked as a Staff Anesthesiologist in Royal Columbian Hospital, British Columbia, Canada. My postgraduate medical training was undertaken over 10 years in the UK. My undergraduate medical training was in Bulgaria. My main clinical achievements are: 1. I am experienced specialist in Cardiothoracic Anaesthesia, Intensive Care, and ECMO (Extra Corporeal Membrane Oxygenation) support; 2. I was an examiner for the Royal College of Anaesthetists, London; 3. I was a specialist in Spinal Cord Stimulation for Refractory Angina Pectoris; 4. I am editor of 3 books by Cambridge University Press. I have excelled in all my studies and professional achievements: I worked in the largest and most advanced cardiothoracic centre in UK for most of my professional career; I was a Valedictor for my university in Bulgaria and received national academic awards; I was a national champion in Judo in 1987 in Bulgaria. I have dedicated a major part of my working life to academic and educational activities. I was a Senior Clinical Tutor for the University of Cambridge, UK from 2016 to 2020. I have taught many Cambridge students basic and specialised medical knowledge, including physiology and pharmacology, cardiovascular pathology, and management of extreme pathophysiology in intensive care, using tutorials and lectures. I have educated junior doctors in extreme cardiovascular support for heart failure and advanced respiratory failure, including heart transplantation, mechanical circulatory support, lung transplantation and extracorporeal membrane oxygenation. I was a tutor for anaesthesia for 3 years and intensive care medicine for 5 years for the Royal College of Anaesthetists, London. I stopped this work to become Examiner for the College from 2017 to 2020. After my training on a course Teaching the Teacher to Teach I was a faculty of many national and international courses, notably ECMO, Pain Management, and Cardiac Advanced Life Support. I also edited one of the major reference books in the world: The Second Edition of Core Topics in Cardiothoracic Critical Care by Cambridge University Press. My teaching interests started as Demonstrator in Anatomy during my medical school years. My research activities have been multifaceted. My latest interests have revolved around ECMO, and I have 20 publications in this area. I have published in the areas of intensive care and pain management. I have also participated in several international research trials. My research interests started in Cell Biology and mast cell research as a medical student in Bulgaria and research student in King’s College London. I am also an assistant editor of the BMR journal. I believe I can contribute to the education of undergraduate and postgraduate medical students with a wealth of current medical knowledge and stimulate next generations of medics.

name: Valerie Gan Huei Li
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Valerie Gan is a Senior Consultant Urologist at the Department of Urology, Singapore General Hospital (SGH). She is currently Director, Quality & Safety, Programme Director, Pancreas Transplant and Surgical Director, Renal Transplant at SingHealth Duke-NUS Transplant Centre. She is also a Visiting Consultant with National University Centre for Organ Transplantation (NUCOT). She graduated from NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine in 2007 with a MBBS degree and later obtained her MRCS (Edinburgh) in 2009 and MMed (Surgery) in 2014. She trained for her specialization in Urology at SGH and was certified as a Urologist by the Specialist Accrediation Board / Singapore Medical Council in November 2014. Dr Gan was accorded the Health Manpower Development Plan (HMDP) Award and did her fellowship training in kidney and pancreas transplantation at Glickman Urological & Kidney Institute, Cleveland Clinic, Ohio, USA in 2015. She also received training in microsurgical techniques for male subfertility at Center for Male Reproductive Medicine and Microsurgery, Cornell Institute for Reproductive Medicine, New York, USA. Besides transplantation and male subfertility, she specializes in minimally invasive surgery for kidney cancers and other urological diseases. Dr Gan is a gazetted transplant surgeon by the Ministry of Health, Singapore, and serves on its advisory subcommittee on renal transplantation. She has been appointed Deputy Director, National Pancreas Transplant Programme. She is also actively involved in medical education and is the current Programme Director for SingHealth Urology Residency. She holds positions as Clinical Assistant Professor with Duke-NUS Medical School and Adjunct Assistant Professor with NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine.

name: Vasudevan Subhash
department: Signature Research Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases
title: Professor
summary: Professor Vasudevan’s unique research and teaching career since 1989 also includes 5 years in a senior management position in a large Swiss pharmaceutical company. He has built an extensive academic network that serves as a significant professional asset and has published more than 150 peer-reviewed articles that have been cited more > 11000 times, with a H-index of 61. His professional interests include finding better health solutions through bench to bedside research, teaching innovations and public health initiatives to prevent infections. He founded Singapore Advanced Biologics Pte Ltd in 2011 and led Singapore’s first Phase 1b clinical trial to test an antiviral drug in dengue patients in 2013 that has been licensed from NUS.

name: Veluchamy Amutha Barathi
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Veluchamy A. Barathi, is an Assistant Director and Principal Investigator at Singapore Eye Research Institute. Assoc Prof Barathi earned her Doctor of Philosophy from the Department of Ophthalmology, Yong Loo Lin school of Medicine, National University of Singapore. She is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Ophthalmology, National University of Singapore and National University Hospital System and Associate Professor at DUKE-NUS Medical School, Eye ACP in Ophthalmology. She is a Vet & Molecular Biologist with research focused on understanding the pathophysiological mechanism and new therapeutics for myopia, glaucoma, ocular infectious diseases, retinal degenerations, retinal atrophies, retinal angiogenic diseases (diabetic retinopathy and Age-related Macular Degeneration). Her research has led to the translational and pre-clinical testing of ocular diseases using specific animal models. She has published more than 95 peer-reviewed papers; 60 proceedings; 100 conference presentations and obtained more than US$ 20M research funds from National grant funding body and Pharmaceutical Industry. She has vast experience in lab animals and her specialisation encompasses non-human primates, pigs, rabbits, mice, rats, Guinea-pigs and chicken. She is currently collaborating with seven international institutes and six inter-institutions. She regularly peer reviews at least six international journals and also regularly reviews SHSIACUC animal study protocols and SHSIBC study protocols. She currently holds competitive research grants from National Medical Research Council (NMRC) and BioMedical Research Council (BMRC), and Pharma Industry funding. She is a chair of Singhealth Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).

name: Venkataraman Anantharaman
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Regarded as the “Father of Emergency Medicine” in Singapore, Professor Venkataraman Anantharaman is principally responsible for the early initiatives in developing emergency care in the country. He introduced organised clinical reasoning, and systematic and yet judicious use of investigative services, including blood and imaging services into the Emergency Department. He initiated the principles of making more definitive diagnoses for patients seeking emergency care, and having treatments started quickly – standards which have become the norm for all public emergency departments in Singapore. Professor Anantharaman has spent all his working life in public service, serving in many national roles, notably as the MOH’s Field Medical Commander during national emergencies. Above all, he is a passionate humanitarian, leading volunteers on many medical missions to regional countries. Thanks to Professor Anantharaman’s many efforts, Singapore is recognised by the international emergency medicine community as one of the few countries in the world with a first-world emergency medical care system.

name: Victor Samuel Rajadurai
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Prof Victor Samuel Rajadurai is a Senior Consultant at the Department of Neonatology at the KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital, Singapore. He has served as the Head of the Department for 11 years from 1.1.2006 until 31.12.2016. He is an Adjunct Professor in the Duke-NUS Graduate Medical School, Adjunct Associate Professor of Paediatrics, Lee Kong Chian (LKC) School of Medicine and also a visiting Professor of Tianjin Central Hospital and Shijiazhuang, China. He is also a Clinical Physician Faculty Member, Academic Medicine Research Institute (AMRI), SingHealth Residency Program. Prof Rajadurai has extensive experience in Neonatology spanning more than 30 years. His past academic roles include memberships of the Editorial Board, Annals of Academy of Medicine and Joint Committee on Specialist Training (Paediatric Medicine & Neonatology) and President of the College of Paediatrics and Child Health. His current academic responsibilities include Chairman of JCST Neonatology, Director of the Staff Registrar Scheme – Diploma in Neonatology Programme and President of the Perinatal Society of Singapore. He is also a member of the Perinatal Society of Malaysia, Perinatal Society of Australia and New Zealand and the IPOKRaTES Group. Prof Rajadurai has a number of research collaborations locally as well as internationally. His research interests are pulse oximetry of newborn, perinatal asphyxia, inhaled nitric oxide therapy, chronic lung disease of prematurity, neonatal nutrition, newborn screening and newer modes of neonatal ventilation. He was one of the pioneers in establishing the National Expanded Newborn Screening Program for metabolic disorders in Singapore. Prof Rajadurai has been invited to speak at national and international conferences and to conduct seminars / workshops on neonatal care in Malaysia, Indonesia, Bangladesh, India, China, Japan and Myanmar. His publications include 117 articles in journals, 4 chapters in books and 180 abstracts, which were presented in national and international conferences.

name: Vikneswaran Namasivayam
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Vikneswaran is Senior Consultant with the Department of Gastroenterology & Hepatology, Singapore General Hospital. He obtained his medical degree from National University of Singapore in 2000 and his specialist accreditation in Gastroenterology in 2009. He completed an advanced fellowship at Mayo Clinic, Rochester, USA and further attachment at National Cancer Center Hospital, Tokyo, Japan. His main area of training is in endoscopic resection (EMR and ESD) of early cancers and advanced endoscopic imaging techniques (NBI, chromoendoscopy) for early cancer and polyp detection in the esophagus (Barrett’s esophagus), stomach and colon. His training also encompasses gastroesophageal reflux disease and motility disorders (achalasia, functional dyspepsia and irritable bowel syndrome). He performs pH-impedance testing, Bravo capsule insertion and esophageal manometry for the evaluation of reflux and swallowing disorders and endoscopic dilatation of strictures and achalasia. He is actively involved in research in therapeutic endoscopy, reflux and motility disorders and colorectal cancer. He has authored several publications in peer-reviewed international journals and book chapters and has served as reviewers for several GI journals. In addition, he has also served as an invited speaker and faculty at several conferences and endoscopy workshops in the region.

name: Vora Bimal Mayur Kumar
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Vora is a Consultant with the Department of Diagnostic Radiology. He obtained his medical degree from the National University of Singapore in 2011. Dr Vora underwent his specialist training under the SingHealth Radiology Residency Programme in Singapore and obtained his Fellowship of the Royal College of Radiologists in 2017. His current interests are diagnostic and interventional musculoskeletal radiology. He also has an ardent interest in undergraduate and postgraduate education.

name: Wahab Syed Shahul Hameed
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Neuroscience Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Shahul Hameed is a Senior Consultant Neurologist at National Neuroscience Institute, Singapore General Hospital Campus, SingHealth and Clinical Associate Professor at DUKE NUS Medical School. He graduated MBBS from University of Madras, India and attained his MRCP UK while working as medical officer in Malaysia. He developed interest in Neurology while working as Medical Registrar in Fremantle Hospital, Western Australia and to pursue his interest in Neurology he got trained as Neurologist by undergoing speciality training in Singapore and obtained his FAMS(Neurology). He underwent one year Fellowship in Dementia and Neurobehavioural Programme at University of British Columbia, Vancouver, Canada. He subspecializes in Cognitive Neurology and Sleep medicine. He is doing General Neurology clinical work daily in both inpatient and outpatient setting at Singapore General Hospital and also doing Dementia and Sleep Neurology clinics at outpatient setting. He is actively involved in Medical Education both undergraduate and post graduate education in Singapore. He was the Program Director of SingHealth Neurology Senior Residency programme and during that period he was also in charge of crafting Neurology curriculum for the programme. He is also the Member of Ministry of Health Singapore Neurology EPA committee. He also helmed the post of Neurology Clerkship Coordinator at DUKE-NUS Medical School from 2012 to 2017. He is an outstanding clinician and has won multiple awards for his excellent quality service. He is an avid educator and constantly keeps himself updated in the field of medical education, he has obtained Certificate Diploma in Medical Education from University of Dundee, United Kingdom. He does collaborative medical research with SingHealth and DUKE-NUS partners mostly in Cognitive Neurology and sleep medicine.

name: Wang Hongyan
department: Signature Research Programme in Neuroscience & Behavioural Disorders
title: Professor
summary: Dr Hongyan Wang is currently Professor and Acting Director of the Neuroscience & Behavioral Disorders (NBD) Program at Duke-NUS Medical School, Singapore. She received her PhD in 2004 under the guidance of Prof. Mohan Balasubramanian in the National University of Singapore and joined Prof. William Chia’s laboratory in Temasek Life Sciences Laboratory as a postdoctoral Research Fellow, where she focused on the asymmetric division of Drosophila neural stem cells from 2004 to 2007. Dr. Wang joined Duke-NUS Medical School as an Assistant Professor in October 2007 and was promoted to Professor with tenure in January 2020. Her laboratory uses both Drosophila and mammalian systems to investigate the mechanisms of brain development and the underlying causes of neurodevelopmental disorders. She received the Singapore National Academy of Sciences and A*STAR Young Scientist Award in the year 2008 and a National Research Foundation (NRF) fellowship in 2009. Dr. Wang is an elected EMBO Associate Member in 2020, a life-long honor in recognition of her research excellence and outstanding accomplishments.

name: Wang Jenn Chyuan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Wang Jenn Chyuan graduated from the Queen’s University, Belfast in 1996, winning several awards and scholarships in the process which included the coveted Sir John Henry Biggart prize for Ophthalmology. He underwent further specialization in the field of Cornea, Cataract and Refractive Surgery with a fellowship in The Eye Institute in 2005. This was followed by a highly sort-after anterior segment fellowship in the prestigious Moorfield's Eye Hospital, London in 2006. Dr Wang was Head of Corneal Department in National University Hospital in 2009. In 2010, he joined SNEC to further advance his career. Dr Wang has numerous publications in internationally renowned books and journals. He also co-authored a book chapter on modern glaucoma tube implants in Asia. He has refereed papers for international and local peer-reviewed journals. He has led teaching teams to Yangon Eye Hospital in Myanmar and performed charity cataract surgery in Phnom Penh and shared experiences with premium lenses in Vietnam. He has been regularly teaching Phacodynamics in the Basic Phacoemulsification Course and Presbyopia treatment in the Lasik Accreditation Course. His current interest is in cataract surgery and implantation with premium lenses like extended range of vision, multifocal and astigmatism correcting lenses.

name: Wang Lian Chek Michael
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Wang Linfa
department: Signature Research Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases
title: Professor
summary: Prof Wang is a Professor in the Programme in Emerging Infectious Diseases at Duke-NUS Medical School and the inaugural Executive Director of the Programme for Research in Epidemic Preparedness and Response (PREPARE), Singapore. He is one of the world’s leading experts in zoonotic diseases, bat immunology and pathogen discovery. Prof Wang holds a PhD degree from the University of California, Davis USA and a Bachelor's degree from the East China Normal University, Shanghai China. Prof Wang is a member of multiple World Health Organization committees on COVID-19. He has more than 500 scientific papers including papers in Science, Nature, New England Journal of Medicine, Lancet, along with nine patents and many invited book chapters. He holds a number of honorary positions and memberships and has received numerous awards. Prof Wang was elected to the Australian Academy of Technological Sciences and Engineering in 2010, the American Academy of Microbiology in 2021 and Australian Academy of Sciences in 2023 in recognition of his achievement in new and emerging diseases. He received the Singapore’s President Science Award in 2021.

name: Wang Mei
department: Signature Research Programme in Cancer & Stem Cell Biology
title: Associate Professor
summary: I received my PhD degree in Biochemistry and Molecular Biology from University of Miami, followed by training as a Howard Hughes Research Fellow in Professor Robert Lefkowitz’s lab at Duke to study G-protein coupled receptors (GPCRs) and its intracellular signaling. I received my MD degree and residency training at University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill (UNC) and UNC Hospital. My research laboratory at Duke-NUS focuses on cancer cell signaling and drug development with a molecular focus on protein prenylation.

name: Wang Meng
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Research Fellow
summary: I was born on November 11, 1992, in Shanxi Province, China. My passion for healthcare drives my dedication to making a positive impact in the field of medicine. My journey began with a strong interest in cardiovascular diseases, and I am eager to learn and grow in this dynamic environment. Currently, I am researching the role of branched-chain amino acids (BCAAs)—specifically leucine, isoleucine, and valine—in relation to cardiovascular health. My work focuses on how these essential amino acids influence insulin sensitivity and overall cardiovascular function. Through this research, I aim to uncover the potential benefits and risks of BCAA supplementation in individuals with cardiovascular conditions. By examining the underlying biochemical pathways, I hope to contribute valuable insights that can inform clinical practices and dietary recommendations for enhancing heart health.

name: Wang Shuang
department: Centre for Biomedical Data Science
title: Research Fellow
summary: • 20 years of clinical treatment and research experience in Tianjin Medical University, China. Good at diagnosis and treatment of oral disease, and clinic basic research according to clinical symptoms. • 4 years of scientific research training for Ph.D. in Nihon University, Japan. Research protein gene transcription and signal pathway. Proficient in the experimental skills of molecular biology such as cell culture, real-time PCR, gel shift assay, Northern hybridization, Western blot assay, Histology and Animal experiments. • 3 years of the research fellow for Dental Pulp Regeneration in Dentistry of NUS. Research the novel dental pulp matrix hydrogel scaffold for dental pulp regeneration and exosome regulate regeneration of dental pulp and calvarial defect. Master the skills of molecular, cell and tissue biology in vitro and in vivo animal experiment. • Good team player with good interpersonal skills and good work ethics.

name: Wang Xiaomeng
department: Centre for Vision Research
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Xiaomeng Wang is an Associate Professor at the Centre for Vison Research, Duke-NUS Medical School, Director of Laboratory Translational Research, Deputy Director of Education, and Head of Ocular Therapeutics and Drug delivery at Singapore Eye Research Institute, Senior Principal Investigator at IMCB, A*STAR. Prior to this appointment, Dr. Wang worked as an Associate Professor at the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, Nanyang Technological University Singapore. Dr. Wang obtained her BSc. in Biology at Beijing Normal University, China and her PhD in Genetics at Newcastle University, UK. Thereafter, she undertook post-doctoral training at Manchester University and University College London, UK. Dr. Wang's laboratory focuses on understanding the molecular and cellular mechanisms behind fibrovascular complications in the eye. Our ultimate goals are to: Identify reliable biomarkers for disease prediction, diagnosis, and prognosis. Develop novel therapeutic approaches for effective disease management. Establish disease-relevant in vitro, ex vivo, and in vivo models. Create minimally invasive and sustained drug delivery systems.

name: Wang Yee Moon Angela
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Professor Angela Wang is currently Clinical Professor with Medicine Academic Clinical Programme at the SingHealth Duke-NUS Academic Medical Centre and Senior Consultant at the Department of Renal Medicine, Singapore General Hospital. She was formerly a staff nephrologist and clinician scientist at the Department of Medicine, Queen Mary Hospital, the University of Hong Kong, Hong Kong SAR, China. Angela has a strong track record in both clinical and academic medicine in nephrology. She has strong experience in international clinical practice guidelines (CPG) development and is world recognized for her research in cardiovascular-kidney-metabolic health, chronic kidney disease-mineral bone disease (CKD-MBD), biomarkers research for personalized cardiovascular-kidney-metabolic care, peritoneal dialysis and translational nutrition research in kidney disease. Her latest research endeavors are in clinical trials and innovations to improve the outcomes of patients with kidney diseases, precision medicine in cardiovascular-kidney-metabolic care as well as preventive kidney medicine. She is the Lead Principal Investigator of OPERA, PROCEED and PROOF Trials and also an Executive Member of POSIBIL6-ESKD Trials. She actively contributes to international societies by being the current Councilor of the International Society of Nephrology (ISN), Chair of the ISN Education Working Group and Chair of the ISN North and East Asia Regional Board. She is the Past President of the International Society of Renal Nutrition and Metabolism (ISRNM). She co-chaired the Global Kidney Nutrition Care Atlas, a joint initiative between ISRNM and ISN with an aim to advance global kidney nutrition care. She is a Council member of the International Society of Peritoneal Dialysis (ISPD) and a member of the ISN Cardio-Renal Roadmap, a collaborative with the European Society of Heart Failure (ESHF), Renal Physician Association (RPA), American Society of Preventive Cardiology (ASPC) and Kidney Disease Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) aiming to advance global cardio-kidney care. She was an Executive Committee member of Kidney Disease Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO). She is also a Core Committee Member of ISN-Advancing Clinical Trial (ACT) with an aim to drive global clinical trials conduct in kidney diseases and an Executive Committee Member of the Standardized Outcomes in Nephrology (SONG) Initiative. She has served as a workgroup member of many international CPG including the latest KDIGO anaemia guidelines update in CKD (2022 -), the Joint ISPD and Global Renal Exercise Network (GREX) Practice Recommendations on Physical Activity and Exercise in PD in 2021, Kidney Disease Outcome Quality Initiative (KDOQI) Nutrition Guidelines in CKD in 2020, the ISPD Prescribing High Quality Goal-Directed PD Guidelines in 2020, and KDIGO CKD-Mineral Bone Disease (MBD) CPG in 2009. She chaired the ISPD Cardiovascular and Metabolic Guidelines for Adult Peritoneal Dialysis Patients in 2015 and is the Chair of the First Joint ISRNM and ISPD Practice Recommendations on Nutrition Management in Peritoneal Dialysis Patients (2023- ). Angela is also the co-chair of KDIGO CKD-MBD 2017 guidelines implementation Asia summit in 2017 and KDIGO Diabetes and Blood pressure in CKD Asia-Pacific guidelines implementation summit in 2024. She has received several prestigious international awards in recognition of her work in cardiovascular-metabolic-nutrition complications in kidney disease including the Thomas Addis Award, a lifetime award of the International Society of Renal Nutrition and Metabolism in Dec 2022, the Joel D. Kopple Award from the United States National Kidney Foundation in April 2018 in recognition of her contributions in the field of renal nutrition and metabolism. She has received the Travelling Lecturer Award of the Asia Pacific Federation of Clinical Biochemistry in 2011/2012 and the John F. Maher Award of the ISPD in 2006 in recognition of her research work in cardiac biomarkers, cardiovascular disease and peritoneal dialysis, respectively. She was invited to give Dr VN Acharya’s Oration at the 1st Annual Chapter of Women in Nephrology (WIN) -International Conference 2022. She has published over 230 papers, 15 book chapters and delivered over 200 invited lectures at international and regional conferences. She currently serves on the editorial boards of several leading kidney journals including: Kidney Int, American Society of Nephrology (ASN) Journals, Nephrol Dial Transplant (Theme Editor), Nephron Clinical Practice (Associate Editor), J Renal Nutrition, J Diabetes, etc and was previously Associate Editor of Am J Kidney Dis. She is also the current Editor-in-Chief of European Medical Journal (EMJ)-Nephrology.

name: Wang Yibin
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Professor
summary: Dr. Wang obtained his training in molecular genetics and cell biology from Baylor College of Medicine, and conducted post-doctoral research in neurobiology and cardiovascular diseases at The Scripps Research Institute and UC, San Diego. Since 1998, Dr. Wang’s laboratory has focused on genetic network and molecular basis of cardiovascular physiology and diseases. Dr. Wang’s lab has a long track record in discovering novel components and novel interactions in stress-signal transduction pathways in mammalian cells through biochemical, proteomic, molecular and genomic approaches. Recently, Wang lab has also employed systems-based approach in gene and pathway discovery for cardiovascular and metabolic disease. Dr. Wang’s lab has established a broad spectrum of research approaches in cardiac biology, from molecular biology, cell physiology to intact heart physiology, genomics and systems biology. Dr. Wang has been supported by NIH, DoD and other extramural grants throughout his research career and published more than 240 scientific articles. Dr. Wang served as the Chair of Cardiovascular Theme at UCLA from 2015 to 2019, a new initiative to build an integrated Cardiovascular Medicine and Research infrastructure at UCLA. D. Wang has four licensed patents and is a scientific founder of a biotech start-up. In addition to being the PI of an 12 people research team, Dr. Wang is also actively engaged in teaching and education, served as the Education Director for UCLA Cardiovascular Research Laboratories and vice Chair of UCLA Molecular, Cellular and Integrated Physiology Ph.D. program.

name: Wang Yue
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Wang Yue is a Consultant with the Department of Cardiology at Changi General Hospital (CGH). She completed her training with the SingHealth Cardiology Senior Residency Program in 2019 before joining CGH in 2020. Her interest lies in cardiac arrhythmias, cardiac electronic implanted devices and electrophysiological study (EPS).

name: Wang Zhenxun
department: Centre for Vision Research
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Wang graduated with a PhD from Cold Spring Harbor School of Biological Sciences in 2012 and underwent postdoctoral training at the Genome Institute of Singapore, initially working to dissect key metabolic networks in cancers, and later focusing on investigating disease mechanisms in glaucoma. He was appointed Assistant Professor at the Centre for Vision Research in 2021, with concurrent appointments in Singapore Eye Research Institute and the Genome Institute of Singapore. Glaucoma is a group of eye diseases that lead to the degeneration of retina ganglion cells in the optic nerve. As such, it is the leading cause of blindness worldwide. However, how and why this degeneration occurs is not well understood. Dr. Wang's group is interested in deeply understanding the pathophysiology of different glaucoma subtypes in order to find new ways of preventing and curing the disease. As a starting point, we use risk genes uncovered from whole exome sequencing of large patient cohorts to discover downstream and related pathways contributing to glacuoma disease progression, in order to find clinically-relevant genetic participants in glaucoma. To this end, we have found recurrent mutations in specific genes that are strongly associated with the risk of developing specific subtypes of glaucoma. To obtain insights in the disease process and understand how these mutations confer risk, we study the functional consequences of these mutations to ascertain their roles in glaucoma.

name: Wee Choon Peng Jeremy
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Emergency Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Wee Choon Meng Jeremy is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Emergency Medicine, Singapore General Hospital.

name: Wee Tien Seng Joseph
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Wee is a Senior Consultant at the Division of Radiation Oncology He subspecialises in Head and Neck Oncology with special interests in Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma (NPC). He was awarded the National Medical Research Council Clinician Scientist Award (Senior Investigator), from 2009-2013. He started the Intensity Modulated Radiation Therapy (IMRT) program for NPC in Singapore 2002. He was also the Principal Investigator for 2 Randomised Phase 3 trials for NPC. He sits on the Editorial Board of several Oncology journals.

name: Wee Tze Lin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Wee is a Senior Consultant Ophthalmologist with the General Cataract & Comprehensive Ophthalmology Department at the Singapore National Eye Centre (SNEC). Dr Wee graduated from the National University of Singapore in 1990. He obtained his Master of Medicine (Ophthalmology) of Singapore in 1995 and was also admitted to the Fellowship of the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh (Ophthalmology) that same year. He completed his fellowship in Cornea and Refractive Surgery at the Cornea-Plastic Unit and Eye Bank of Queen Victoria Hospital NHS Trust, East Grinstead, United Kingdom in 1999. Upon his return to Singapore, Dr Wee joined the Refractive Surgery Service of the Singapore National Eye Centre and was promoted to Senior Consultant Ophthalmologist of SNEC in 2006. In 2006, he was admitted as a Fellow to the Academy of Medicine (Singapore). His current and past external appointments include medical board president appointments to the Singapore Armed Forces, board member to SingHealth Centralized Institutional Review Board as well as selected committees of the Singapore Medical Council. Dr Wee has been active in research in the field of refractive surgery and in particular LASIK. He was a clinical investigator to several major global clinical trials including the Wavefront-guided LASIK and Aspheric LASIK trials, Acufocus Implant trial for presbyopia and the Personalized Aspheric LASIK trial. He has also been active in the teaching of LASIK and cataract surgery. He had conducted numerous teaching courses and in-service programmes regionally and internationally. He has contributed book chapters in the areas of LASIK and refractive surgery. He was a certified trainer for Bausch & Lomb and is also an Asia-Pacific Society of Cataract and Refractive Surgeons (APSCRS) Certified Educator.

name: Wee Wei Yi, Lynette (Huang Weiyi)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Wiryasaputra Shaan @ Liem Wen Shan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Wiryasaputra received her undergraduate medical degree from the National University of Singapore (NUS) and completed her ophthalmology training under the auspices of the National Healthcare Group ACGME accredited Ophthalmology Residency Programme. During her training, she served as Deputy Chief Resident and a Clinical Lecturer with both Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore and Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, Nanyang Technological University. Following her qualification as an ophthalmologist locally, she undertook further subspecialty training in uveitis and medical retina at Sydney Eye Hospital, Australia. She is currently working as an Associate Consultant with both the Medical Retina and Ocular Inflammation and Immunology Departments at the Singapore National Eye Centre.

name: Wong Bak Siew Steven
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Steven Wong is the Chairman of the division of Hyperacute Care at Sengkang General Hospital. His expertise is in Spinal, Musculoskeletal and Sports Radiology and his research interests includes imaging of Articular Cartilage and Degenerative Joint Diseases. He is in charge of the Radiology Informatics, including RISPACS, for Sengkang General Hospital.

name: Wong Chui Mae
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Paediatrics Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor

name: Wong Gar-Ling Theodore
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Also known as Dr Ted Wong, was born raised and trained in Canada. He obtained his medical degree (MD 1989) and anesthesia degree (FRCP 1996) from the University of Manitoba, Canada. This was followed by a neuroanesthesia fellowship at the University Hospital, London, Ontario, Canada (1998), with emphasis training on anesthesia for awake craniotomies and initial clinical trials on the use of remifentanil for neuroanesthesia. He has been practicing anesthesia for the past 10 years in Singapore with special interests in neuroanesthesia and airway management. He was a team member of the 5-day separation of craniopagus twins and the primary neuroanesthesiologist for awake craniotomies in the IMRI suite.

name: Wong Gee Chuan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Wong’s interest is in malignant haematology. Her special interest is in acute leukemias. Trained in MD Anderson Cancer Center, Houston, Texas, Dr Wong spearheaded the setup of the Leukemia Registry and Myeloproliferative Disorders Registry in the Department of Hematology, Singapore General Hospital and developed leukemia protocols, antibiotics and antifungal guidelines for immunocompromised hosts. Besides authoring patient guidebooks on leukemias, lymphoma and febrile neutropenia, she has also published on malignant haematology, myeloproliferative neoplasm, febrile neutropenia and invasive fungal Infections. She is also a principal investigator in multiple trials in these areas. Besides contributing actively in advisory boards, Dr Wong is also a reviewer for medical and hematology journals and has also been appointed as examiner in multiple medical examinations. She has won multiple academic and scientific awards at national and international scientific meetings and has also been awarded multiple Excellent Service Awards and Star Awards. Dr Wong received specialist accreditation in Palliative Medicine in 2010 and has an interest in Palliative Medicine in hematological malignancies.

name: Wong Huimin, Bernice (Huang Huimin)
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Senior Research Fellow
summary: Bernice is a Senior Research Fellow in the Programme in Cardiovascular and Metabolic Disorders at Duke-NUS Medical School. She received her BSc in Biochemistry and Molecular Biology from the University of Melbourne and her PhD in Integrated Biology and Medicine from Duke-NUS Medical School under the mentorship of Professor David Silver. Prior to working at Duke-NUS, Bernice worked as a Senior Research Officer at National Cancer Centre, Singapore with Professor Teh Bin Tean, where she co-led a project on anti-angiogenic therapies as part of the Roche-Singapore Hub for Translational Medicine. Her current research focuses on epidermal barrier maintenance with the hope of developing therapies to improve wound healing or skin diseases such as psoriasis and dermatitis. Her work is supported by the Khoo Postdoctoral Fellowship Award and Open Fund - Young Individual Research Grant (OF-YIRG) and she was selected to attend the 11th HOPE Meeting organized by the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science in 2019.

name: Wong Jolin
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Anaesthesiology & Perioperative Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Wong Jolin is a Senior Consultant with Department of Surgical Intensive Care, under Division of Anaesthesiology and Perioperative Medicine at Singapore General Hospital.

name: Wong Kang Min
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Radiological Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Wong Kang Min is a senior consultant with the Department of Radiology at Changi General Hospital (CGH). Dr Wong received his medical degree from the National University of Singapore Faculty of Medicine in 2005. He received his specialist training in the Department of Radiology at Changi General Hospital and Singapore General Hospital. Dr Wong’s subspecialty interest is in abdominal imaging, in particular prostate MRI. He was awarded the Eastern Health Alliance Health Manpower Development Programme Fellowship in 2014 and had the opportunity to undertake an attachment as a visiting fellow at the Abdominal Imaging Division, Massachusetts General Hospital, USA, in 2015. He is experienced in all modalities of diagnostic breast imaging (including MRI), as well as imaging guided breast biopsies.

name: Wong Kok Seng
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Medicine Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: A/Prof Wong Kok Seng is a Senior Consultant with the Department of Renal Medicine & Department of Internal Medicine, Singapore General Hospital. A/Prof Wong is also the Deputy CEO (Clinical Services), SingHealth Community Hospitals (SCH).

name: Wong Merng Koon
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Performing, teaching, researching for the last 30 years on Orthopaedic Traumatology. Expertise in primary to tertiary reconstruction of fractures including joint replacement as final repair. Expertise extends to technology design and improvement of Orthopaedic Traumatology. International and Reginal collaboration allow cross-pollination of ideas and techniques resulting in AO Orthopaedic Trauma Implants design wins and publication of worldwide multi-centre trials on hot topics fractures like distal radius and hip. Always looking our for new talents and ideas to translate from bench to bedside care and optimistic that the future hold boundless possibilities.

name: Wong Ming Xiang, Colin
department: Signature Research Programme in Cardiovascular & Metabolic Disorders
title: Research Assistant
summary: I graduated from Latrobe University with a degree in biomedical science. From applying laboratory techniques and seeing purified DNA appear on a gel to discovering new therapeutic targets, the intricate and eye-opening field of biology is something that has always captivated me. Aside from that, I also enjoy aquascaping and fish-keeping during my free time.

name: Wong Ningyan
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Clinical Assistant Professor Wong Ningyan is a Consultant from the Department of Cardiology at National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS).

name: Wong Peiyan
department: Signature Research Programme in Neuroscience & Behavioural Disorders
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Peiyan Wong is a neuroscientist whose diverse expertise spans neuroanatomy, behavioral neuroscience, and animal models of neuropsychiatric disorders. Her research career, rooted in a fascination with the human brain's complex organization and computational power, now focuses on unraveling the neurobiology of neuropsychiatric disorders and developing innovative therapeutic interventions. Dr. Wong's early work, as a graduate student with Professor Jon Kaas at Vanderbilt University, focused on elucidating the neocortical organization in various animals, including short-tailed opossums, tarsiers, gray squirrels, tree shrews, and galagos, using histochemical and immunohistochemical methods. She also studied functional connectivity of various brain areas in these species using tract tracing techniques and contributed to research on cellular scaling rules in rodent and primate brains. Following their doctoral studies, Dr. Wong received intensive training in pharmacology and mouse behavioral phenotyping under Professor William C. Wetsel at Duke University. Currently, Dr. Wong manages two rodent behavioral phenotyping facilities, at Duke-NUS and NUS, where she collaborates with researchers across various neuroscience disciplines. Her expertise in psychology, neuroscience, and animal behavior enables her to contribute effectively to various neuroscience research projects, from experimental design to grant applications and manuscript preparation. Dr. Wong is dedicated to advancing the understanding of neuropsychiatric diseases and developing well-tolerated, effective treatments for patients.

name: Wong Sung Lung Aaron
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Cardiovascular Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Clinical Associate Professor Aaron Wong is Deputy Chief Executive Officer (Technology Advances and Governance), Senior Consultant from the Department of Cardiology and the Director of Cardiac Catherisation Laboratory at the National Heart Centre Singapore. His clinical interest is in interventional cardiology.

name: Wong Tien Yin
department: Office of Academic Medicine
title: Professor
summary: Professor Wong Tien Yin is a Senior Advisor to SingHealth and a senior consultant ophthalmologist (part-time) sub-specialising in medical retina at the Singapore National Eye Centre. In 2022, Professor Wong is appointed as the Founding Head and Chair Professor of Tsinghua Medicine, 清华医学部,a new academic health system based at Tsinghua University, Beijing, China, with a vision to develop academic medicine and to strengthen the foundations of medical education, research and innovation at the School of Medicine and affiliated Tsinghua hospitals and institutes. Professor Wong was Medical Director of the Singapore National Eye Centre (SNEC) from 2014-2021. He has served as Deputy Group Chief Executive Officer (Research and Education), SingHealth and Vice-Dean, Duke-NUS Medical School, National University of Singapore. Professor Wong has also served as Professor and Head of the Department of Ophthalmology, National University of Singapore, Chairman of the Department of Ophthalmology, Royal Victorian Eye and Ear Hospital, the University of Melbourne, Australia and was the past Executive Director of the Singapore Eye Research Institute (SERI) and previous chair to SERI’s Board, where he continues as a Senior Principal Clinician Scientist (Adjunct). Professor Wong completed medical school at National University of Singapore (NUS) as a President's Scholar, clinical ophthalmology training at SNEC, and retinal fellowships at the University of Wisconsin, Madison, USA, and the University of Sydney, Australia. He obtained his MPH and PhD from the Johns Hopkins University, USA. Professor Wong leads a broad-based research focused on the epidemiology of retinal diseases, such as diabetic and hypertensive retinopathy and age-related macular degeneration, and on ocular imaging technology including the development and application of artificial intelligence (AI) for diabetic retinopathy screening and prediction of cardiovascular diseases. Professor Wong has published more than 1,500 peer-reviewed papers, including papers in the New England Journal of Medicine and the Lancet, has given more than 500 invited named, plenary and symposium lectures, and received more than US$100million in grant funding from the National Medical Research Council in Singapore, the National Institutes of Health (NIH), USA, and the National Health and Medical Research Council, Australia. He is a Highly Cited Researcher (top 1% of all researchers) Professor Wong is currently serving or has previously served on multiple Editorial Boards: The American Journal of Ophthalmology, British Journal of Ophthalmology, JAMA-Ophthalmology, Investigative Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences, Diabetes Care, Journal of Hypertension, Ophthalmologica and Lancet Digital Health. He is a Board Member of the National Medical Research Council, Singapore and has served in the Council of various professional societies, including the International Council of Ophthalmology, the Macula Society, the Asia-Pacific Academy of Ophthalmology and the Asia-Pacific Vitreo-Retina Society. For his service and research, Prof Wong has been recognised nationally and internationally with numerous awards. He was recognised as the top researcher in any field from two leading Universities: the Outstanding Researcher Award from the National University of Singapore (winning it twice in 2004 and 2009) and the Woodward Medal from the University of Melbourne. He has received the Alcon Research Institute Award, USA, the Novartis Prize in Diabetes (Global Young Investigator), the Commonwealth Health Minister's Award for Excellence in Health and Medical Research, the Australian Society of Medical Research AMGEN Medical Research of the Year Award for "outstanding contribution to translational research", and the Arnall Patz Medal from the Macula Society, USA. Professor Wong is a two-time recipient of the Singapore Translational Researcher Award (2008 and 2014). He was conferred the Arthur Lim Professorship in Ophthalmology in 2019. He received the National Clinician Scientist Award, the President's Science Award in 2010 and the President's Technology Award in 2014. He is an elected member of the U.S. National Academy of Medicine.

name: Wong Ting Hway
department: Signature Research Programme in Health Services Research & Population Health
title: Adjunct Associate Professor

name: Wong Wai Keong
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Surgery Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Professor
summary: Professor Wong is a General Surgeon with a special interest in Upper Gastro-intestinal Surgery, Laparoscopic Surgery and Therapeutic Endoscopy. In Upper GI Surgery, he specialises in Stomach Cancer and Oesophageal Cancer as well as Laparoscopic GI Surgery. Professor Wong graduated from Medical Faculty (MBBS), National University of Singapore in 1984. He subsequently obtained his Fellowship in Surgery (FRCS) from Royal College of Edinburgh, UK in 1990 and became a Member of Fellow of Academy of Medicine (FAMS), Singapore in 1996. Professor Wong subsequently went to Japan, England and Hong Kong to undergo training in Upper GI Surgery and Endoscopy.

name: Wong Wen Kuan Doric
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Wong is a Senior Consultant in the Surgical Retina Department at the Singapore National Eye Centre. He is Chair of the Centre’s Professionalism and Ethics Council, the Chief Risk Officer, the Chair of the Health Performance Office and Vice Chair of the Centre’s Quality Assurance Committee. He was Head of the Retina Service and later the Retina Centre from 2005, completing his term in 2016. He was a member of the Centre’s Strategic Planning Committee/Executive Committee from 2008 completing his term in 2017. He represents Singapore on the Council of the ASEAN Ophthalmological Society and is the Chair of the Society’s Scientific Committee. Dr Wong completed his basic medical training and postgraduate training in ophthalmology in Singapore. He went on to a surgical-medical retina fellowship in the Singapore National Eye Centre in 1997-1998. He was an international fellow at the Manhattan Eye Ear and Throat Hospital in New York in 1998 - 1999. His practice typically includes surgery for retinal detachments, diabetic retinopathy and macular disorders (e.g. macular holes, epiretinal membranes, vitreomacular traction, myopic macular schisis). He manages complex retinal problems like proliferative vitreoretinopathy, trauma and retinal surgery in combination with anterior segment disease. In the field of medical retina, Adjunct Associate Professor Wong manages disorders that typically include age-related macular degeneration, choroidal neovascular disease, diabetic retinopathy and other retinal conditions. Dr Wong is also a staff with the General Cataract and Comprehensive Ophthalmology Department and cataract surgery with intraocular lens implantation continues to be a significant part of his clinical work. He is on the advisory panel of several pharmaceutical and medical equipment companies.

name: Wong Yi Ting, Evelyn
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Oncology Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Evelyn Wong is a Medical Oncologist who practices at National Cancer Centre Singapore (NCCS) and Sengkang General Hospital. She specializes in Gastrointestinal Oncology, with a sub-speciality interest in Colorectal Cancer. She founded AYABytes, a mobile app, co-created by patients and physicians, for Adolescents and Young Adults (AYA) cancer patients. She was the Director of Seeds of Nations Pte Ltd (2013-2017), a corporation focused on global community projects, having worked with communities in Chennai (India), Kathmandu (Nepal), and Mae Na Chon (Thailand). She currently holds a concurrent appointment as a faculty member under Oncology ACP, SingHealth Duke-NUS Global Health Institute. She has received accolades for her educational efforts including Residency in Singhealth Excels Inspiring Resident Educator Award 2020 and the NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine Medical Society Outstanding Tutor Award 2018. She is passionate about gastrointestinal oncology, patient empowerment, and improving the quality of care through innovation and health systems. Her research focus is on evaluating the cost, value, and policy in cancer care to quantify patient experiences, addressing social determinants to enhance provider efficiency, and advancing equity in cancer care with a personal interest in resource-limited settings in Asia.

name: Wong Yick Mun Edmund
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Associate Professor
summary: Dr Edmund Wong is the Deputy Medical Director (Clinical Services) and Head of the Surgical Retina Department at the Singapore National Eye Centre. He graduated from the Faculty of Medicine, National University of Singapore with a Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery degree in 1991. He obtained his Fellowship from the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh in 1998, and in the same year, obtained the Master of Medicine (Ophthalmology) degree from the National University of Singapore. He completed his fellowship with the Vitreo-retinal Department in Singapore National Eye Centre in 2001 and a fellowship in advanced vitreo-retina at Beetham Eye Institute, Joslin Diabetes Centre, Harvard Medical School in 2003. Dr Wong has a distinguished academic track record in Ophthalmology. A Gold Medal award winner, he continues to serve as Examiner in Ophthalmology, National University of Singapore and the Royal College of Surgeons, Edinburgh, United Kingdom. He also holds concurrent appointments as Clinical Lecturer in Ophthalmology, National University of Singapore, and Clinical Adjunct Fellow, Singapore Eye Research Institute. He is Principal Investigator in several studies of the retina involving diabetic retinopathy, retinal detachment and age-related macular degeneration. Dr Wong's subspecialty training in diseases of the vitreous and retina with special focus on diabetic retinopathy at the Singapore National Eye Centre and Harvard Medical School have equipped him with a strong base to treat patients with diseases such as complicated diabetic eye disease, age-related macular degeneration and retinal detachment. He is well-experienced with state-of-art investigations, laser and surgery for the retina including sutureless, small-gauge vitrectomy. He is often invited to give lectures and demonstrations on retinal surgery regionally and internationally. In addition to diseases of the retina, Dr Wong also has a strong interest in Cataract surgery and uses the most current small incision phacoemulsification techniques and intraocular lenses after consideration of each individual’s special circumstances. Dr Wong believes that effective communication of findings, diagnosis, special features, and treatment options enhances a patient’s understanding of his or her unique condition and facilitates proper management.

name: Woo Jyh Haur (Hu Zhihao)
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Woo is a Senior Consultant ophthalmologist in the Cornea & External Eye Disease Department of the Singapore National Eye Centre (SNEC). Dr Woo has an active practice which specializes in cornea, anterior segment disorders and corneal transplantation in both adults and children, but also includes cataract surgery, refractive surgery and comprehensive (general) ophthalmology. He also runs the paediatric cornea service at both SNEC and KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital. Dr Woo graduated from the National University of Singapore with a Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery in 2006, winning the Gold Medal and Book Prize in Ophthalmology. He trained at SNEC and obtained the Master of Medicine (Ophthalmology) degree in 2012. He attained accreditation as a Specialist in Ophthalmology in 2015, after passing the Joint Committee in Specialist Training Exit Examination as a multiple Gold Medalist. Dr Woo holds a Master of Science degree in Evidence-based Health Care from the University of Oxford. He is also a qualified Fellow of the Royal College of Ophthalmologists (United Kingdom) and Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh by examination as well as a Fellow of the Academy of Medicine of Singapore. Dr Woo completed a 2-year fellowship in Cornea and Refractive Surgery at SNEC in 2018. From 2019 to 2020, he underwent a 1-year specialized fellowship in Paediatric Cornea and External Diseases at The Hospital for Sick Children (Sickkids) in Toronto, Canada, a major paediatric centre in North America for clinical care and research. His stint at Sickkids makes him one of only a few doctors internationally to have been specifically trained in the management of corneal conditions (both common and rare) and transplantation in children. In conjunction with his clinical work, Dr Woo is engaged in both research and education as a faculty member under the SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme. His research interests include outcomes of corneal transplantation, paediatric corneal diseases and corneal neurotization, a novel procedure he and his multidisciplinary team have performed to restore corneal sensation in patients with neurotrophic keratopathy since 2021. Dr Woo is also involved in the training and education of undergraduates, residents, fellows and allied health professionals. His contribution to undergraduate education was recognized with the Dean’s Excellence Award in Teaching and Education in 2017. He regularly participates in local and international conferences as an invited speaker and also serves as faculty for corneal transplantation skills transfer courses regionally. Dr Woo holds the administrative appointment of the Director of Risk Office at SNEC. Concurrent to his role in risk management, he is involved in the Safety and Quality initiatives at the centre by serving as a member of the Quality Assurance, Quality Improvement and Ensure Safer Systems committees. He is also the co-chair of the Patient Safety, Care Transition and Supervision Subgroup under the SingHealth Ophthalmology Residency Programme. External to SNEC, he is a member of the Complaints Panel under the Singapore Medical Council.

name: Woo Yew Lok
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Musculoskeletal Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Woo Yew Lok completed his Medical Degree in 2010. He attained his Master of Medicine (Orthopaedic Surgery) from National University of Singapore in 2018. He completed his specialist training is Orthopaedic Surgery and was appointed as Fellow of the Royal College of Edinburgh in 2019. Dr Woo is an Orthopaedic Trauma Surgeon and subspecialise in Pelvic and Acetabular Trauma. Beyond his clinical profile, Dr Woo has a special interest in medical simulation and technological advancements in the field of postgraduate medical education. He believes that technology will be able to augment surgical training and learner outcomes. As such, he has organized and assisted in numerous under- and postgraduate courses.

name: Wu Grace Yuen Man
department: SingHealth Duke-NUS Ophthalmology & Visual Sciences Academic Clinical Programme
title: Clinical Assistant Professor
summary: Dr Grace Wu graduated from the University of Bristol Medical School, England. She holds both specialist degree in ophthalmology as well as paediatric medicine and is a registered specialist with Singapore Medical Council and UK General Medical Council. Before embarking on a career in ophthalmology in UK, she attained the Diploma in Child Health from the Royal College of Physicians in 1997. She was awarded F.R.C.S (ophthalmology) by the Royal College of Surgeons, Edinburgh and the UK specialist registration (ophthalmology) upon completion of her advanced surgical training. In addition, she undertook a further one-year fellowship to sub-specialize in paediatrics and adult strabismus at Central Manchester Children’s Hospital, largest children’s’ hospital in Europe. In 2010, she was appointed consultant ophthalmic surgeon at the University Hospital County Durham and the Darlington Memorial Hospital NHS trust in UK. She came to Singapore in 2014 to take up the position of consultant ophthalmologist in comprehensive cataract surgery and paediatrics ophthalmology and strabismus. Having been actively involved in teaching medical students and residents, she had been invited as guest speaker in numerous international conferences. She is currently a member of the Royal College of Ophthalmologist, London, Royal College of Surgeons, Edinburgh and American Association of Paediatric Ophthalmology and Strabismus.

name: Wu Xiaoting
department: Centre for Biomedical Data Science
title: Research Fellow
summary: Xiaoting graduated with a bachelor's degree from Xiamen University in China.
